items.title: Twilight in the Wilderness
items.description: In his New York studio, Church painted this spectacular view of a blazing sunset over wilderness near Mount Katahdin in Maine, which he had sketched during a visit nearly two years earlier. Although Church often extolled the grandeur of American landscape in his work, this painting appears to have additional overtones. Created on the eve of the Civil War, the painting's subject can be interpreted as symbolically evoking the coming conflagration. Church's considerable technical skills and clever showmanship contributed to his fame as the premier artist of his generation. Rather than debut this painting in an annual exhibition with works by other artists as was the custom, Church instead exhibited it by itself at a prestigious art gallery. Coaxed by advance publicity and highly favorable press reviews, several hundred spectators flocked to admire it during its seven-week run.
items.description_zh: 在纽约的工作室里，丘奇绘制了这幅壮观的日落景象，描绘了缅因州卡塔丁山附近的荒野，这是他近两年前访问时素描的。尽管丘奇经常在作品中颂扬美国风景的壮丽，但这幅画似乎还有额外的含义。创作于内战前夕，这幅画的主题可以被解释为象征性地唤起即将到来的大火。丘奇精湛的技术技巧和巧妙的表演技巧使他成为同代最杰出的艺术家。他没有像惯例那样在年度展览中与其他艺术家的作品一起首次展出这幅画，而是单独在一家著名的画廊展出。在前期宣传和高度好评的媒体评论的诱导下，数百名观众在七周的展期内蜂拥而至欣赏它。
items.cardline_zh: 燃烧的天空下，荒野静默如永恒
items.cardline: Blazing sky above, wilderness silent as eternity

items.title: A Woman's Work
items.description: Trained as a journalist, the young Sloan explored social issues more vigorously than most of the painters of his time, portraying working-class urbanites engaged in ordinary activities. He observed this particular scene through a rear window of his Manhattan apartment. Perched on a narrow fire escape, a woman hangs fresh laundry to dry on clotheslines strung between tenements. As evidenced by the painting, the labors of American women at the turn of the 1900s were most often confined to the domestic realm.
items.description_zh: 作为记者出身的斯隆，比同时代的大多数画家更积极地探索社会问题，描绘从事日常活动的工人阶级城市居民。他通过曼哈顿公寓的后窗观察到了这一特定场景。一位妇女站在狭窄的防火梯上，将刚洗好的衣物挂在公寓楼之间的晾衣绳上。正如这幅画所证明的，20世纪初美国女性的劳动大多局限于家庭领域。
items.cardline_zh: 绳上衣物轻摆，城市呼吸在晾晒间
items.cardline: Laundry sways gently, city breathes between the lines

items.title: Paul Lafond and Alphonse Cherfils Examining a Painting
items.description: The upper left corner of this painting bears a dedication by Degas "to his dear friends." The double portrait depicts Paul Lafond (left), future curator of the art museum at Pau, in southwestern France, and Alphonse Cherfils (right), an art collector and defender of the Impressionists. Through the influence of these friends, Degas's works were selected for the annual exhibitions of the Société des Amis des Arts (Society of Friends of the Arts), and his painting, A Cotton Office (1873), was the first by the artist to be purchased by a museum. Since the painting held by the two men is probably by Degas, this intimate image commemorates both personal and business relationships.
items.title_zh: 保罗·拉丰和阿尔方斯·谢尔菲斯在审视一幅画
items.description_zh: 这幅画的左上角有德加的题词“献给他亲爱的朋友们”。这幅双人肖像描绘了保罗·拉丰（左），他是法国西南部波城艺术博物馆未来的馆长，以及阿尔方斯·谢尔菲斯（右），一位艺术收藏家和印象派的捍卫者。通过这些朋友的影响，德加的作品被选入“艺术之友协会”的年度展览，他的画作《棉花办公室》（1873年）成为第一幅被博物馆购买的德加作品。由于两人手中拿的画很可能出自德加之手，这幅亲密的图像纪念了个人和商业关系。
items.cardline_zh: 画中观画，目光里藏着另一个世界
items.cardline: Gazing at art, their eyes hold another world

items.title: View of Florence
items.description: Cole visited Italy in 1831 and made a small pencil sketch of this vista of Florence shortly before sunset. In his New York studio six years later, he transformed the drawing into this large oil painting, adding picturesque humans and goats to the foreground. Exhibiting View of Florence alongside a painting of the Catskill Mountains in New York in 1837, Cole set out to prove that he had mastered the very different landscapes of the Old and New Worlds.
items.description_zh: 科尔于1831年访问意大利，并在日落前不久画了一幅佛罗伦萨景色的铅笔速写。六年后，他在纽约的工作室里将这幅素描转化为这幅大型油画，并在前景中添加了如画的人物和山羊。1837年，科尔将《佛罗伦萨景色》与一幅纽约卡茨基尔山脉的画作一同展出，旨在证明他已经掌握了旧世界和新世界截然不同的风景。
items.cardline_zh: 余晖洒满佛罗伦萨，远山如黛近人安
items.cardline: Sunset bathes Florence, distant hills near and still

items.title: The Race Track (Death on a Pale Horse)
items.description: Ryder’s subject was inspired by a horse race that took place in New York during 1888. One of the artist’s friends wagered $500 on the race and then died by suicide after the horse lost. Medieval symbolism infuses the composition: death appears as a skeleton on horseback holding a scythe with which he cuts down the living, while a snake—a sign of temptation and evil—slithers in the foreground. An intense man, Ryder worked on the painting for several years and was deeply reluctant to part with it.
items.title_zh: 赛马场（苍白马上的死亡）
items.description_zh: 赖德的创作灵感来源于1888年在纽约举行的一场赛马。艺术家的一位朋友在赛马上押了500美元，马输了之后自杀身亡。中世纪象征主义融入了构图：死亡以骷髅骑手的形式出现，手持镰刀砍杀活人，而一条蛇——诱惑和邪恶的象征——在前景中蜿蜒爬行。赖德性格强烈，在这幅画上工作了数年，非常不愿意与之分离。
items.cardline_zh: 死神持镰追马，蛇影在地面低语
items.cardline: Death with scythe chases, snake whispers on the ground

items.title: The Large Plane Trees (Road Menders at Saint-Rémy)
items.description: Like Two Poplars in the Alpilles near Saint-Rémy (on view nearby), Vincent van Gogh painted this autumnal landscape while living in a psychiatric hospital near Saint-Rémy in southern France where he was treated for severe depression. Understanding that painting from nature eased his symptoms, Van Gogh’s physician permitted the artist to paint landscapes outside. Van Gogh described this painting in a letter to his brother Theo: “The last study I did is a view of the village, where they were at work under some enormous plane trees—repairing the pavements. . . . There are heaps of sand, stones, and the gigantic trunks—the leaves yellowing and here and there you can get a glimpse of a house front and small figures.”
items.title_zh: 大梧桐树（圣雷米的修路工）
items.description_zh: 与《阿尔皮耶山附近圣雷米的两棵白杨树》（在附近展出）一样，文森特·梵高在法国南部圣雷米附近的一家精神病院治疗严重抑郁症时，绘制了这幅秋日风景。梵高的医生理解写生能缓解他的症状，允许他在户外画风景。梵高在给弟弟西奥的信中描述了这幅画：“我最后画的一幅习作是村庄的景色，他们正在一些巨大的梧桐树下修路……有沙堆、石头和巨大的树干——叶子变黄了，到处可以看到房屋的正面和小人物。”
items.cardline_zh: 梧桐叶落，笔触里藏着治愈的秋光
items.cardline: Plane leaves fall, brushstrokes hold healing autumn light

items.title: The Biglin Brothers Turning the Stake
items.description: Eakins's painting celebrates athletic teamwork while commemorating an actual event, a famous rowing race that took place on the Schuylkill River in Philadelphia during May 1872. Throngs of spectators line the riverbank and watch as Barney and John Biglin negotiate the tricky turn around a stake marking the halfway point in the contest. Their competitors, seen in the middle distance at the right, lag behind. The Biglin brothers won the race, cementing their status as the most celebrated oarsmen of the era. Trained in the United States and France, Eakins spent almost his entire artistic career in his hometown of Philadelphia. He is renowned for the unsentimental realism in his paintings, whose compositions he developed through painstakingly prepared figure and perspective drawings.
items.description_zh: 埃金斯这幅画颂扬了团队合作精神，同时纪念了一个真实事件：1872年5月在费城斯库尔基尔河上举行的一场著名划船比赛。成群的观众站在河岸上，观看巴尼和约翰·比格林在比赛中巧妙地绕过标志着半程点的标杆。他们的竞争对手在右中景处落后。比格林兄弟赢得了比赛，巩固了他们作为那个时代最著名桨手的地位。埃金斯在美国和法国接受训练，几乎整个艺术生涯都在家乡费城度过。他以画作中不带感情色彩的写实主义而闻名，其构图是通过精心准备的人物和透视素描发展而来的。
items.cardline_zh: 转身处，汗水与河水辉映成勇毅
items.cardline: At the turn, sweat and river shimmer with courage

items.title: Portrait of Dora Wheeler
items.description: Dora Wheeler became Chase's first student when he returned from overseas study in Munich and set up a teaching studio in New York. At the time, few American artists accepted women as private pupils. After her course of study, Wheeler joined her mother in launching a successful decorating firm, one of the first businesses in the country to be operated entirely by women. For the firm, she designed luxurious textiles, and the embroidered silk tapestry that fills the background in her portrait references her occupational interest. Chase's portrait was awarded a gold medal at an international exhibition of contemporary art in Munich in 1883, and later that year was also shown in Paris. At some later point, the painting was acquired by the sitter, who subsequently donated it to the museum.
items.description_zh: 当蔡斯从慕尼黑留学归来并在纽约设立教学工作室时，多拉·惠勒成为他的第一个学生。当时，很少有美国艺术家接受女性作为私人学生。完成学业后，惠勒与母亲一起创办了一家成功的装饰公司，这是美国最早完全由女性经营的企业之一。她为公司设计了奢华的纺织品，肖像背景中填满的刺绣丝绸挂毯暗示了她的职业兴趣。蔡斯的肖像在1883年慕尼黑国际当代艺术展上获得金奖，同年晚些时候也在巴黎展出。后来，这幅画被画中人购得，随后捐赠给了博物馆。
items.cardline_zh: 蓝裙静谧，她指尖栖息着星辰与织物
items.cardline: Blue dress still, her fingers hold stars and fabric

items.title: The Burning of the Houses of Lords and Commons, 16 October 1834
items.description:
Fire consumed London’s famous Houses of Parliament on the night of October 16, 1834, and people gathered along the banks of the river Thames to gaze in awe at the horrifying spectacle. Initially, a low tide made it difficult to pump water to land and hampered steamers towing firefighting equipment along the river. The blaze burned uncontrollably for hours.

J. M. W. Turner records the struggle as the boats in the lower-right corner head toward the flames. Although Turner based the painting on an actual event, he magnified the height of the flames, using the disaster as the starting point to express man’s helplessness when confronted with the destructive powers of nature. Brilliant swathes of color and variable atmospheric effects border on abstraction.
items.title_zh: 1834年10月16日上议院和下议院大火
items.description_zh: 1834年10月16日晚，大火吞噬了伦敦著名的议会大厦，人们聚集在泰晤士河岸边，敬畏地凝视着这可怕的景象。起初，低潮使得向陆地抽水变得困难，也阻碍了拖曳消防设备的蒸汽船沿河行驶。大火失控地燃烧了几个小时。J. M. W. 透纳记录了右下角的船只驶向火焰的挣扎。尽管透纳以真实事件为基础创作了这幅画，但他放大了火焰的高度，以灾难为出发点，表达了人类在面对自然破坏力时的无助。绚丽的色彩和变化多端的大气效果近乎抽象。
items.cardline_zh: 火舌舔舐夜空，泰晤士河倒映一场毁灭
items.cardline: Flames lick the night, Thames reflects the doom

items.title: View of Schroon Mountain, Essex County, New York, After a Storm
items.description:
Championing the American wilderness, Cole declared, "We are still in Eden," in his Essay on American Scenery, published two years before he painted this view of the Adirondacks. The artist sketched the scene in early summer, but when he created the painting in his studio, he rendered it in a dramatic blaze of fall colors. Such a choice likely had nationalistic overtones; he once proclaimed that autumn was "one season where the American forest surpasses all the world in gorgeousness."

Cole included two Indigenous men in the painting’s right foreground foliage. At this time, the Adirondacks remained home to many Native Americans long after most had been forcibly removed from land east of the Mississippi River. While continuing to live, hunt, and fish in the area, these Algonquian and Iroquoian peoples were compelled to significantly adapt their existence amid increasing White settlement and its attendant lumber, mining, and tourist industries.
items.title_zh: 风暴后的斯克龙山，纽约州埃塞克斯县
items.description_zh: 科尔倡导美国荒野，他在《美国风景随笔》中宣称“我们仍在伊甸园”，该文发表于他绘制这幅阿迪朗达克山脉景色之前两年。艺术家在初夏素描了场景，但当他在工作室创作这幅画时，却以戏剧性的秋色渲染。这样的选择很可能带有民族主义色彩；他曾宣称秋天是“美国森林在华丽程度上超越全世界的季节”。科尔在画作右前景的树叶中画了两个原住民男子。当时，阿迪朗达克山脉仍然是许多美洲原住民的家园，尽管大多数人已被强制迁离密西西比河以东的土地。这些阿尔冈昆语系和易洛魁语系的人民在继续生活、狩猎和捕鱼的同时，不得不在日益增多的白人定居及其伴随的伐木、采矿和旅游业中显著调整自己的生存方式。
items.cardline_zh: 风暴过后，山峦披上秋日的金红战袍
items.cardline: After storm, hills don autumn's gold and red

items.title: Church Street El
items.description: Capturing the soaring heights of New York City, this painting is a dramatic bird's-eye view of Broadway and Wall Street, showing a conglomeration of buildings at left and center, and the Church Street elevated train at right. Sheeler based his composition on an image from the short movie Manhatta (1920-21), which he made with the photographer Paul Strand. One of the first avant-garde American films, Manhatta celebrates the dynamic metropolis through a series of carefully composed shots of Lower Manhattan. As typical with Sheeler's work, the artist simplified forms and eliminated textures in Church Street El to concentrate on rhythmic interplay of shapes and color, and patterns of light and shadow.
items.description_zh: 这幅画捕捉了纽约市的高耸景象，是百老汇和华尔街的戏剧性鸟瞰图，左侧和中央是建筑群，右侧是教堂街高架列车。希勒的构图基于他与摄影师保罗·斯特兰德合作的短片《曼哈塔》（1920-21）中的一幅图像。《曼哈塔》是美国最早的先锋电影之一，通过一系列精心构图的曼哈顿下城镜头来颂扬这座充满活力的都市。正如希勒作品的典型特点，他在《教堂街高架铁路》中简化了形式，消除了纹理，专注于形状和色彩的节奏性相互作用，以及光影图案。
items.cardline_zh: 铁轨如线，切割城市的天际与喧嚣
items.cardline: Elevated tracks slice skyline and city's roar

items.title: The Artist's Studio
items.description: Johann Georg Platzer specialized in small, highly detailed paintings scaled for a connoisseur’s cabinet. At the center of this composition is an idealized portrait of the artist himself, clad in a fur-lined robe and holding a painting for the inspection of a visiting connoisseur. This work makes a statement about painting as a noble and intellectual pursuit, and the importance of rigorous training and practice: the artist at the easel is surrounded by a studio teeming with live models, pupils, assistants, and inspiring examples of art from earlier eras. The paintings on the wall, easel, and floor not only reference great artists of the past, but together present an allegory of the five senses.
items.description_zh: 约翰·格奥尔格·普拉策专攻小型、高度精细的画作，适合鉴赏家的陈列柜。构图中心是艺术家本人的理想化肖像，身着毛皮衬里的长袍，手持一幅画供来访的鉴赏家审视。这幅作品阐述了绘画作为一项崇高和智力追求的理念，以及严格训练和实践的重要性：画架旁的艺术家被一个充满模特、学生、助手和早期时代鼓舞人心的艺术范例的工作室所包围。墙上的、画架上的和地板上的画作不仅参考了过去的伟大艺术家，而且共同呈现了五种感官的寓言。
items.cardline_zh: 画架前，灵感如光透过时间的琥珀
items.cardline: At the easel, inspiration glows through time's amber

items.title: The Red Kerchief
items.description: This painting depicts Monet's first wife, Camille, outside on a snowy day passing by the French doors of their home at Argenteuil. Her face is rendered in a radically bold Impressionist technique of mere daubs of paint quickly applied, just as the snow and trees are defined by broad, broken strokes of pure white and green.
items.description_zh: 这幅画描绘了莫奈的第一任妻子卡米耶，在一个雪天，她经过阿让特伊家中的法式门外。她的脸以极其大胆的印象派技法呈现，只是快速涂抹的颜料斑点，就像雪和树由宽阔、破碎的纯白和绿色笔触定义一样。
items.cardline_zh: 一抹红巾，点亮雪中的孤寂与温柔
items.cardline: Red kerchief brightens snow's solitude and grace

items.title: Stag at Sharkey's
items.description: Bellows was no stranger to Sharkey’s Athletic Club, a raucous saloon with a backroom boxing ring, located near his studio. Founded by Tom “Sailor” Sharkey, an ex-fighter who had also served in the US Navy, the club attracted men seeking to watch or participate in matches. Because public boxing was illegal in New York at the time, a private event had to be arranged in order for a bout to take place. Participation was usually limited to members of a particular club, but whenever an outsider competed, he was given temporary membership and known as a “stag.” Although boxing had its share of detractors who considered it uncouth at best or barbaric at worst, its proponents—among them President Theodore Roosevelt—regarded it a healthy manifestation of manliness. Around the time Bellows painted Stag at Sharkey’s, boxing was moving from a predominantly working-class enterprise to one with greater genteel appeal. For some contemporaries, boxing was a powerful analogy for the notion that only the strongest and fittest would flourish in modern society.
items.description_zh: 贝洛斯对沙基体育俱乐部并不陌生，这是一家喧闹的酒吧，后面有拳击台，靠近他的工作室。由前拳击手、也曾在美国海军服役的汤姆“水手”沙基创立，该俱乐部吸引了寻求观看或参加比赛的男人。由于当时公共拳击在纽约是非法的，必须安排私人活动才能进行比赛。参与通常仅限于特定俱乐部的成员，但每当有外人参赛时，他会获得临时会员资格，并被称为“雄鹿”。尽管拳击有不少批评者，他们认为它充其量是不雅的，最坏是野蛮的，但其支持者——包括西奥多·罗斯福总统——认为它是男子气概的健康表现。在贝洛斯创作《沙基俱乐部的雄鹿》前后，拳击正从主要是工人阶级的活动转变为更具绅士吸引力。对一些同时代人来说，拳击是只有最强者和最适者才能在现代社会中繁荣发展的有力类比。
items.cardline_zh: 拳头挥舞，汗与血在灯光下沸腾
items.cardline: Fists swing, sweat and blood seethe under the light

items.title: Frieze of Dancers
items.description: This painting may depict a single dancer seen from four different viewpoints. The young woman is placed in an undefined setting, surrounded by mere wisps of color, applied so spontaneously that the paint ran and dripped. Degas even added the circles in the foreground with his thumb. Such audacity, while acceptable in a small sketch, must have shocked the artist's contemporaries when presented on a six-foot canvas. Equally radical is the idea of combining multiple views of a single figure. Degas's unusual presentation may have been inspired by the photographs of Eadweard Muybridge (1830-1904)
items.description_zh: 这幅画可能描绘了从四个不同视角看到的同一个舞者。年轻女子被置于一个未定义的环境中，周围只有淡淡的色彩，如此自发地涂抹，以至于颜料流淌滴落。德加甚至用拇指在前景中添加了圆圈。这种大胆，如果出现在小幅素描中尚可接受，但呈现在六英尺的画布上时，一定震惊了艺术家的同时代人。同样激进的是将单个形象的多个视角结合起来的想法。德加不寻常的表现方式可能受到埃德沃德·迈布里奇（1830-1904）照片的启发。
items.cardline_zh: 舞影重重，色彩在画布上自由流淌
items.cardline: Dancer's ghost, colors flow free across the canvas

items.title: In the Waves (Dans les Vagues)
items.description: Painted at Pont-Aven in northwest France, this depiction of a nude figure throwing herself into the sea suggests a metaphor for a modern European woman forsaking civilization and abandoning herself to her natural, primitive instincts. The simplified lines and exaggerated colors, especially the contrasting green and orange, seem invented rather than observed from life. Exhibiting the painting at the Café Volpini in Paris in 1889, Gauguin established himself as a leader of the Symbolist movement in art.
items.description_zh: 这幅画创作于法国西北部的蓬塔旺，描绘了一个裸体人物跳入大海，暗示了一个现代欧洲女性抛弃文明、放纵于自然原始本能的隐喻。简化的线条和夸张的色彩，尤其是对比鲜明的绿色和橙色，似乎是虚构而非观察自生活。1889年，高更在巴黎的沃尔皮尼咖啡馆展出这幅画，确立了自己作为艺术中象征主义运动领袖的地位。
items.cardline_zh: 纵身入海，裸身拥抱原始的呼唤
items.cardline: Plunge into sea, naked embrace primal call

items.title: Peregrine Falcons (Duck Hawks)
items.description: A vigorous portrayal of two peregrine falcons with their fresh duck prey, John James Audubon's painting relates to his groundbreaking Birds of America publication that sought to depict every species of native North American bird posed in action with elements of their habitats. Based on his artistic and scientific successes, Audubon became the premier naturalist in the United States. After his death, he continued to be associated with bird and nature conservation, and several organizations devoted to such causes were named in his honor. However, in recent years, as Audubon's slaveholding and looting of Indigenous graves has become more widely acknowledged, an increasing number of these groups are changing their names.
items.description_zh: 约翰·詹姆斯·奥杜邦这幅生动描绘两只游隼及其新鲜鸭类猎物的画作，与他开创性的《美洲鸟类》出版物相关，该出版物旨在描绘每一种北美本土鸟类，以动态姿态呈现并包含其栖息地元素。基于他的艺术和科学成就，奥杜邦成为美国首屈一指的博物学家。他去世后，继续与鸟类和自然保护相关联，几个致力于这些事业的组织以他的名字命名。然而，近年来，随着奥杜邦的奴隶主身份和掠夺原住民坟墓的行为被更广泛地承认，越来越多的这些团体正在改名。
items.cardline_zh: 利爪擒获，羽翼间藏住野性的尊严
items.cardline: Talons seize prey, wings hold wild dignity

items.title: Nathaniel Hurd
items.description: Hurd was a prominent silversmith and engraver in Boston, and the warm gaze and unforced smile in his portrait by Copley suggest the friendship between the two artists. Hurd's open-collared shirt, as well as the rakishly tilted turban that covers his shaved head in place of a ceremonial powdered wig, create an air of informality that is unusual for a portrait of this time.
items.description_zh: 赫德是波士顿著名的银匠和雕刻师，科普利为他画的肖像中温暖的目光和不做作的微笑暗示了两位艺术家之间的友谊。赫德敞开的衬衫，以及代替正式粉状假发、歪戴在光头之上的头巾，营造出一种非正式的氛围，这在当时的肖像画中是不寻常的。
items.cardline_zh: 无袍无冠，微笑里住着自由的灵魂
items.cardline: No wig, no robe, his smile houses a free soul

items.title: Samuel Williams
items.description: Allston’s portrait of his London banker and business agent explores the experience of reverie. Williams looks off absently, perhaps inspired by the pictures in the book he holds. With its view of an Italianate landscape beyond the columns of this invented setting, the painting seemingly invites viewers to join the sitter in dreaming about faraway places.
items.description_zh: 奥尔斯顿为他在伦敦的银行家和商业代理人所作的肖像探索了遐想的体验。威廉姆斯心不在焉地凝视着，也许被他手中书里的图片所启发。透过这个虚构场景的柱廊，可以看到意大利式的风景，这幅画似乎邀请观众与画中人一起梦想遥远的地方。
items.cardline_zh: 书页开启，目光漫游在远方的山色
items.cardline: Book open, eyes wander in distant hills

items.title: Portrait of a Woman
items.description: At the age of 25, Rembrandt moved from his hometown of Leiden to the bustling city of Amsterdam. Within a few years he had become one of the city’s most successful portrait painters, creating remarkably vivid likenesses that seemed to express the personality and soul of the sitter. Here, Rembrandt used smooth, fluid brushstrokes to recreate the soft texture of the woman’s skin. In contrast, thicker, more textured strokes draw attention to the lavish display of her lace collar and gold jewelry—details sure to please a wealthy client. The extraordinary demand at that time for a portrait by Rembrandt meant that in many cases (including, probably, this painting) he called upon assistants to help complete the work.
items.description_zh: 25岁时，伦勃朗从家乡莱顿搬到了繁华的阿姆斯特丹。几年之内，他成为该市最成功的肖像画家之一，创作出极其生动的肖像，似乎表达了画中人的个性和灵魂。在这里，伦勃朗使用光滑流畅的笔触再现了女子皮肤的柔软质感。相比之下，更厚、更有质感的笔触突出了她奢华的蕾丝领子和黄金首饰——这些细节肯定会取悦富有的客户。当时对伦勃朗肖像画的巨大需求意味着在许多情况下（可能包括这幅画），他请助手帮助完成作品。
items.cardline_zh: 肌肤如脂，笔触如歌，灵魂在光影中微颤
items.cardline: Skin soft as silk, brush sings, soul trembles in light

items.title: Ruin by the Sea
items.description: The dark, ruined villa in this painting is made especially mysterious by the strange cypress trees tossed by the wind. A nervous green light flickers through the scene. Influenced by German Romantic art of the early 1800s, Böcklin was preoccupied with dreamlike images suggestive of death.
items.description_zh: 这幅画中黑暗破败的别墅因被风吹动的奇异柏树而显得格外神秘。一道不安的绿光闪烁在场景中。受19世纪初德国浪漫主义艺术的影响，勃克林专注于暗示死亡的梦幻意象。
items.cardline_zh: 风扭丝柏，绿光如鬼魅。
items.cardline: Cypress trees twist in ghostly green light.

items.title: The Power of Music
items.description: Set in rural Long Island before the Civil War, Mount's complex painting presents an African American laborer listening intently to a fiddle tune enjoyed by white men. While a love of music unites the figures in a bond of shared humanity, the two races occupy different spaces--one inside, one outside, both separated by a barn door--effectively symbolizing the pronounced divisions in America at the time.
items.description_zh: 这幅画以南北战争前的长岛乡村为背景，芒特复杂的画面呈现了一位非裔美国劳工专注地聆听白人演奏的小提琴曲。虽然对音乐的热爱将人物团结在共同的人性纽带中，但两个种族占据着不同的空间——一个在屋内，一个在屋外，被一扇谷仓门隔开——有效地象征了当时美国明显的分裂。
items.cardline_zh: 琴声相闻，门隔两重天。
items.cardline: Fiddle unites, barn door divides.

items.title: Branch Hill Pond, Hampstead
items.description: An amateur meteorologist, John Constable was most at home painting meticulously observed cloud formations, weather conditions, and natural light effects. He believed an accurate rendering of these constantly shifting elements could convey his vision of the vitality and magnificence of the English countryside. Working from the summerhouse he rented at Hampstead from 1819–26, Constable painted a landscape focusing more on the dark rain clouds than on the laborers in the foreground, keeping the details of their backbreaking work at a picturesque distance.
items.title_zh: 汉普斯特德的布兰奇山池塘
items.description_zh: 作为业余气象学家，约翰·康斯特布尔最擅长描绘细致观察的云层、天气状况和自然光效。他认为准确表现这些不断变化的元素能够传达他对英国乡村活力与壮丽的愿景。康斯特布尔在1819年至1826年间租住于汉普斯特德的避暑别墅中创作，这幅风景画更多地聚焦于乌云而非前景中的劳动者，将他们艰苦劳作的细节保持在如画的远处。
items.cardline_zh: 流云与光，皆入牧人眼。
items.cardline: Clouds and light, the shepherd's sky.

items.title: Elizabeth Shewell West and Her Son, Raphael
items.description: West was the first American artist to study in Italy, where he spent three years before permanently settling in London. He so admired the artistic ideals of the Italian Renaissance master Raphael that he named his eldest son after him, and he imitated Raphael’s celebrated Madonna of the Chair when composing this tender double portrait of his wife and child.
items.title_zh: 伊丽莎白·谢维尔·韦斯特与她的儿子拉斐尔
items.description_zh: 韦斯特是第一位在意大利学习的美国艺术家，他在那里度过了三年，之后永久定居伦敦。他非常钦佩意大利文艺复兴大师拉斐尔的艺术理想，因此以他的名字命名自己的长子，并在创作这幅妻子与孩子的温柔双人肖像时模仿了拉斐尔著名的《椅中圣母》。
items.cardline_zh: 圣母怀中，母子亦圣洁。
items.cardline: Mother and child, a modern Madonna.

items.title: Portrait of Lisa Colt Curtis
items.description: One of the most sought-after painters of his era, Sargent achieved considerable critical and financial success portraying cosmopolitan members of high society on both sides of the Atlantic. Here, the artist depicts an acquaintance—a relative of the Colt firearms family—who had recently married his distant cousin Ralph. In her portrait, Curtis wears an elegant satin dress and poses as if she were welcoming guests into her palatial Venetian home. The painting apparently was a wedding gift to the couple by the artist; its inscription at the top right reads, "To Ralph and Mrs. Ralph, John S. Sargent 1898."
items.title_zh: 丽莎·科尔特·柯蒂斯肖像
items.description_zh: 萨金特是他那个时代最受欢迎的画家之一，通过描绘大西洋两岸上流社会的国际人士，获得了巨大的评论和商业成功。在这幅画中，艺术家描绘了一位熟人——科尔特枪支家族的亲戚——她最近嫁给了他的远房表亲拉尔夫。在肖像中，柯蒂斯穿着优雅的缎面连衣裙，摆出仿佛在威尼斯豪宅中迎接客人的姿势。这幅画显然是艺术家送给这对夫妇的结婚礼物；其右上角的题词写道：“致拉尔夫和拉尔夫夫人，约翰·S·萨金特，1898年。”
items.cardline_zh: 绸缎流光，目光如远舟。
items.cardline: Satin gleams, her gaze a distant ship.

items.title: Water Lilies (Agapanthus)
items.description: A skilled horticulturalist as well as an artist, Claude Monet spent the last 30 years of his life painting the private garden he designed and helped cultivate at his home in Giverny in northern France. The resultant canvases are notable for their varied motifs, formats, and sizes. Monumental in scale, this rendering of his water lily pond focuses on the momentary effects of sunlight as it both penetrates and reflects off its shimmering surface. By zeroing in on the water and omitting its horizon and surrounding banks, Monet infers a limitless expanse—a perception amplified by the painting’s vast horizontal format that fills the viewer’s field of vision.
items.description_zh: 克劳德·莫奈既是技艺高超的园艺家，也是艺术家，他生命的最后30年都在描绘自己设计并帮助培育的位于法国北部吉维尼家中的私人花园。由此产生的画布以其多样的主题、格式和尺寸而著称。这幅描绘睡莲池的作品规模宏大，聚焦于阳光穿透并反射在波光粼粼水面上的瞬间效果。通过聚焦水面并省略地平线和周围河岸，莫奈暗示了无限的广阔——这种感知通过画作巨大的横向格式得以放大，充满了观众的视野。
items.cardline_zh: 日影穿水，莲叶接天光。
items.cardline: Sunlight pierces water, lilies float.

items.title: Elizabeth Beltzhoover Mason
items.description: The sitter’s high-waisted dress, pearl necklace, and elegant hairstyle of cascading ringlets reflect the height of fashion around 1800. Her gaze is assured: her chin is tilted upward but her eyes peer down with confidence. Mason’s husband was a prosperous lawyer, an influential figure in President Thomas Jefferson’s administration, and a plantation owner in Maryland. According to an inventory from 1807, the couple enslaved approximately 185 people, ranging from the newborn Abraham to 79-year-old Rachel. The family’s wealth afforded Mason the opportunity to be painted by Gilbert Stuart, a much sought-after portraitist.
items.title_zh: 伊丽莎白·贝尔特胡佛·梅森
items.description_zh: 画中人物高腰连衣裙、珍珠项链以及瀑布般卷发的优雅发型反映了1800年左右时尚的巅峰。她的目光自信：下巴微微上扬，但眼睛自信地向下凝视。梅森的丈夫是一位富有的律师，在托马斯·杰斐逊总统政府中具有影响力的人物，也是马里兰州的种植园主。根据1807年的清单，这对夫妇奴役了大约185人，从新生儿亚伯拉罕到79岁的雷切尔。家族的财富使梅森有机会被吉尔伯特·斯图尔特这位备受追捧的肖像画家描绘。
items.cardline_zh: 珍珠垂颈，目光傲如霜。
items.cardline: Pearls and curls, a confident gaze.

items.title: Vale of Kashmir
items.description: Cincinnati-based Duncanson was the first African American artist to achieve recognition both nationally and abroad. This panoramic painting, one of his grandest, was inspired by an episode in Thomas Moore’s then-popular epic poem, Lalla-Rookh (1817), which describes a Persian princess’s journey to the Indian subcontinent to be married. In Duncanson’s conception, members of a courtly entourage depart a quasi-Islamic palace, arriving by boat onto a scrim of land where they ascend a monumental staircase to a plaza with a fountain spraying an impressive plume of water. Despite these substantial storytelling details, the human elements remain subordinate to the overwhelming splendor of the natural landscape in and of itself.
items.description_zh: 辛辛那提的邓肯森是第一位在国内和国际上获得认可的非裔美国艺术家。这幅全景画是他最宏伟的作品之一，灵感来自托马斯·摩尔当时流行的史诗《拉拉·鲁克》（1817年）中的一个情节，该诗描述了一位波斯公主前往印度次大陆结婚的旅程。在邓肯森的构思中，宫廷随从的成员离开一座准伊斯兰宫殿，乘船到达一片狭长的土地，在那里他们登上宏伟的楼梯，来到一个广场，广场上的喷泉喷出壮观的水柱。尽管有这些丰富的叙事细节，但人类元素仍然从属于自然景观本身的壮丽辉煌。
items.cardline_zh: 诗行化景，远山藏宫阙。
items.cardline: Epic landscape, mountains hide palaces.

items.title: Mme L . . . (Laure Borreau)
items.description: Gustave Courbet painted four portraits of MadameLaure Borreau; this is the last and most highlydeveloped. As a Realist, Courbet aimed to representwhat he saw in the visible, tangible world. Herepresented Madame Borreau in a black silk dressembellished with lace and a matching hat. Goldand violet lights of sunset softly illuminate thebackground. However, the portrait is not idealized.Courbet’s inclusion of her shadowed eyes and full-fleshedneck prompted one critic to write, “I daresayin nature she may be very pretty, but in Courbet’spicture she is nothing less than offensive.”
items.description_zh: 古斯塔夫·库尔贝为劳尔·博罗夫人画了四幅肖像；这是最后一幅，也是最成熟的一幅。作为现实主义者，库尔贝旨在表现他在可见、可触的世界中看到的东西。他将博罗夫人描绘成穿着黑色丝绸连衣裙，饰有蕾丝和相配的帽子。日落时金色和紫色的光线柔和地照亮了背景。然而，这幅肖像并非理想化。库尔贝描绘了她阴影下的眼睛和丰满的脖子，促使一位评论家写道：“我敢说，在自然中她可能非常漂亮，但在库尔贝的画中，她简直令人不悦。”
items.cardline_zh: 黑缎映霞，真实不饰容。
items.cardline: Black silk and sunset, unadorned truth.

items.title: Two Poplars in the Alpilles near Saint-Rémy
items.description: Vincent van Gogh painted this autumnal landscape while interned at a psychiatric hospital near Saint-Rémy in southern France. Although initially restricted to painting in his room, he soon resumed working outdoors. This painting reveals the full power of his mature style. Trees twist and lean against a darkening sky, while the intense colors applied with charged brushstrokes convey his emotional reaction to the subject.
items.title_zh: 圣雷米附近阿尔皮耶的两棵白杨树
items.description_zh: 文森特·梵高在法国南部圣雷米附近的一家精神病院住院期间画了这幅秋日风景。虽然最初被限制在房间里作画，但他很快恢复了户外创作。这幅画揭示了他成熟风格的全部力量。树木在渐暗的天空下扭曲倾斜，而用充满活力的笔触涂抹的强烈色彩传达了他对主题的情感反应。
items.cardline_zh: 白杨扭曲，天空将入夜。
items.cardline: Twisting poplars against a darkening sky.

items.title: Noah:  The Eve of the Deluge
items.description: This painting powerfully illustrates changes in taste over time. Before it was even completed, the painting was purchased for the considerable sum of £1,000 by a newly wealthy industrialist who admired John Linnell’s modern approach to religious subjects. In 1913, however, the painting was sold at auction for only £130. Victorian painting had fallen out of favor among collectors who did not appreciate Linnell’s hotly colored landscapes that combined biblical stories and poetry (in this case Milton’s Paradise Lost) with close study of the English landscape. Linnell was also known for financially supporting the destitute and elderly William Blake, whose mesmerizing painting of Saint Matthew is in the museum's collection.
items.description_zh: 这幅画有力地说明了品味随时间的变化。在完成之前，这幅画就被一位新近富有的实业家以1000英镑的巨款购得，他欣赏约翰·林内尔对宗教主题的现代处理方式。然而，在1913年，这幅画在拍卖会上仅以130英镑售出。维多利亚时代的绘画在收藏家中失宠，他们不欣赏林内尔将圣经故事和诗歌（此处为弥尔顿的《失乐园》）与对英国风景的细致研究相结合的热烈色彩风景画。林内尔还以在经济上支持贫困年迈的威廉·布莱克而闻名，布莱克那幅迷人的《圣马太》画作收藏于该博物馆。
items.cardline_zh: 洪涛将至，鸦影压苍黄。
items.cardline: Crows gather as the deluge nears.

items.title: Fight Between a Tiger and a Buffalo
items.description: Having never ventured outside France, Henri Rousseau derived his jungle scenes from reading travel books and visiting the Paris botanical garden. He placed this imaginary scene of a tiger attacking a buffalo within a fantastic jungle environment in which botanical accuracy was of little importance (note the bananas growing upside down). Here, sharply outlined hothouse plants are enlarged to fearsome proportions. Rousseau was working on this painting while imprisoned for fraud in December 1907. Officials granted him an early release to finish it for exhibition at the Salon des Indépendants, where this major composition, one of the artist's largest and most important, appeared in March 1908. A self-taught artist and retired customs inspector, Rousseau was admired by Pablo Picasso and other avant-garde artists for his originality and the naïve purity of his vision.
items.description_zh: 亨利·卢梭从未踏出法国，他通过阅读旅行书籍和参观巴黎植物园来构思他的丛林场景。他将这幅老虎攻击水牛的想象场景置于一个奇妙的丛林环境中，其中植物学的准确性并不重要（注意香蕉倒挂着生长）。在这里，轮廓鲜明的温室植物被放大到可怕的比例。卢梭在1907年12月因欺诈入狱期间正在创作这幅画。官员们允许他提前获释以完成这幅画，以便在1908年3月的独立沙龙上展出，这幅重要作品是艺术家最大、最重要的作品之一。作为自学成才的艺术家和退休的海关检查员，卢梭因其原创性和天真纯粹的视野而受到巴勃罗·毕加索和其他前卫艺术家的钦佩。
items.cardline_zh: 蕉叶斜生，虎牛斗蛮林。
items.cardline: Upside-down bananas, tiger versus buffalo.

items.title: George Washington at Princeton
items.description:
Because Peale served under General Washington and befriended him during the Revolutionary War, he was an ideal candidate to commemorate Washington's important early victory at Princeton, NJ. Here, in the quiet aftermath of battle, the general is accompanied by his horse and a groomsman, identified by some scholars as William Lee, a mixed-race enslaved man Washington forced to work as his personal assistant. Also present is a line of captured British redcoat soldiers that animates the background at left.

Peale produced several versions of this composition amid a rally of enthusiasm for the American cause. Whereas some were commissioned by American colonists—as in the case here—others were ordered by Washington's admirers in France, Spain, Holland, Cuba, and even England.
items.title_zh: 普林斯顿的乔治·华盛顿
items.description_zh:
由于皮尔曾在华盛顿将军麾下服役，并在独立战争期间与他成为朋友，因此他是纪念华盛顿在普林斯顿（新泽西州）早期重要胜利的理想人选。在这里，在战斗后的宁静中，将军由他的马和一名马夫陪同，一些学者认为这名马夫是威廉·李，一位被华盛顿强迫作为私人助理的混血奴隶。左侧背景中还有一排被俘的英国红衣士兵，使画面充满活力。

皮尔在美国事业热情高涨的浪潮中创作了这幅作品的几个版本。有些版本是由美国殖民者委托创作的——就像这里的情况一样——而其他版本则是由华盛顿在法国、西班牙、荷兰、古巴甚至英国的崇拜者订购的。
items.cardline_zh: 战袍未解，身后有缰绳。
items.cardline: Victory's calm, the enslaved man waits.

items.title: British Manufactory; A Sketch
items.medium: oil on paper mounted on wood
items.description: As part of his work for the British court, Benjamin West produced several compositions celebrating the strength of the nation's economy. In this example, women in classical costumes paint vases, spin, and weave—a comparison of British manufacturing with the accomplishments of antiquity. One of a series of nine related sketches, this design was copied as a large mural decorating the Queen's Lodge at Windsor Castle, a room eliminated by remodeling in 1823.
items.description_zh: 作为为英国宫廷工作的一部分，本杰明·韦斯特创作了几幅颂扬国家经济实力的作品。在这个例子中，身着古典服装的女性绘制花瓶、纺纱和织布——将英国制造业与古代的成就进行比较。作为一系列九幅相关草图之一，这个设计被复制为装饰温莎城堡女王小屋的大型壁画，该房间在1823年的改建中被拆除。
items.cardline_zh: 古典衣袍，织就帝国梦。
items.cardline: Classical robes spin an empire's dream.

items.title: Sunny Autumn Day
items.description: Highly prolific, Inness created more than 1,000 works during a career lasting 50 years. Dated just two years before his death, Sunny Autumn Day epitomizes his late style in which the physical landscape is transformed into something akin to a spiritual apparition. Featuring blurred forms and atmospheric effects from translucent colors, this scene reflects Inness’s mystical view of the natural world.
items.description_zh: 伊尼斯多产，在长达50年的职业生涯中创作了1000多幅作品。这幅《晴朗的秋日》创作于他去世前两年，是他晚期风格的代表，其中物理景观被转化为类似精神幻象的东西。这幅场景以模糊的形态和半透明色彩的氛围效果为特色，反映了伊尼斯对自然世界的神秘看法。
items.cardline_zh: 秋光朦胧，树影如魂魄。
items.cardline: Hazy autumn sun, trees like spirits.

items.title: Cupid and Psyche
items.description: David used the story of Cupid and Psyche to explore the conflict between idealized love and physical reality. Cupid, lover of the beautiful mortal Psyche, visited her nightly on the condition that she not know his identity. Cupid was usually depicted as an ideal adolescent, but here David presents him as an ungainly teenager smirking at his sexual conquest. David took inspiration from a number of ancient texts, including an obscure, recently published Greek poem by Moschus that describes Cupid as a mean-spirited brat with flashing eyes and curly hair.
items.description_zh: 大卫利用丘比特与普绪克的故事探讨了理想爱情与肉体现实之间的冲突。丘比特爱上了美丽的凡人普绪克，每晚都来拜访她，条件是她不能知道他的身份。丘比特通常被描绘成理想的青少年，但在这里大卫将他呈现为一个笨拙的青少年，对他的性征服露出得意的笑容。大卫的灵感来自许多古代文本，包括一篇晦涩难懂、最近出版的希腊诗人莫斯库斯的诗歌，该诗将丘比特描述为一个脾气暴躁的小子，有着闪亮的眼睛和卷曲的头发。
items.cardline_zh: 少年窃笑，爱欲非完美。
items.cardline: Smirking youth, love not idealized.

items.title: Portrait of the Ladies Amabel and Mary Jemima Yorke
items.description: Sisters Amabel (left) and Mary Jemima are shown playing on the grounds of their family’s country estate outside London. They wear loose, flowing garments invented specifically for the portrait rather than their usual outdoor dresses. Their father commissioned the painting to hang in the family’s city home in London’s St. James Square and it highlights their status as landowners. Joshua Reynolds painted this portrait after his return from Italy, where his studies inspired him to incorporate classical references. The famous ancient Roman Statue of a Girl with a Dove is echoed in Mary Jemima’s pose as she shields a dove from the family dog.
items.title_zh: 阿玛贝尔和玛丽·杰迈玛·约克女士肖像
items.description_zh: 姐妹阿玛贝尔（左）和玛丽·杰迈玛在伦敦郊外家族乡村庄园的场地上玩耍。她们穿着专门为这幅肖像发明的宽松飘逸的服装，而不是她们通常的户外连衣裙。她们的父亲委托创作这幅画，挂在伦敦圣詹姆斯广场的家族城市住宅中，它突出了她们作为地主的地位。约书亚·雷诺兹在从意大利返回后绘制了这幅肖像，他在那里的研究启发他融入了古典参考。著名的古罗马《持鸽少女雕像》在玛丽·杰迈玛的姿势中得到了呼应，她正保护一只鸽子免受家犬的伤害。
items.cardline_zh: 草地嬉戏，裙摆如云影。
items.cardline: Sisters at play, gowns like clouds.

items.title: Reading
items.description:
Morisot’s sister, Edma, posed for this painting of a woman seated on her shawl in a field, her eyes on the pages of a book, with a parasol and fan discarded on the grass. Her white dress reflects the light and shadows of the outdoor setting; its delicate floral pattern echoes the meadow’s wildflowers.

X-ray examination reveals that Morisot revised Edma’s eyes, which previously looked directly at the beholder, in a manner more typical of Manet’s figures (see The Railway on view in this gallery). Whereas women in Manet’s paintings often return the viewer’s look, Morisot’s figures are usually absorbed in tasks or private reflection.
items.description_zh:
莫里索的妹妹埃德玛为这幅画摆姿势，画中一位女子坐在田野里的披肩上，目光落在书页上，阳伞和扇子被丢弃在草地上。她的白色连衣裙反射出户外环境的光线和阴影；其精致的花卉图案呼应了草地上的野花。

X光检查显示，莫里索修改了埃德玛的眼睛，之前它们直接看着观者，现在更符合马奈人物的典型风格（参见本展厅展出的《铁路》）。而在马奈的画作中，女性常常回视观者的目光，莫里索的人物则通常沉浸在任务或私人沉思中。
items.cardline_zh: 书页静立，白裙映草花。
items.cardline: Reading in a field, white dress blooms.

items.title: Mount Starr King, Yosemite
items.medium: oil on canvas on panel-back stretcher
items.description:
During the summer of 1863, Bierstadt visited Yosemite Valley in California and made numerous sketches. Back in his New York studio, he used them to produce many majestic paintings, including this view of the distant granite peak Mount Starr King. Such scenes thrilled East Coast audiences and helped encourage early movements to safeguard natural wonders. In 1864, President Lincoln signed a bill preserving Yosemite as public property; it became a national park in 1890.

Bierstadt included two Native Americans with a packhorse in his composition, and their presence references the fact that Indigenous peoples had inhabited or seasonally visited the region for millennia. Over time, various government and military agencies dispossessed them of these ancestral lands. Although no longer residents in the national park, descendants of the seven Indigenous nations with ties to Yosemite persevered to live in neighboring areas.
items.title_zh: 约塞米蒂的斯塔尔金山
items.description_zh:
1863年夏天，比兹塔特访问了加利福尼亚州的约塞米蒂山谷，并画了许多素描。回到纽约的工作室后，他利用这些素描创作了许多宏伟的画作，包括这幅远处花岗岩山峰斯塔尔金山的景色。这样的场景让东海岸的观众激动不已，并有助于鼓励早期保护自然奇观的运动。1864年，林肯总统签署法案，将约塞米蒂保留为公共财产；它于1890年成为国家公园。

比兹塔特在构图中包括了两名带着驮马的美洲原住民，他们的存在暗示了原住民数千年来居住或季节性访问该地区的事实。随着时间的推移，各种政府和军事机构剥夺了他们对这些祖传土地的所有权。尽管不再是国家公园的居民，但与约塞米蒂有联系的七个原住民国家的后裔坚持在邻近地区生活。
items.cardline_zh: 远峰如剑，松林沉寂。
items.cardline: Granite peak stands, forests silent.

items.title: Maine Coast
items.description: Located off the coast of Maine, Monhegan Island has long drawn artists attracted to its rugged headlands, brooding forests, and dramatic ocean vistas. Unlike most colleagues who visited during summer, Kent built a home and lived there year-round, working as a well digger, lobsterman, and carpenter from 1906–8. This boldly rendered winter scene was painted from his studio window.
items.description_zh: 位于缅因州海岸外的蒙赫根岛长期以来吸引着艺术家，他们被其崎岖的海岬、阴郁的森林和壮丽的海景所吸引。与大多数在夏季访问的同行不同，肯特建造了一所房子并全年居住在那里，从1906年到1908年，他从事挖井、捕龙虾和木工等工作。这幅大胆描绘的冬景是从他的工作室窗户画出的。
items.cardline_zh: 冬海苍茫，雪压孤窗。
items.cardline: Winter sea, snow at the window.

items.title: Calliope, Muse of Epic Poetry
items.description: The eldest of the nine Greek muses, Calliope was the goddess of music, song, and dance. She was also known as the goddess of epic poetry and conferred the gift of eloquence on kings and princes. She stands here before a bust of Homer, the ancient Greek poet who wrote the Odyssey and the Iliad. This painting belongs to a cycle of five works commissioned by businessman François Boyer-Fonfréde for his home in Toulouse.
items.description_zh: 作为九位希腊缪斯中最年长的一位，卡利俄珀是音乐、歌曲和舞蹈的女神。她也被称为史诗女神，并将口才赐予国王和王子。她站在古希腊诗人荷马的半身像前，荷马创作了《奥德赛》和《伊利亚特》。这幅画属于一组五件作品，由商人弗朗索瓦·布瓦耶-丰弗雷德委托为其在图卢兹的住宅创作。
items.cardline_zh: 笔尖栖神，史诗在静默中呼吸
items.cardline: Sacred ink rests, epic breathes in silent gaze

items.title: Portrait of Tieleman Roosterman
items.description: An extremely wealthy linen and silk merchant in Haarlem, Tieleman Roosterman was 36 years old when Frans Hals painted his portrait. Unlike many Dutch painters of the period, Hals favored strong, visible brushstrokes that convey the vitality of his sitters. Roosterman strikes a confident pose, his costume sober but luxurious: his sleeves are decorated with braid and buttons, and ribbon rosettes encircle the waist of his doublet. The rich black of his suit is offset by the broad linen collar and lace-edged cuffs—fashionable details befitting a successful cloth merchant.
items.description_zh: 蒂勒曼·鲁斯特曼是哈勒姆一位极其富有的亚麻和丝绸商人，弗兰斯·哈尔斯为他绘制肖像时他36岁。与当时许多荷兰画家不同，哈尔斯偏爱强烈、可见的笔触，传达出画中人的活力。鲁斯特曼摆出自信的姿态，服装朴素而奢华：袖子饰有编织带和纽扣，紧身短上衣的腰部环绕着缎带玫瑰花结。他服装的浓黑色被宽大的亚麻领和蕾丝边袖口衬托出来——这些时尚细节正适合一位成功的布料商人。
items.cardline_zh: 衣料低语，笔触是呼吸的余韵
items.cardline: Fabrics whisper, brushstrokes pulse with life

items.title: Painting Party
items.medium: hanging scroll; ink and color on paper
items.dimensions: Painting only: 139 x 69 cm (54 3/4 x 27 3/16 in.); Including mounting: 221 x 97.5 cm (87 x 38 3/8 in.)
items.description: In this collaborative painting, artists create works in response to patrons’ requests. Kawanabe Kyōsai painted the figures and outlines of the screens and scrolls, and 10 bunjin completed them. Each calligraphy and painting has a signature. Physician and calligrapher Nakane Hanrei (中根半嶺) (1831–1914) wrote “longevity” (壽) with an enormous ink-filled brush. Painter Fukushima Ryūho (福島柳圃) (1820–1889) did the plum on the folding screen, while Matsuoka Kansui (松岡環翠) (1818–1887) did the lotus; the two seem to be standing nearby, pointing others toward their accomplishment.
items.description_zh: 在这幅合作画中，艺术家们根据赞助人的要求创作作品。河锅晓斋绘制了屏风和卷轴上的人物和轮廓，10位文人完成了其余部分。每幅书法和绘画都有署名。医生兼书法家中根半嶺（1831–1914）用巨大的饱墨毛笔书写了“寿”字。画家福岛柳圃（1820–1889）绘制了折叠屏风上的梅花，而松冈环翠（1818–1887）绘制了莲花；两人似乎站在附近，指着他们的作品。
items.cardline_zh: 墨色聚会，笔端流转众人心
items.cardline: Ink gathers, each stroke a shared soul

items.title: The Young Eastern Woman
items.description: Although the artist provocatively titled this painting Young Eastern Woman, it is obvious that the model is not Asian, but merely wears a Turkish costume. The rich fabrics and glowing light create an exotic atmosphere, revealing a Western fascination with "Oriental" images and themes.
items.description_zh: 尽管艺术家挑衅性地将这幅画命名为《东方少妇》，但很明显模特并非亚洲人，只是穿着土耳其服装。丰富的织物和发光的光线营造出异国情调的氛围，揭示了西方对“东方”图像和主题的迷恋。
items.cardline_zh: 异域衣香，禁锢于西方之眼
items.cardline: Exotic silk, captured in a Western gaze

items.title: Jeremiah Belknap
items.description: Lacking formal training, Badger got his start painting houses and signs in Boston, then shifted his focus to accommodate the increasing market for portraits in the colonies. Jeremiah Belknap is shown here at about 7 years old, but his attire gives him the appearance of a miniature adult. The artist’s portraits of children often include fruit and animals—the ripening peach in Belknap’s right hand suggests maturation, while the dog at his feet symbolizes loyalty.
items.description_zh: 由于缺乏正规训练，巴杰尔在波士顿从绘制房屋和招牌开始，然后转向适应殖民地日益增长的肖像画市场。耶利米·贝尔纳普在这里大约7岁，但他的着装使他看起来像一个小大人。艺术家为儿童绘制的肖像通常包括水果和动物——贝尔纳普右手中成熟的桃子暗示着成熟，而脚边的狗象征着忠诚。
items.cardline_zh: 稚童老成，手中桃果未熟
items.cardline: Child in man's clothes, peach still unripe

items.title: The Call
items.description: Painted on a remote island in Polynesia a year before the artist's death, The Call belongs to a series of late works that explore the mysteries of life and death. Two women stand with bare feet, as if on sacred ground. One woman gestures to someone outside the picture, perhaps responding to a "call" from fate or destiny. Gauguin realized his ambition of painting from memory and the imagination through such mysterious, dreamlike images.
items.description_zh: 这幅画作于艺术家去世前一年在波利尼西亚的一个偏远岛屿上，属于探索生死奥秘的晚期系列作品。两位女性赤脚站立，仿佛站在圣地上。一位女性向画外的人做手势，也许是在回应命运或天命的“召唤”。高更通过这种神秘、梦幻般的图像实现了凭记忆和想象作画的抱负。
items.cardline_zh: 赤足立于圣土，命运在呼唤
items.cardline: Bare feet on sacred ground, destiny calls

items.title: Early Morning After a Storm at Sea
items.description: The powerful Atlantic surf pounding against the desolate coast of Prouts Neck, Maine, provided primary subject matter for the dramatic paintings that Homer created during his final decades. This example, which the artist proclaimed as "the best picture of the sea that I have painted," was initially conceived as a watercolor. Undertaking the composition in oil after a lapse of nearly two decades, Homer patiently waited for the appropriate atmospheric conditions, executing the work in four different sessions spread over two years.
items.description_zh: 强大的大西洋海浪拍打着缅因州普鲁茨内克荒凉的海岸，为霍默在最后几十年创作的戏剧性画作提供了主要题材。这幅作品被艺术家宣称是“我画过的最好的海景画”，最初是作为水彩画构思的。在近二十年后用油画重新创作时，霍默耐心等待合适的大气条件，在两年内分四次完成。
items.cardline_zh: 风暴退去，海在喘息中平静
items.cardline: Storm retreats, sea breathes in quiet might

items.title: Adeline Ravoux
items.description: In May 1890, Vincent van Gogh arrived in Auvers, a small town north of Paris, where he rented a room at the inn of Arthur Ravoux. This portrait, completed during the last months of the artist’s life, depicts Ravoux’s 13-year-old daughter, Adeline. Van Gogh wrote that rather than photographic resemblance, he wanted his portraits to convey the “impassioned aspects” of contemporary life through the “modern taste for color.”
items.description_zh: 1890年5月，文森特·梵高抵达巴黎北部的小镇奥维尔，在亚瑟·拉武的旅馆租了一个房间。这幅肖像画于艺术家生命的最后几个月，描绘了拉武13岁的女儿阿德琳。梵高写道，他希望肖像画传达当代生活的“激情方面”和“现代的色彩品味”，而不是照片般的相似。
items.cardline_zh: 蓝衣少女，凝视中藏着时光
items.cardline: Blue-clad girl, time held in her gaze

items.title: On the Beach, No. 3
items.description: Prendergast developed a distinctive painting style featuring rounded patches of thickly applied color. Like many of his works, On the Beach, No. 3 visually approximates a densely woven tapestry.
items.description_zh: 普伦德加斯特发展出一种独特的绘画风格，以圆形厚涂色块为特点。与他的许多作品一样，《海滩上，第三号》在视觉上近似于密织的挂毯。
items.cardline_zh: 色块如织，海滩成斑斓梦境
items.cardline: Tapestry of color, beach a dream

items.title: George III
items.description: King George III commissioned more than 80 paintings from West, including this portrait that celebrates his military leadership by presenting him in a suit of armor. Such an image likely was intended to bolster George III's reputation, which had been tarnished the previous year when he was forced to formally recognize American independence.
items.description_zh: 国王乔治三世委托韦斯特创作了80多幅画作，包括这幅通过让他身穿盔甲来颂扬其军事领导力的肖像。这样的形象很可能旨在提升乔治三世的声誉，此前一年他被迫正式承认美国独立，声誉受损。
items.cardline_zh: 铁甲藏王权，光辉掩盖裂痕
items.cardline: Armor hides a crown, light veils the rift

items.title: The Sacrifice of Isaac
items.description: In this dramatic test of faith from the Old Testament book of Genesis, Abraham agrees to slay his son Isaac on God’s command. As Abraham raises the knife, an angel suddenly appears to halt the sacrifice. This work gains its power from the complex expressions of father and son, combining grief, strength, resignation, fear, and realization in their faces and bodies, the latter inspired by ancient sculpture and Michelangelo. Andrea del Sarto never finished this painting, and it lays bare his working methods. He transferred the design to the panel from a drawing, reinforcing the chalk with painted lines—best seen in the donkey at the far right. He then worked over the whole panel at once with thin, brushy veils of color, letting him alter the composition while painting—especially evident in the angel, Isaac’s body, and Abraham’s head.
items.description_zh: 在《旧约·创世记》中这一戏剧性的信仰考验中，亚伯拉罕同意遵奉上帝的命令杀死儿子以撒。当亚伯拉罕举起刀时，一位天使突然出现阻止了献祭。这幅作品的力量源于父子复杂的面部表情，结合了悲伤、力量、顺从、恐惧和领悟，他们的身体则受到古代雕塑和米开朗基罗的启发。安德烈亚·德尔·萨尔托从未完成这幅画，这暴露了他的工作方法。他将设计从素描转移到画板上，用粉笔加强线条——最明显的是最右边的驴。然后他同时用薄薄的、毛茸茸的色彩覆盖整个画板，使他在绘画时能够改变构图——尤其在天使、以撒的身体和亚伯拉罕的头部中明显可见。
items.cardline_zh: 刀光与天使，信仰的极限时刻
items.cardline: Blade and angel, faith at its edge

items.title: The Troubadour
items.description: The troubadour, a medieval traveling poet and entertainer, was a popular subject in 19th-century French art. Associated with chivalry and courtly love, the theme reflects a broader, romantic fascination with France's medieval past. Although Daumier was particularly inspired by the troubadour paintings of French Rococo artist Jean-Antoine Watteau of the 1700s, he rendered the subject here in a powerful style of simplified, muscular form that appealed to modern artists of his own time.
items.description_zh: 游吟诗人是中世纪旅行的诗人和表演者，是19世纪法国艺术中流行的主题。与骑士精神和宫廷爱情相关，这一主题反映了对法国中世纪过去更广泛的浪漫迷恋。尽管杜米埃特别受到18世纪法国洛可可艺术家让-安托万·华托的游吟诗人画作的启发，但他在这里以简化的、肌肉发达的形式的强烈风格呈现了这一主题，吸引了同时代的现代艺术家。
items.cardline_zh: 琴弦未动，歌声已穿越千年
items.cardline: Strings silent, song echoes through ages

items.title: Anna Dummer Powell
items.description: Less than two months before the 80-year-old Powell passed away, one of her sons commissioned this portrait in order to commemorate her life and create an heirloom for future generations. According to the sitter’s granddaughter, Powell was “a little woman of sober conviction and strong Presbyterian faith.” The painting passed through a line of female descendants until shortly before it was given to the museum.
items.description_zh: 在80岁的鲍威尔去世前不到两个月，她的一个儿子委托创作了这幅肖像，以纪念她的一生并为后代创造一件传家宝。据画中人的孙女说，鲍威尔是“一个有着清醒信念和强烈长老会信仰的小女人”。这幅画通过女性后代传承，直到不久前才捐赠给博物馆。
items.cardline_zh: 老妇面容，镌刻岁月与信仰
items.cardline: Aged face, etched with years and faith

items.title: A Home in the Wilderness
items.description: Gifford’s view of Mount Hayes in New Hampshire records human intrusion into a remote landscape. On the left riverbank a log cabin stands amid a recently cleared patch of land with several tree stumps, while figures in its doorway greet a man who has arrived with a canoe of supplies.
items.description_zh: 吉福德对新罕布什尔州海斯山的景色记录了人类对偏远景观的侵入。左岸上，一间小木屋矗立在一片最近清理过的土地上，有几棵树桩，门口的人正在迎接一位带着一独木舟补给品到达的男子。
items.cardline_zh: 荒野小屋，人迹侵入寂静
items.cardline: Cabin in wild, human mark on silence

items.title: Saint Jerome
items.description: Saint Jerome (about ad 347–420) translated biblical texts into Latin. Although he sometimes appears as a scholar in his study, Ribera represents him here as a penitent who withdrew to the desert, beating his chest with a stone to emulate Christ’s suffering and contemplating a skull symbolizing human vanity. Working in Naples, Ribera enthusiastically adapted Caravaggio’s characteristic use of light and dark and naturalistic models.
items.description_zh: 圣杰罗姆（约公元347–420年）将圣经文本翻译成拉丁文。尽管他有时以学者形象出现在书房中，但里贝拉在这里将他描绘成一位隐退到沙漠的忏悔者，用石头捶打胸膛以模仿基督的苦难，并凝视着象征人类虚荣的头骨。在那不勒斯工作期间，里贝拉热情地借鉴了卡拉瓦乔对明暗对比和自然主义模特的典型运用。
items.cardline_zh: 石击胸膛，骷髅静观虚空
items.cardline: Stone beats chest, skull stares into void

items.title: Morning Glory with Black
items.description: O'Keeffe is best known for close-up flower subjects whose magnified forms fill the entire space of each canvas. Likely inspired by similar compositions in modern photography, these images are not only celebrations of nature, but also striking essays in abstract design. Although many critics interpreted O'Keeffe's flower paintings as reflections of femininity in general and female sexuality in particular, the artist strongly opposed such readings. Throughout most of her career, she frequently attempted to persuade others to discuss her work without referring to her gender, writing on one occasion, "I have always been very annoyed at being referred to as a 'woman artist' rather than an 'artist.'"
items.description_zh: 欧姬芙以特写花卉题材闻名，其放大的形态填满了每幅画布的全部空间。这些图像可能受到现代摄影中类似构图的启发，不仅是对自然的赞美，也是抽象设计的精彩尝试。尽管许多评论家将欧姬芙的花卉画解读为女性气质乃至女性性欲的反映，但艺术家强烈反对这种解读。在她的职业生涯中，她经常试图说服他人讨论她的作品时不提及她的性别，曾写道：“我一直很恼火被称为‘女艺术家’而不是‘艺术家’。”
items.cardline_zh: 花心深处，暗藏宇宙脉动
items.cardline: Deep in bloom, a universe pulses

items.title: Apollo, God of Light, Eloquence, Poetry, and the Fine Arts with Urania, Muse of Astronomy
items.description: A recipient of the Grand Prix de Rome in 1789, Charles Meynier became a member of the Académie des Beaux-Arts in 1815 and painted large ceiling decorations at the Louvre and numerous pictures glorifying the Napoleonic legend. Erato, Muse of Lyrical Poetry belongs to a cycle of five paintings executed to fulfill a commission from businessman François Boyer-Fonfréde. They were intended to decorate his home in his native Toulouse and constitute a rare instance of a complete cycle of major paintings that remain intact rather than being split up and separately dispersed.
items.title_zh: 阿波罗，光、口才、诗歌与美术之神，与乌拉尼亚，天文缪斯
items.description_zh: 1789年罗马大奖得主，夏尔·梅尼耶于1815年成为美术学院的成员，并为卢浮宫绘制了大型天花板装饰，以及许多颂扬拿破仑传奇的画作。《埃拉托，抒情诗缪斯》属于一组五幅画作，为完成商人弗朗索瓦·布瓦耶-丰弗雷德的委托而创作。这些画作旨在装饰他在家乡图卢兹的住宅，构成了一组完整的主要画作系列，至今完好无损，而非被分割散落，实属罕见。
items.cardline_zh: 神与星辰，诗意在光芒中流淌
items.cardline: God and star, poetry flows in light

items.title: Rock at Sea
items.description: The radically stylized tendencies in Rock at Sea were motivated by Jonson’s earlier work as an avant-garde scenic and lighting designer for the esteemed Chicago Little Theatre. Inspired by the jagged coast of Ogunquit, Maine, the painting’s visionary rendering of nature embodies the artist’s long-standing interest in communicating mystical concepts. Later, Jonson would move to Albuquerque, New Mexico, and co-found the Transcendental Painting Group, an association of artists dedicated to expressing the imaginative and spiritual realms through abstract art.
items.description_zh: 《海中岩石》中激进的风格化倾向源于琼森早期作为芝加哥小剧院前卫舞台和灯光设计师的工作。受缅因州奥甘奎特锯齿状海岸的启发，这幅画对自然的幻想式呈现体现了艺术家长期以来对传达神秘概念的兴趣。后来，琼森搬到新墨西哥州的阿尔伯克基，共同创立了超验绘画团体，这是一个致力于通过抽象艺术表达想象和精神领域的艺术家协会。
items.cardline_zh: 海岩嶙峋，化作灵魂的风景
items.cardline: Jagged sea rock, a landscape of soul

items.title: Edge of the Woods Near L'Hermitage, Pontoise
items.description: Born to a French-Jewish family on St. Thomas island in the Caribbean, Pissarro painted this monumental landscape when he was desperately poor and struggling to sell his paintings. It depicts a man slumbering in the sun-dappled backwoods of the Hermitage, a rural village near Pontoise, where the artist had been living since 1872. Restricting his palette to pure hues, Pissarro applied brushstrokes in systematic diagonal patterns, producing an effect that he likened to knitting. This canvas was included in the fourth Impressionist exhibition of 1879.
items.title_zh: 蓬图瓦兹隐修院附近的树林边缘
items.description_zh: 毕沙罗出生于加勒比海圣托马斯岛的一个法裔犹太家庭，在极度贫困、难以卖出画作时绘制了这幅宏伟的风景画。它描绘了一个男人在蓬图瓦兹附近隐修院乡村的阳光斑驳的偏远树林中沉睡，艺术家自1872年以来一直居住在那里。毕沙罗将调色板限制在纯色调，以系统的对角线图案涂抹笔触，产生了他比作编织的效果。这幅画布被收录在1879年的第四届印象派展览中。
items.cardline_zh: 阳光斑驳，沉睡者在林间梦游
items.cardline: Dappled sun, sleeper dreams in woods

items.title: Flowers in a Glass
items.description: One of the first artists to specialize in flower painting, Ambrosius Bosschaert may have been inspired by the botanical gardens and scientific collections in his hometown of Middelburg. The flowers in this bouquet might be common today, but in the 1600s they were costly rarities. Bosschaert captured their fragile beauty with luminous colors and exquisite detail.
items.description_zh: 作为最早专攻花卉画的艺术家之一，安布罗修斯·博斯哈尔特可能受到家乡米德尔堡植物园和科学收藏的启发。这束花中的花朵今天可能很常见，但在17世纪它们是昂贵的珍品。博斯哈尔特用明亮的色彩和精致的细节捕捉了它们脆弱的美丽。
items.cardline_zh: 玻璃中花，瞬间即永恒
items.cardline: Flowers in glass, a fragile eternity

items.title: Egyptian Family (Sketch for "The Battle of the Pyramids")
items.description: In 1810, Gros exhibited a massive painting of Napoleon at the 1798 Battle of the Pyramids, one of the rare French triumphs in the failed campaign to conquer Egypt (1789-1801). After Napoleon first fell from power in 1814, the painting went into storage, until the new king Louis-Philippe chose to resurrect it for a history museum in Paris. However, perhaps to diminish Napoleon's significance, the government asked Gros to amplify the original with an addition at each end. This painting incorporates General Kléber, a famously successful military leader who had been excluded from the original painting.
items.title_zh: 埃及家庭（《金字塔战役》习作）
items.description_zh: 1810年，格罗展出了一幅描绘拿破仑在1798年金字塔战役中的巨幅画作，这是法国在征服埃及（1789-1801）的失败战役中少有的胜利。1814年拿破仑首次下台后，这幅画被收藏起来，直到新国王路易-菲利普选择将其重新用于巴黎的历史博物馆。然而，或许是为了削弱拿破仑的重要性，政府要求格罗在两端各增加一部分来扩充原作。这幅画加入了克莱贝尔将军，一位被排除在原作之外的著名军事领袖。
items.cardline_zh: 黄沙卷起历史的尘埃，母与子在巨幕前凝望
items.cardline: Sand and silence, a mother and child before empire's shadow.

items.title: A Painter
items.description: Meissonier often depicted artists and musicians of the 1600s and 1700s engaged in their work. This artist is busy painting a small canvas depicting a nymph and satyr. The artist’s 18th-century costume—nearly 100 years out of date at the time of the painting’s creation—transforms the scene into a charming historical fiction. Meissonier exhibited the painting at the Paris Salon of 1857.
items.description_zh: 梅索尼埃经常描绘17、18世纪的艺术家和音乐家从事他们的工作。这位画家正忙着画一幅描绘仙女和萨提尔的小画布。艺术家18世纪的服装——在画作创作时已过时近100年——将场景变成了一段迷人的历史虚构。梅索尼埃在1857年的巴黎沙龙上展出了这幅画。
items.cardline_zh: 画笔轻点，时光在画布上凝固成戏
items.cardline: Brush poised, the past becomes a charming masquerade.

items.title: Romaine Lacaux
items.description: This painting may be Renoir's earliest signed canvas. Its sensitive display of color and light communicates an ideal of delicate, youthful beauty. The luminous tones of the background drapery and of the child's white blouse result from the artist's careful observation of reflected light and color on translucent materials. The delicate nuances of color, particularly in the young girl's face, reveal Renoir's previous training as a decorator of porcelain. He painted this portrait, commissioned by the vacationing Lacaux family, during his stay at an artist's colony in the village of Barbizon, near Paris.
items.description_zh: 这幅画可能是雷诺阿最早签名画布。它对色彩和光线的敏感展示传达了一种精致、年轻美丽的理想。背景帷幔和孩子白色上衣的明亮色调源于艺术家对半透明材料上反射光线和色彩的仔细观察。色彩的微妙变化，尤其是小女孩脸上的，揭示了雷诺阿之前作为瓷器装饰师的训练。他应度假中的拉科家族委托创作了这幅肖像，当时他住在巴黎附近巴比松村的一个艺术家聚居地。
items.cardline_zh: 光影轻抚，少女的脸庞如晨露般纯净
items.cardline: Luminescence caresses youth, a fragile morning bloom.

items.title: Painting of One Hundred Themes
items.medium: Ten-panel folding screen affixed with album leaves (obverse), calligraphy (reverse), ink and color on silk
items.description: The front of this screen features an assortment of subjects: birds and flowers, landscapes, and scenes of everyday life, mostly in monochromatic ink with light colors. The screen’s reverse side conveys a number of classical poems about the fleeting beauty of the four seasons. Traditionally, only one side of a folding screen bears painted or embroidered images, since these were used as a background furnishing. In Korean houses by the 1800s and early 1900s, however, two-sided folding screens became noticeably popular, possibly inspired by Japanese double-sided folding screens, which mainly served as room dividers in Japanese households.
items.description_zh: 这幅屏风正面展示了各种主题：花鸟、风景和日常生活场景，大多为单色水墨加淡彩。屏风背面传达了几首关于四季短暂之美的古典诗歌。传统上，折叠屏风只有一面有绘画或刺绣图像，因为它们被用作背景家具。然而，在19世纪和20世纪初的韩国房屋中，双面折叠屏风变得明显流行，可能受到日本双面折叠屏风的启发，后者在日本家庭中主要用作房间隔断。
items.cardline_zh: 屏风上，四季的诗意在墨色中流转
items.cardline: Seasons whisper through ink, a screen of fleeting grace.

items.title: Nymphs and a Satyr (Amor Vincit Omnia)
items.description: Like his colleague Claude Lorrain, Poussin depicted historical and mythological subjects in landscapes inspired by the countryside around Rome. His themes were often complex, and frequently incorporated witty allusions to classical texts. Here, a playful cupid tugs Pan, the goatlegged Greek god of the woods, toward Venus, the goddess of love. The painting cleverly illustrates the Latin phrase amor vincit omnia, or “love conquers all” (in Greek, pan means “all”). The woodland setting represents the idyllic paradise of Pan’s home.
items.title_zh: 仙女与萨提尔（爱征服一切）
items.description_zh: 与他的同事克劳德·洛兰一样，普桑在受罗马乡村启发的风景中描绘历史和神话主题。他的主题通常复杂，并经常包含对古典文本的诙谐引用。在这里，一个顽皮的丘比特将潘——羊腿的希腊森林之神——拉向维纳斯，爱神。这幅画巧妙地诠释了拉丁短语“amor vincit omnia”，即“爱征服一切”（在希腊语中，“pan”意为“一切”）。林间环境代表了潘家园的田园天堂。
items.cardline_zh: 爱神轻拉，山林间欲望与天真共舞
items.cardline: Cupid tugs, where desire meets innocence in shaded groves.

items.title: Under the Trees (from "The Public Gardens")
items.description: This painting is from a nine-panel decorative series showing children playing in two of Paris’s most spacious parks: the Tuileries Gardens and the Bois de Boulogne. It depicts a game of hide-and-seek in which a girl conceals herself behind a tree. The stillness of the women resting on green chairs at the right is countered by children running at the left. Rather than a realistic rendering of the subject, Édouard Vuillard arranged the composition in bands of colorful, decorative patterns so that the painting seems like a tapestry.
items.description_zh: 这幅画来自一个九幅装饰系列，展示孩子们在巴黎两个最宽敞的公园——杜乐丽花园和布洛涅森林——玩耍。它描绘了一场捉迷藏游戏，一个女孩躲在树后。右边坐在绿色椅子上休息的女人的静止被左边奔跑的孩子所抵消。爱德华·维亚尔没有对主题进行写实渲染，而是将构图安排成彩色装饰图案的带状，使画作看起来像挂毯。
items.cardline_zh: 树影间，静默与奔跑编织成夏日的谜
items.cardline: Stillness and motion weave a summer hide-and-seek.

items.title: Rest
items.description: One of the most celebrated academic painters of his time, Bouguereau underscores the moral virtues of the Italian peasants in this painting by depicting the dome of St. Peter's in the distance. The idealized figures and their triangular grouping recall the Holy Family paintings by Renaissance master Raphael 400 years earlier. The painting was purchased directly from the artist by Cleveland banker and philanthropist Hinman B. Hurlbut in July 1879.
items.description_zh: 作为当时最著名的学院派画家之一，布格罗通过描绘远处的圣彼得大教堂穹顶，强调了这幅画中意大利农民的道德美德。理想化的人物及其三角形构图让人联想到400年前文艺复兴大师拉斐尔的圣家族画作。这幅画于1879年7月由克利夫兰银行家和慈善家欣曼·B·赫伯特直接从艺术家手中购得。
items.cardline_zh: 圣彼得穹顶下，疲惫化作永恒的安宁
items.cardline: Beneath the dome, labor finds its sacred rest.

items.title: Antiochus and Stratonice
items.description: Struck by a mysterious illness, Antiochus lies in bed near death. His grieving father, who has recently taken Stratonice as his wife, summons a doctor. Noticing that the sight of the young woman quickens Antiochus’s pulse and that passion causes his sickness, the doctor describes the youth’s predicament to his father, who selflessly offers Stratonice to his son. The subject, both a love story and an example of parental devotion, enjoyed considerable popularity well into the 1800s.
items.title_zh: 安条克与斯特拉托妮可
items.description_zh: 安条克被一种神秘的疾病击中，躺在床上濒临死亡。他悲痛的父亲，最近娶了斯特拉托妮可为妻，召来了医生。医生注意到年轻女子的出现使安条克的脉搏加快，激情导致了他的疾病，于是向父亲描述了年轻人的困境，父亲无私地将斯特拉托妮可献给了儿子。这个主题，既是爱情故事又是父母奉献的典范，一直流行到19世纪。
items.cardline_zh: 病榻上的心跳，泄露了不能说的秘密
items.cardline: A pulse betrays what lips cannot speak.

items.title: Mount Sainte-Victoire
items.description: During the last 20 years of his life, Cezanne repeatedly painted Mount Sainte-Victoire near his home in Aix-en-Provence in southern France. Cezanne painted this view from life at one of his favorite sites, a small road that led from Aix eastward toward Mount Sainte-Victoire. He set up his easel at the edge of the road where he could see two umbrella pines and the mountain towering over the scene. A branch of one of the pines extends from the left of the canvas across the sky, echoing the slope of the mountain, linking the near and the far.
items.description_zh: 在他生命的最后20年里，塞尚反复描绘他位于法国南部普罗旺斯地区艾克斯的家附近的圣维克多山。塞尚在他最喜欢的地点之一——一条从艾克斯向东通往圣维克多山的小路上——写生创作了这幅画。他在路边架起画架，可以看到两棵伞松和耸立在场景之上的山。其中一棵松树的树枝从画布左侧伸向天空，呼应着山的斜坡，连接了近处和远处。
items.cardline_zh: 松枝低语，山峦在色块中永恒矗立
items.cardline: Pines whisper as the mountain stands in colored light.

items.title: The Crucifixion of Saint Andrew
items.description: Saint Andrew’s efforts to introduce Christianity to Greece in the first century CE were met with hostility from local authorities. He was sentenced to die on the cross because he refused to acknowledge pagan gods. For two days, Andrew preached from his martyr’s station to an increasingly sympathetic crowd. Bowing to public demand, Andrew’s would-be executioners attempted to untie him, but their hands were mysteriously paralyzed. Andrew’s desire for martyrdom was thus fulfilled and he died enveloped in divine light. Caravaggio’s innovative interpretation involves the viewer more closely in the event by presenting the crucifixion as intimate and private, rather than as a gruesome public spectacle. Bold contrasts of light and dark suggest the presence of God. A masterpiece of Baroque painting, Caravaggio’s Crucifixion of Saint Andrew is the only altarpiece by the artist in America.
items.description_zh: 圣安德烈在公元1世纪将基督教引入希腊的努力遭到了当地当局的敌视。他因拒绝承认异教神祇而被判处死在十字架上。两天来，安德烈从他的殉难讲坛向越来越同情的群众布道。迫于公众要求，想要处决安德烈的人试图解开他，但他们的手神秘地瘫痪了。安德烈殉道的愿望因此实现，他在神圣的光芒中死去。卡拉瓦乔的创新诠释通过将殉难呈现为亲密而私密的事件，而非可怕的公开场面，使观众更紧密地参与其中。明暗的强烈对比暗示了上帝的存在。作为巴洛克绘画的杰作，卡拉瓦乔的《圣安德烈的殉难》是美国唯一一幅这位艺术家的祭坛画。
items.cardline_zh: 暗影中，圣者的光芒照亮十字架的沉重
items.cardline: In darkness, a saint's light hallows the heavy cross.

items.title: Portrait of Colonel Charles Heathcote
items.description: Assuming a relaxed but commanding pose, Colonel Charles Heathcote (1734–1797) stands beside a large oak tree in the foreground of an expansive landscape. He wears military dress, the single epaulette on his right shoulder designating his rank as colonel. Wright’s composition demonstrates his new-found fascination with landscape: while the figure is relatively smoothly painted, the landscape is painted far more energetically, using thickly applied daubs of color.
items.title_zh: 查尔斯·希思科特上校肖像
items.description_zh: 查尔斯·希思科特上校（1734–1797）以放松但威严的姿态站在广阔风景前景的一棵大橡树旁。他穿着军装，右肩上的单肩章标明了他的上校军衔。赖特的构图展示了他对风景的新发现：人物画得相对平滑，而风景则用厚厚的颜料点画得更加有力。
items.cardline_zh: 橡树旁，威仪与荒野在暮色中交融
items.cardline: By the oak, command meets the wild's calm gaze.

items.title: White Flower
items.description: O’Keeffe is best known for close-up flower subjects whose magnified forms fill the entire space of each canvas. Likely inspired by similar compositions in modern photography, these images are not only celebrations of natural forms, but also striking essays in abstract design. Although many critics interpreted O’Keeffe’s flower paintings as reflections of femininity in general and female sexuality in particular, the artist strongly opposed such readings. Throughout most of her career, she frequently attempted to persuade others to discuss her work without referring to her gender, writing on one occasion, “I have always been very annoyed at being referred to as a ‘woman artist’ rather than an ‘artist.’”
items.description_zh: 欧姬芙以特写花卉题材闻名，其放大的形态填满了每幅画布的整个空间。可能受到现代摄影中类似构图的启发，这些图像不仅是对自然形态的赞美，也是抽象设计的惊人尝试。尽管许多评论家将欧姬芙的花卉画解读为女性气质特别是女性性欲的反映，但艺术家强烈反对这种解读。在她职业生涯的大部分时间里，她经常试图说服他人讨论她的作品时不提及她的性别，曾写道：“我一直很讨厌被称为‘女艺术家’而不是‘艺术家’。”
items.cardline_zh: 一瓣纯白，在画面中绽放成无垠宇宙
items.cardline: A single petal blooms into an infinite white.

items.title: Point Judith, Rhode Island
items.description: One of only a handful of nocturnal scenes painted by Heade, this evocative painting captures the effects of glimmering moonlight on land, sea, and sky. Although the artist depicts a specific location, his nighttime rendering shrouds most details in shadow, imparting a greater sense of mood than place. Human presence manifests itself through two sailing ships on the left side of the composition.
items.description_zh: 作为希德仅有的几幅夜景画之一，这幅令人回味的画作捕捉了月光在陆地、海洋和天空上的闪烁效果。尽管艺术家描绘了一个特定地点，但他的夜间渲染将大多数细节笼罩在阴影中，赋予了比地点更强的氛围感。人类的存在通过构图左侧的两艘帆船体现出来。
items.cardline_zh: 月光轻洒，海与岸在夜色中低语
items.cardline: Moonlight murmurs on sea and shore in silver hush.

items.title: Diana and Her Nymphs Departing for the Hunt
items.description: Diana, the Roman goddess of the hunt, wears a crescent on her forehead, also identifying her as the moon goddess. Diana lived apart from men, accompanied by a group of nymphs; she often represented unattainable beauty or chastity. With a nymph at left fending off a lustful satyr, Rubens refers to a struggle between vice and virtue, combining a sensual display of female bodies with a moral undertone. To bring the viewer more fully into the narrative, Rubens pulls the full-bodied figures to the front of the picture plane, and Diana steps forward, activating the space between viewer and subject. The nymph at the right has the features of Isabella Brant, the artist’s wife, and can be compared with Rubens’s portrait of her, also in the museum's collection.
items.title_zh: 戴安娜与仙女们出发狩猎
items.description_zh: 戴安娜，罗马狩猎女神，额头上戴着新月，也表明她是月亮女神。戴安娜远离男人生活，由一群仙女陪伴；她常代表难以企及的美或贞洁。左边的一个仙女抵挡着一个好色的萨提尔，鲁本斯在此指涉了恶与善的斗争，将女性身体的感官展示与道德寓意相结合。为了让观众更深入地进入叙事，鲁本斯将丰满的人物拉到画面前方，戴安娜向前迈步，激活了观众与主题之间的空间。右边的仙女具有艺术家妻子伊莎贝拉·布兰特的特征，可以与博物馆收藏中鲁本斯为她画的肖像相比较。
items.cardline_zh: 森林深处，贞洁与欲望在追逐中交织
items.cardline: Chastity flees, as satyr's lust shadows the hunt.

items.title: Summer
items.description: This painting is a smaller version of a mural on the same subject painted for the Hôtel de Ville in Paris, which has a companion mural titled Winter. A writer at the time described Summer as "filled with joy, happiness, rest, and the apotheosis of life." Georges Seurat, Paul Gauguin, and Pablo Picasso were among the many modernists influenced by Puvis de Chavannes’s dreamlike themes and anti-naturalistic style of simplified, flattened forms.
items.description_zh: 这幅画是巴黎市政厅同一主题壁画的较小版本，该壁画有一幅名为《冬天》的姊妹画。当时的一位作家将《夏天》描述为“充满欢乐、幸福、休息和生命的升华”。乔治·修拉、保罗·高更和巴勃罗·毕加索是受皮维·德·夏凡纳梦幻主题和反自然主义简化扁平形式风格影响的众多现代主义者中的几位。
items.cardline_zh: 金色阳光里，生命沉醉于无尽的夏梦
items.cardline: Gold light drowns in summer's dream of joy and ease.

items.title: Low Waterfall in a Wooded Landscape with a Dead Beech Tree
items.description: Jutting from a dune in the foreground, the massive silvery trunk of a dead tree leads the eye across a waterfall and toward a distant sunlit field where travelers and a dog traverse a sandy path. Partly masked by trees, a ruined building is turned gold by the sun. In Jacob van Ruisdael’s landscapes, dead trees, waterfalls, and ruined buildings were visual expressions of the passage of time. Ruisdael devoted equal attention to the cloud-filled skies looming above the land, creating dramatic patterns of light and shadow and revealing the unseen movements of the wind.
items.title_zh: 林地瀑布与枯山毛榉树
items.description_zh: 前景沙丘上伸出一棵巨大的银色枯树树干，引导视线穿过瀑布，朝向远处阳光照耀的田野，那里有旅行者和一只狗走过沙路。部分被树木遮挡的废墟建筑被阳光染成金色。在雅各布·凡·雷斯达尔的风景画中，枯树、瀑布和废墟建筑是时间流逝的视觉表达。雷斯达尔同样关注笼罩在土地上空布满云彩的天空，创造出戏剧性的光影图案，揭示了风的不可见运动。
items.cardline_zh: 枯木指向天际，流水诉说着时光的痕迹
items.cardline: Dead tree points skyward as water tells time's tale.

items.title: The Ghost Story
items.description: During the 1880s, the Netherlands became a magnet for several American artists, among them Walter MacEwen. His most famed painting, The Ghost Story, presents a group of individuals in a Dutch interior listening with rapt attention to spooky narration delivered by a woman at a spinning wheel. A young girl clutches her doll in fascination.
items.description_zh: 在1880年代，荷兰吸引了几位美国艺术家，其中包括沃尔特·麦克尤恩。他最著名的画作《鬼故事》描绘了一群人在荷兰室内聚精会神地听一位坐在纺车旁的女人讲述恐怖故事。一个小女孩着迷地抱着她的娃娃。
items.cardline_zh: 纺车低吟，夜色中故事在耳畔蔓延
items.cardline: Spinning wheel hums as whispers weave a ghostly tale.

items.title: The Building of the Dam
items.description: Julian Alden Weir's painting shows the construction of the Scotland Dam, built between 1907 and 1909 on the Shetucket River near his summer home in Windham, Connecticut. The artist had a vested interest in the subject, as the dam ensured an energy supply to the area's inhabitants that included his family.
items.description_zh: 朱利安·奥尔登·威尔的画作展示了苏格兰大坝的建设，该大坝建于1907年至1909年间，位于他康涅狄格州温德姆避暑别墅附近的谢图克特河上。艺术家对这个主题有既得利益，因为大坝确保了包括他家在内的地区居民的能源供应。
items.cardline_zh: 铁骨与流水，在画中筑起新的地平线
items.cardline: Iron and water build a new horizon on canvas.

items.title: The Brook
items.description: Although Cezanne exhibited twice with the Impressionists, he rejected his friends’ goals of capturing the fleeting effects of light and atmosphere in their art. Instead, he sought to create balance among the forms and colors in his compositions. This painting depicts the valley of the Arc River that runs southeast of Cezanne’s home in Aix-en-Provence. Throughout the composition, he juxtaposed strokes of different hues, describing the leaves, branches, and the stream with color rather than drawing the outlines of forms.
items.description_zh: 尽管塞尚曾两次与印象派一起展出，但他拒绝了朋友们在艺术中捕捉光线和大气瞬间效果的目标。相反，他寻求在构图中的形式和色彩之间创造平衡。这幅画描绘了塞尚在普罗旺斯地区艾克斯的家东南方向的阿尔克河山谷。在整个构图中，他并列了不同色调的笔触，用色彩而非勾勒形式轮廓来描述树叶、树枝和溪流。
items.cardline_zh: 溪水在色彩中流淌，山石在笔触间呼吸
items.cardline: Streams flow in color, rocks breathe in brush and hue.

items.title: The Monastery of San Pedro (Our Lady of the Snows)
items.description: Although the location that inspired the painting has been subject to scholarly debate, many believe it derives from Church’s travels through the Ecuadorean Andes nearly a quarter century earlier. The composition reads as an allegory of spiritual salvation: perched atop a dramatic cliff, a brilliantly backlit monastery overlooks a shadowed foreground where a solitary figure navigates a rugged path.
items.title_zh: 圣佩德罗修道院（我们的雪中圣母）
items.description_zh: 尽管激发这幅画的地点一直存在学术争议，但许多人认为它源于丘奇近四分之一世纪前穿越厄瓜多尔安第斯山脉的旅行。构图读作精神救赎的寓言：一座被背光照亮的修道院栖息在陡峭的悬崖上，俯瞰着阴影的前景，那里有一个孤独的身影在崎岖的小路上前行。
items.cardline_zh: 悬崖上的光，引领旅人穿越黑暗的谷底
items.cardline: Cliff-top light guides the lone soul through shadowed depths.

items.title: The Rathskeller
items.description: Economic differences infuse Sloan’s scene of patrons at a Philadelphia bar. Here, a working-class woman sharing a beer with a companion captures the attention of a lone man who has ordered the more costly champagne. One contemporary critic characterized this wealthier man as “a spider watching for the fly.”
items.description_zh: 经济差异渗透了斯隆描绘的费城酒吧场景。在这里，一位工人阶级女性与同伴分享啤酒，吸引了独自点了更贵香槟的男人的注意。一位当代评论家将这个更富有的男人形容为“等待苍蝇的蜘蛛”。
items.cardline_zh: 酒杯间，阶级的暗流悄然涌动。
items.cardline: In the clink of glasses, class tensions stir.

items.title: Holiday on the Hudson
items.description: A sun-dappled scene of middle-class leisure, Holiday on the Hudson is an atypical work by Luks, who more often turned to gritty tenement subjects for inspiration. Throughout his career, the painter fashioned himself as a brash, profane, and hard-drinking antagonist to New York’s genteel art-world establishment. The museum purchased this work just four months before the artist was beaten to death in the wake of a speakeasy argument.
items.description_zh: 《哈德逊河上的假日》是一幅阳光斑驳的中产阶级休闲场景，是卢克斯的非典型作品，他更常以肮脏的廉租公寓题材为灵感。在整个职业生涯中，这位画家将自己塑造成纽约文雅艺术界的一个粗鲁、亵渎、酗酒的对手。博物馆在艺术家因地下酒吧争执被打死前仅四个月购入了这幅作品。
items.cardline_zh: 阳光斑驳，假日闲情如画。
items.cardline: Dappled light paints a leisurely escape.

items.title: Charles Apthorp
items.description: Called “the greatest merchant on this Continent” in his obituary, Apthorp helped transform Boston into one of the key commercial centers in the American colonies. When the fashionable painter Robert Feke came to town, Apthorp commissioned this elegantly tailored and confidently posed likeness of himself. The sailing ship in the distant background symbolizes Apthorp’s deep financial interests in the Atlantic trade, which included not only textiles, wine, and guns, but also enslaved people. In fact, the slave trade and its attendant commerce comprised a significant portion of colonial Boston’s economy until the decades after the Revolutionary War, when anti-slavery attitudes in New England gained momentum.
items.description_zh: 阿普索普在讣告中被称为“这片大陆上最伟大的商人”，他帮助将波士顿转变为美洲殖民地的主要商业中心之一。当时髦画家罗伯特·费克来到镇上时，阿普索普委托创作了这幅优雅剪裁、自信姿态的肖像。远处背景中的帆船象征着阿普索普在大西洋贸易中的深厚经济利益，这不仅包括纺织品、葡萄酒和枪支，还包括被奴役的人。事实上，奴隶贸易及其相关商业构成了殖民地波士顿经济的重要部分，直到革命战争后的几十年，新英格兰的反奴隶制态度才获得势头。
items.cardline_zh: 商人目光如海，航船在远方。
items.cardline: Merchant's gaze, a distant ship sails.

items.title: General Jean-Baptiste Kléber and Egyptian Family (Sketches for "The Battle of the Pyramids")
items.description: In 1810, Gros exhibited a massive painting of Napoleon at the 1798 Battle of the Pyramids, one of the rare French triumphs in the failed campaign to conquer Egypt (1789-1801). After Napoleon first fell from power in 1814, the painting went into storage, until the new king Louis-Philippe chose to resurrect it for a history museum in Paris. However, perhaps to diminish Napoleon's significance, the government asked Gros to amplify the original with an addition at each end. This painting incorporates General Kléber, a famously successful military leader who had been excluded from the original painting.
items.title_zh: 让-巴蒂斯特·克莱贝尔将军与埃及家庭（《金字塔战役》习作）
items.description_zh: 1810年，格罗展出了一幅描绘拿破仑在1798年金字塔战役中的巨幅画作，这是法国在征服埃及（1789-1801）失败战役中罕见的胜利之一。1814年拿破仑首次下台后，这幅画被存放起来，直到新国王路易-菲利普选择将其复活用于巴黎的历史博物馆。然而，也许是为了削弱拿破仑的重要性，政府要求格罗在两端各加一部分来放大原作。这幅画包含了克莱贝尔将军，一位著名的成功军事领袖，他被排除在原作之外。
items.cardline_zh: 将军的温柔，在异国孩童间。
items.cardline: General's gentleness amid foreign children.

items.title: Panoramic View of the Alps, Les Dents du Midi
items.description: Courbet was still working on this large landscape, intended for the Paris Universal Exposition of 1879, when he died in December 1877. He painted it during his exile in Switzerland, where he had fled after being condemned for subversive activities in the Paris Commune of 1871. The view looks south over Lake Geneva toward the mountains called Les Dents du Midi. While some areas are heavily worked with a palette knife, the lower right remains unfinished.
items.title_zh: 阿尔卑斯山全景，德杜米迪峰
items.description_zh: 库尔贝在1877年12月去世时仍在创作这幅打算用于1879年巴黎世界博览会的大型风景画。他在瑞士流亡期间画了这幅画，此前他因在1871年巴黎公社中的颠覆活动被判刑而逃往瑞士。视野向南越过日内瓦湖，望向名为德杜米迪的山脉。虽然有些区域用调色刀厚重地处理过，但右下角仍未完成。
items.cardline_zh: 群山静默，流亡者笔下的永恒。
items.cardline: Mountains silent, eternal in exile's brush.

items.title: Danaë
items.description: Danaë’s father feared a prophecy that his grandson would kill him, so he imprisoned his daughter to protect her from suitors. Yet Jupiter, king of the gods, fell in love with Danaë, and he came to her in the form of gold streaming from the sky. Gentileschi adopted Caravaggio’s method of painting directly from models, pulled to the front of the picture plane, which gives the painting a startling, tangible quality. However, the graceful way he handled paint and human gestures lends the work a poetic quality unique to the artist.
items.description_zh: 达娜厄的父亲担心一个预言说他的孙子会杀了他，所以他把女儿囚禁起来以保护她免受追求者的伤害。然而，众神之王朱庇特爱上了达娜厄，他以从天而降的金雨形式来到她身边。真蒂莱斯基采用了卡拉瓦乔直接以模特作画的方法，将人物拉到画面最前方，这赋予了画作惊人的真实质感。然而，他处理颜料和人体姿态的优雅方式使作品具有艺术家独特的诗意品质。
items.cardline_zh: 黄金如雨，囚室里神的目光。
items.cardline: Gold rains down, divine gaze in prison.

items.title: Interior of a Church
items.description: Although this spacious church interior is largely imaginary, Emanuel de Witte’s precise description of architectural forms convinces us that he depicted an actual place. To create a sense of familiarity, he included some specific architectural features from the Oude Kerk (Old Church) in Amsterdam. In the late 1500s, militant Protestant sects occupied Catholic churches in the Netherlands and stripped them of rich decorations and “idolatrous” images. The resulting spare, whitewashed interiors became a favorite subject of Dutch artists, who were intrigued by the interplay of light and shadow over the unadorned walls, and by the challenge of depicting perspectival space.
items.description_zh: 虽然这个宽敞的教堂内部大多是虚构的，但埃马纽埃尔·德·维特对建筑形式的精确描绘让我们相信他描绘了一个真实的地方。为了营造熟悉感，他加入了阿姆斯特丹老教堂的一些特定建筑特征。在16世纪末，激进的清教派别占领了荷兰的天主教堂，并剥离了丰富的装饰和“偶像崇拜”的图像。由此产生的朴素、粉刷过的内部成为荷兰艺术家最喜欢的主题，他们被光与影在朴素墙壁上的相互作用以及描绘透视空间的挑战所吸引。
items.cardline_zh: 空寂教堂，光影诉说信仰。
items.cardline: Empty church, light and shadow speak faith.

items.title: La Cervara, the Roman Campagna
items.description: Corot based his large oil painting on drawings and oil sketches made outdoors. Attracted to the beauty of the Italian countryside, he often sketched around Rome, where he lived from 1825 to 1828. This painting's highly structured composition, based on forms moving into the distance along a series of diagonals, is characteristic of Corot's early style and recalls the classical landscapes of 17th-century painter Nicholas Poussin.
