items.description_zh: 柯罗根据户外绘制的素描和油画草图创作了这幅大型油画。他被意大利乡村的美景所吸引，经常在罗马周围写生，他于1825年至1828年居住在那里。这幅画高度结构化的构图基于一系列对角线向远处移动的形式，是柯罗早期风格的特点，并让人想起17世纪画家尼古拉·普桑的古典风景画。
items.cardline_zh: 罗马郊野，古典的呼吸。
items.cardline: Roman countryside, the breath of classic.

items.title: Portrait of Jean Terford David
items.description: According to his own inventory, the astonishingly productive Sully painted more than 2,600 works during his career. Most of these paintings were commissioned portraits, including this one of John Terford David, who had just recently married. French-born David was an American officer who served as a paymaster during the War of 1812. His rank is indicated by the fringed epaulet on his left shoulder and the lack of one on his right. In composing the portrait, Sully ingeniously positioned David's body on an angle to emphasize the single epaulet and downplay the uniform's lack of symmetry.
items.description_zh: 根据他自己的记录，惊人高产的萨利在其职业生涯中创作了超过2600幅作品。这些画作大多是委托肖像，包括这幅约翰·特福德·戴维的肖像，他刚刚结婚。法国出生的戴维是一名美国军官，在1812年战争期间担任军需官。他的军衔由左肩上的流苏肩章和右肩上没有肩章来表示。在构图时，萨利巧妙地将戴维的身体倾斜以突出单个肩章，并淡化制服缺乏对称性。
items.cardline_zh: 军装笔挺，新婚者的坚毅。
items.cardline: Uniform crisp, a bridegroom's steadfast gaze.

items.title: The Trinity
items.description: This vividly colored altarpiece was made in Antwerp, the center of religious learning in Catholic Flanders. In the mid-1500s when the Catholic Church mounted a defense against the spread of Protestant reforms, known as the Counter-Reformation, it encouraged the production of religious images that promoted Catholic doctrine, offering opportunities for artists like Rubens, Van Dyck, and Van Balen. Van Balen’s painting depicts the traditional theme of the Trinity, with God the Father holding the dead Christ and the dove of the Holy Spirit hovering above. Unusually, God wears the purple papal tiara, intended to emphasize the pope’s role as God’s true representative on earth. Encircling the figures of the Trinity are angels holding the instruments of Christ’s Passion. On the clasp of God’s elaborate cape is a small figure of Saint Barbara, perhaps a clue to the original location of the work in a chapel dedicated to her.
items.description_zh: 这幅色彩鲜艳的祭坛画是在安特卫普制作的，安特卫普是天主教佛兰德斯的宗教学习中心。在16世纪中期，天主教会发起对抗新教改革传播的防御，即反宗教改革，鼓励制作宣扬天主教教义的宗教图像，为鲁本斯、凡·戴克和凡·巴伦等艺术家提供了机会。凡·巴伦的画作描绘了传统的三位一体主题，圣父抱着死去的基督，圣灵的鸽子在上方盘旋。不同寻常的是，上帝戴着紫色的教皇三重冠，旨在强调教皇作为上帝在人间真正代表的作用。环绕三位一体人物的是手持基督受难器具的天使。在上帝华丽斗篷的扣子上有一个圣巴巴拉的小雕像，这可能是作品最初位于供奉她的礼拜堂的线索。
items.cardline_zh: 圣光交织，三位一体永恒。
items.cardline: Holy light weaves, Trinity eternal.

items.title: The Holy Family with Mary Magdalen
items.description: The seemingly weightless figures, the startling color combinations, and the moody, imprecise setting all show El Greco’s interest in conveying the intense spirituality of these figures rather than their concrete reality. Above all, the painting addresses Mary’s role as mediator between Christ and the viewer. She supports her son, but her faraway look indicates awareness of his fate. Mary feeds Jesus fruit held by Joseph that is full of symbolic meaning, with the apple representing the fall of man; cherries, the blood of Christ; peaches, salvation; and pears, the sweetness of Christ’s virtue.
items.title_zh: 圣家族与抹大拉的玛利亚
items.description_zh: 看似失重的人物、惊人的色彩组合以及情绪化、不精确的背景都显示出埃尔·格列柯对传达这些人物的强烈灵性而非具体现实的兴趣。最重要的是，这幅画探讨了玛利亚作为基督与观者之间中介的角色。她支撑着她的儿子，但她遥远的目光表明她意识到他的命运。玛利亚用约瑟拿着的果实喂耶稣，这些果实充满象征意义：苹果代表人类的堕落；樱桃代表基督的血；桃子代表救赎；梨代表基督美德的甜蜜。
items.cardline_zh: 圣洁之光，穿透人间悲悯。
items.cardline: Holy light pierces, human sorrow felt.

items.title: The Holy Family on the Steps
items.description: This deceptively simple composition represents a complex meditation on the Holy Family’s role in the redemption of humanity. At the center, Mary presents the Christ child to the world. At the left, Saint Elizabeth leans forward to foretell his eventual death, while her son, Saint John the Baptist, offers Jesus an apple, signifying humanity’s fall from grace in the Garden of Eden. At the right, Saint Joseph holds a compass, a sign of his occupation as a carpenter and also symbolic of God the Father. Poussin developed his composition meticulously and deliberately, using clear primary colors, simple forms, and a geometric organization to express the central importance of the Holy Family in Christian belief.
items.description_zh: 这幅看似简单的构图代表了关于圣家族在人类救赎中角色的复杂沉思。在中心，玛利亚将基督之子呈现给世界。在左边，圣伊丽莎白向前倾身预言他最终的死亡，而她的儿子施洗约翰向耶稣献上一个苹果，象征人类在伊甸园中失去恩宠。在右边，圣约瑟夫拿着一个圆规，这是他木匠职业的标志，也象征圣父。普桑精心而刻意地发展了他的构图，使用清晰的原色、简单的形式和几何组织来表达圣家族在基督教信仰中的核心重要性。
items.cardline_zh: 阶梯之上，救赎悄然展开。
items.cardline: On steps, redemption quietly unfolds.

items.title: A Eunuch's Dream
items.description: This painting, inspired by Charles Montesquieu's Persian Letters (published in 1721), depicts a eunuch who wanted to marry a harem slave. He experienced a vision of her while smoking his opium pipe, but her little companion holding a knife dripping with blood reminds us that the eunuch's anatomy precludes the fulfillment of his dream. The outline of a hand next to the signature is a khamsa, a symbol used to ward off evil.
items.description_zh: 这幅画灵感来自查理·孟德斯鸠的《波斯人信札》（1721年出版），描绘了一个想娶后宫奴隶的太监。他在抽鸦片烟斗时看到了她的幻象，但她的小同伴拿着一把滴血的刀提醒我们，太监的解剖结构无法实现他的梦想。签名旁边的手形轮廓是hamsa，一种用于辟邪的符号。
items.cardline_zh: 烟斗里的幻梦，刀锋上的血。
items.cardline: Dream in smoke, blood on the blade.

items.title: Gardener's House at Antibes
items.description: In 1888, Claude Monet spent four months in Antibes, a city in southeastern France on the Mediterranean coast, to derive inspiration for painting. Although his visit was occasionally challenged by strong winds that threatened to knock over his easel, the artist was able to complete nearly 40 works. This especially vibrant canvas is a depiction of a gardener’s house set against the sea and the distant Alps. Monet portrays intense midday light through thickly applied paint in bright colors that evoke the region’s sun-drenched climate. Small daubs of green on the slender trees framing the house suggest the onset of spring.
items.description_zh: 1888年，克劳德·莫奈在法国东南部地中海沿岸城市昂蒂布度过了四个月，以获取绘画灵感。尽管他的访问偶尔受到强风的挑战，差点吹倒他的画架，但艺术家完成了近40幅作品。这幅特别充满活力的画布描绘了一座园丁小屋，以大海和远处的阿尔卑斯山为背景。莫奈通过厚涂的明亮颜料描绘了强烈的正午光线，唤起了该地区阳光充沛的气候。框架小屋的细长树木上的绿色小点暗示了春天的到来。
items.cardline_zh: 阳光与海，园丁的梦幻小屋。
items.cardline: Sun and sea, a gardener's dream house.

items.title: The Brierwood Pipe
items.description: Created during the United States Civil War, Winslow Homer’s painting depicts two volunteers for the Union Army who sport their regiment’s highly colorful uniforms, a design discovered to be impractical due to its ability to be spotted by sharpshooters. Passing idle time in their encampment, one soldier holds a lit pipe and watches his companion whittle a block of wood. The painting shares its title with a contemporary poem, in which a soldier smokes a brierwood pipe and daydreams of the time when the conflict will end so he can return home. The museum acquired this painting during World War II, and it seems likely that the painting’s subject was viewed as especially significant for its wartime audience.
items.description_zh: 在美国内战期间创作，温斯洛·霍默的画作描绘了两名联邦志愿兵，他们穿着团里色彩鲜艳的制服，这种设计因容易被狙击手发现而被认为不实用。在营地消磨闲暇时光时，一名士兵拿着点燃的烟斗，看着同伴削一块木头。这幅画与一首当代诗歌同名，诗中一名士兵抽着石楠木烟斗，梦想着战争结束可以回家。博物馆在第二次世界大战期间购入了这幅画，似乎画作的主题被认为对战时观众特别有意义。
items.cardline_zh: 军旅间歇，烟斗与刀刻的时光。
items.cardline: War's pause, pipe and whittled wood.

items.title: Composition with Red, Yellow, and Blue
items.description: During the 1910s, Piet Mondrian’s art progressed from Cubism to an increasingly abstract style based on the form of a grid. He completely abandoned the representation of natural appearances, limiting himself to horizontal and vertical rectangles and three primary colors (red, yellow, and blue) along with black and white. Intensely intellectual and deeply philosophical, Mondrian believed that following the devastation of World War I, such abstract compositions could contribute to a more harmonious society by communicating in a universal, visual language.
items.description_zh: 在1910年代，皮特·蒙德里安的艺术从立体主义发展到基于网格形式的日益抽象的风格。他完全放弃了自然外观的表现，将自己限制在水平和垂直矩形以及三种原色（红、黄、蓝）以及黑色和白色。蒙德里安极具理智和哲学深度，他相信在第一次世界大战的破坏之后，这种抽象构图可以通过一种普遍的视觉语言进行交流，从而有助于建立一个更和谐的社会。
items.cardline_zh: 红黄蓝的寂静，宇宙的秩序。
items.cardline: Red, yellow, blue silence, cosmic order.

items.title: Orchid Blossoms
items.description: At the time he created this work, Martin Johnson Heade had made three trips to Central and South America. As an outgrowth of these experiences, he made a large number of paintings of birds and flowers, especially orchids, in a tropical setting.
items.description_zh: 在创作这幅作品时，马丁·约翰逊·希德已经三次前往中美洲和南美洲。作为这些经历的产物，他创作了大量鸟类和花卉的画作，尤其是热带环境中的兰花。
items.cardline_zh: 热带幽兰，静谧绽放。
items.cardline: Tropical orchid, silent bloom.

items.title: Portrait of George Pitt, First Baron Rivers
items.description: Although George Pitt reputedly led a life of debauchery and was a cruel husband, Thomas Gainsborough’s portrait endows him with an aura of dignity and refinement. This work was painted between Pitt’s appointments as ambassador to Turin, Italy, and Madrid, Spain, but he is very much the English gentleman at home on his country estate. Gainsborough balances the deep scarlet and green facing of Pitt’s military uniform with the cool, woodland landscape painted with wide, liquid brushstrokes of mauves, grays, and silvers.
items.title_zh: 乔治·皮特肖像，第一代里弗斯男爵
items.description_zh: 尽管乔治·皮特据称过着放荡的生活，并且是一个残酷的丈夫，但托马斯·庚斯博罗的肖像赋予了他尊严和文雅的光环。这幅作品是在皮特被任命为驻都灵（意大利）和马德里（西班牙）大使之间绘制的，但他完全是英国绅士，在自己的乡村庄园里。庚斯博罗用宽阔、流动的紫灰色、灰色和银色笔触绘制的凉爽林地景观，平衡了皮特军装深红色和绿色的镶边。
items.cardline_zh: 绅士的华服，难掩放浪之魂。
items.cardline: Gentleman's finery, a debauched soul hides.

items.title: Winding Yarn (Interior of a Nantucket Kitchen)
items.description: Set in a rustic kitchen on Nantucket Island, Massachusetts, this painting depicts a woman who winds a ball of yarn from a coil looped in the hands of a man sitting across from her at a respectable distance. At the time, winding yarn was a common symbol of courtship that carried humorous overtones of a woman ensnaring her suitor. The second woman in the composition is likely a chaperone. The suitor’s unrefined, open-legged pose, coupled with his rude action of placing his hat on the floor, adds further comic elements that contemporary audiences would have appreciated.
items.title_zh: 绕纱线（南塔基特厨房内景）
items.description_zh: 这幅画以马萨诸塞州南塔基特岛的一个乡村厨房为背景，描绘了一名女子从坐在对面适当距离的男性手中的线圈绕线球。当时，绕纱线是求爱的常见象征，带有女性诱捕追求者的幽默意味。构图中的第二个女子很可能是 chaperone。追求者不文雅的叉腿姿势，加上他把帽子放在地板上的粗鲁行为，增添了当代观众会欣赏的喜剧元素。
items.cardline_zh: 线团缠绕，乡村的温柔情网。
items.cardline: Yarn winds tight, a rural love snare.

items.title: The Seine at Bas-Meudon
items.description: Although born and trained in Holland, Jongkind spent much of his life painting outdoors in France. In this depiction of Bas-Meudon near Paris, the artist applied paint in small patches of bright color to suggest the intensity of outdoor light. Although typcially finished in the studio from open-air sketches, Jongkind's oil paintings achieve a convincing immediacy that greatly impressed the young Claude Monet. The two met in the early 1860s and spent part of a summer painting together along the coast of Normandy. "From that time he was my real master," Monet later acknowledged, "it was to him that I owe the final education of my eye."
items.description_zh: 尽管出生并在荷兰受训，容金德大部分时间在法国户外作画。在这幅描绘巴黎附近默东的作品中，艺术家用小片亮色颜料来暗示户外光线的强度。虽然通常根据户外写生草图在画室完成，但容金德的油画达到了令人信服的即时性，给年轻的克劳德·莫奈留下了深刻印象。两人在1860年代初相遇，并一起在诺曼底海岸度过了一个夏天的部分时间作画。“从那时起，他就是我真正的老师，”莫奈后来承认，“我的眼睛最终的教育归功于他。”
items.cardline_zh: 塞纳河畔，光的碎片跳跃。
items.cardline: Seine's edge, fragments of light leap.

items.title: Portrait of Isabella Brant
items.description: Isabella Brant was Rubens’s first wife. He painted her many times in many different ways before she died at the age of 35 (she is a model for a nymph in the other work by Rubens in this room). This intimate portrait, painted at least five years before her death, suggests the close relationship between the painter and sitter through the warm palette, the luscious brushstrokes, and her lively expression.
items.description_zh: 伊莎贝拉·布兰特是鲁本斯的第一任妻子。在她35岁去世前，鲁本斯以多种方式多次描绘她（她是本展厅鲁本斯另一幅作品中宁芙的模特）。这幅亲密的肖像画于她去世前至少五年完成，通过温暖的色调、丰润的笔触和她生动的表情，暗示了画家与模特之间的亲密关系。
items.cardline_zh: 暖色笔触间的私语，生命绽放前的永恒瞬间
items.cardline: Warmth in brushstrokes, a life captured before farewell.

items.title: The Return of the Holy Family to Nazareth
items.description: When King Herod learned that he would lose his throne to a child born in Bethlehem, he ordered all male infants to be massacred. Mary and Joseph thus fled to Egypt to protect their son, Jesus. The age of the boy in this painting suggests that Poussin depicted the less-common subject of the Holy Family’s return from Egypt. Jesus reaches toward a vision of a cross borne by angels, a reference to both his eventual crucifixion and Herod’s cruel slaughter of innocent children.
items.description_zh: 希律王得知自己将因伯利恒诞生的一个孩子而失去王位后，下令屠杀所有男婴。因此玛利亚和约瑟带着儿子耶稣逃往埃及。画中男孩的年龄表明，普桑描绘的是较少见的圣家族从埃及返回的主题。耶稣伸手朝向天使托举的十字架幻象，这既预示了他最终的受难，也呼应了希律王对无辜婴儿的残酷屠杀。
items.cardline_zh: 圣途斜阳里，孩童遥指天光十字
items.cardline: Child reaches for cross, journey bathed in light.

items.title: Pond at Ville-d'Avray
items.description: Corot painted this view of a pond near his home at Ville-d'Avray, just west of Paris, in his late style distinguished by soft, hazy forms and gentle, silvery light. Such paintings merge his lifelong study of nature with nostalgic memories and contrast sharply with the more formal compositions and precisely rendered shapes of his early landscapes. Corot's devotion to the direct study of nature was a major influence on the Impressionists.
items.description_zh: 柯罗以他晚期风格描绘了巴黎西部维尔达夫雷住所附近的池塘景色，其特点为柔和朦胧的形态与温婉的银灰色光线。此类作品融合了他毕生对自然的观察与怀旧记忆，与他早期风景画中更严谨的构图和精确的造型形成鲜明对比。柯罗对直接研究自然的执着对印象派产生了重大影响。
items.cardline_zh: 银灰光晕中，水面融化了记忆的轮廓
items.cardline: Silver haze melts pond and memory into one.

items.title: Baron FitzGibbon
items.description: A fearless politician who engineered the act of uniting Ireland with England, FitzGibbon was the newly appointed Lord Chancellor of Ireland (whose duties included that of chief judge) when Stuart painted his portrait. The crownlike upper portion of FitzGibbon's mace, or ceremonial staff, is seen on the table at the right. Propped against the table is a satin and velvet purse embroidered with the royal coat of arms. Worn around the neck like an apron, the purse held the official texts of FitzGibbon's speeches. At the time he painted this work, Stuart was living in Dublin, having spent a dozen years in London. In fact, his Irish patrons regarded him as a British painter. Stuart returned to his native America in 1793, where he remained the rest of his life.
items.description_zh: 菲茨吉本是一位无畏的政治家，策划了爱尔兰与英格兰的合并法案。斯图尔特为其绘制肖像时，他刚被任命为爱尔兰大法官（职责包括首席法官）。桌上右侧可见菲茨吉本权杖（仪式用杖）上端皇冠状的部分。靠在桌边的是一个绣有皇家纹章的缎面天鹅绒钱袋，像围裙一样挂在脖子上，内装菲茨吉本演讲的官方文本。斯图尔特在伦敦生活十二年后，于创作此画时定居都柏林。事实上，他的爱尔兰赞助人视他为英国画家。斯图尔特于1793年返回祖国美国，并在那里度过余生。
items.cardline_zh: 权杖尖端的凛冬，映着铁腕的寒光
items.cardline: Mace and velvet cloak a will of iron.

items.title: On Bos'n's Hill
items.description: Located in New Castle, New Hampshire, Bos’n’s Hill was a favored leisure spot for Tarbell’s family, which included four children. It provides the luminous setting for this depiction of the artist’s wife, Emeline, who strolls through the landscape with a parasol to protect her from the sun, while a beloved pet dog accompanies her. Four years later, the couple purchased a summer house nearby.
items.description_zh: 位于新罕布什尔州纽卡斯尔的博斯恩山是塔贝尔一家（包括四个孩子）钟爱的休闲之地。这幅画以明亮的光线为背景，描绘了艺术家的妻子埃米琳，她撑着阳伞在风景中漫步以遮挡阳光，心爱的宠物狗陪伴在侧。四年后，夫妇俩在附近购买了一栋避暑别墅。
items.cardline_zh: 阳伞微侧，与忠犬共享夏日流光
items.cardline: Parasol and dog share a leisurely sunlit stroll.

items.title: The Orange Christ
items.description: A devout Catholic, Maurice Denis sought to create what he described as “imperishable icons.” In this small painting, he used flat planes of intense colors to conjure emotion in the viewer. Here, a somber group of women, depicted as amorphous black forms, gather around an orange figure of Christ on the cross. The sky is a reddish-purple streaked with green clouds. Pink angels flank the crucified Christ whose head is haloed in brilliant yellow. In this painting, the daring young artist combined religious fervor with unprecedented visual experimentation.
items.description_zh: 作为虔诚的天主教徒，莫里斯·德尼试图创造他所说的“不朽的圣像”。在这幅小画中，他使用平涂的强烈色彩来激发观者的情感。画面中，一群阴郁的女性以模糊的黑色形态出现，聚集在十字架上橙色的基督形象周围。天空是红紫色，夹杂着绿色云彩。粉色的天使分列被钉基督两侧，其头部环绕着明亮的黄色光环。在这幅画中，这位大胆的年轻艺术家将宗教热情与前所未有的视觉实验相结合。
items.cardline_zh: 橘红圣体悬于混沌，哀恸凝成暗影
items.cardline: Orange Christ on cross; grief in blackened forms.

items.title: Berthe Morisot with a Muff
items.description: This is one of two portraits Manet painted of Morisot dressed for winter in a heavy coat and feathered hat. (See Berthe Morisot with a Half Veil, also in this gallery.) Manet’s wide brushstrokes and crosshatchings, which describe areas of shadow, evoke the sketchy quality of Morisot’s own paintings, likely Manet’s nod to his subject’s identity as an artist.
items.title_zh: 持手笼的贝尔特·莫里索
items.description_zh: 这是马奈为莫里索绘制的两幅冬装肖像之一（另一幅为《戴半面纱的贝尔特·莫里索》，亦在本展厅）。马奈宽阔的笔触和交叉排线描绘了阴影区域，唤起了莫里索本人绘画的速写质感，这可能是马奈对其模特艺术家身份的致意。
items.cardline_zh: 笔触如呼吸，描摹冬衣下的艺术家灵魂
items.cardline: Fur and brushstrokes mirror an artist's soul.

items.title: The Park-Winter
items.dimensions: Unframed: 91.5 x 122 cm (36 x 48 1/16 in.); Framed: 109.2 x 139.7 x 7.6 cm (43 x 55 x 3 in.)
items.description: During the 1920s, Kroll earned a great deal of success painting scenes of everyday urban life. In this example, he depicted ice skaters on a pond in snow-covered Central Park, with a looming skyline of Manhattan apartment buildings in the background.
items.description_zh: 1920年代，克罗尔因描绘日常都市生活场景而大获成功。在这幅作品中，他描绘了雪覆中央公园的池塘上滑冰的人们，背景是曼哈顿公寓楼林立的天际线。
items.cardline_zh: 滑冰者点破雪白寂静，都市天际线默默旁观
items.cardline: Skaters carve silence; skyline watches in snow.

items.title: The Pigeon Tower at Bellevue
items.description: Paul Cezanne painted this composition in three horizontal planes: the azure blue of the sky; the luminous white of the tower and the farmyard buildings; and the burnt-orange shade of the foreground earth, whose color is indicative of the iron-rich soil of Provence. The shimmering greens of Cezanne’s favorite cypress and olive trees are interspersed across the middle ground. The composition is pared down to its essentials, devoid of extraneous detail; it exemplifies his ambition “to make something solid and enduring, like the art in museums.”
items.description_zh: 保罗·塞尚将这幅构图分为三个水平面：天空的蔚蓝；塔楼和农舍建筑的明亮白色；前景土地的焦橙色，其颜色暗示了普罗旺斯富含铁质的土壤。塞尚最喜爱的柏树和橄榄树的闪烁绿色散布在中景。构图精简至本质，无多余细节；它体现了他的抱负：“创造出像博物馆艺术一样坚实而持久的东西。”
items.cardline_zh: 碧空、白塔、赭土，三重奏响南法阳光
items.cardline: Azure sky, white tower, ochre earth sing Provence.

items.title: Basket of Plums
items.description: During the later part of the 1700s, fruit still lifes were extremely popular, and a basket of plums was a common motif. In Vallayer's painting the details, especially the plums and the basket, emerge from deep shadows. The colors are vivid reds and violets and a sense of immediacy is invoked by the growing moss, the cakes in their crisp papers, and the glass of water with its sparkling highlights and reflections.
items.description_zh: 18世纪后期，水果静物画极为流行，一篮李子是常见主题。在瓦莱的画作中，细节（尤其是李子和篮子）从深色阴影中浮现。色彩是鲜艳的红色和紫色，而苔藓的生长、脆纸包裹的蛋糕以及带有闪烁高光和倒影的水杯，营造出一种即时感。
items.cardline_zh: 暗影托起一篮紫红，时光凝固在水晶杯沿
items.cardline: Deep shadows lift plums; time glistens on glass.

items.title: Wood Interior
items.description: Through luminescent color, filtered light, and a subtle evocation of atmosphere, Carlsen created a quiet, meditative mood in Wood Interior. The delicate trees, trickling brook, and soft bed of the forest floor invite a tranquil retreat from bustling city life. The painting’s lighter palette is complemented by its custom-made gilded frame, which is crafted in muted 16-karat gold rather than the standard, more vibrant 18-karat.
items.description_zh: 通过发光的色彩、过滤的光线和微妙的氛围营造，卡尔森在《森林内部》中创造了一种宁静冥想的情绪。纤细的树木、潺潺的小溪和柔软的林地地面，邀请人们从喧嚣的城市生活中获得宁静的休憩。画作较浅的色调与其定制的镀金画框相得益彰，该画框采用柔和的16K金而非标准更鲜艳的18K金制成。
items.cardline_zh: 林间滤光如低语，溪水轻抚青苔石
items.cardline: Filtered light whispers; brook caresses mossy stones.

items.title: Portrait of Machtelt Suijs
items.description: Maerten van Heemskerck lived in Rome for four years (1532–36) and was deeply affected by the city's art and antiquities. Here, the half-length, seated figure, the tense yet elegant hands, and even the grotesque classical mask reflect the impact of that experience, while the love of meticulously represented textures is traditionally associated with northern European art. Machtelt Suijs married Dirick van Teijlingen in 1535 and lived in Alkmaar (the Netherlands), where Heemskerck must have painted her. The coat-of-arms that hangs from the mask combines family emblems, indicating that her portrait must have been accompanied by one (now lost) of her husband.
items.title_zh: 马赫特尔德·苏伊斯肖像
items.description_zh: 马尔滕·范·海姆斯凯克在罗马生活了四年（1532–36年），深受该城艺术与古物的影响。画中半身坐姿、紧张而优雅的双手，甚至怪诞的古典面具，都反映了这种影响；而对精细描绘质感的钟爱则传统上与北欧艺术相关。马赫特尔德·苏伊斯于1535年与迪里克·范·泰林根结婚，居住在阿尔克马尔（荷兰），海姆斯凯克必定在那里为她绘制了肖像。悬挂在面具上的纹章结合了家族徽记，表明她的肖像曾有一幅（现已失传）其丈夫的肖像与之配对。
items.cardline_zh: 古典面具与织物质感，在指尖对峙
items.cardline: Ancient mask and velvet texture meet in her hands.

items.title: Villerville Seen from Le Ratier
items.description: The town of Villerville on the Normandy coast appears just to the right of center in this expansive landscape by Daubigny, a pioneer of outdoor painting and a major influence on Claude Monet and the Impressionists. Daubigny introduced a new kind of natural landscape based on outdoor studies of light, water, and atmospheric conditions. Here, streaks of bright light along the horizon set off the dark masses of the rocky shore in the foreground.
items.description_zh: 诺曼底海岸的维勒维尔镇出现在这幅广阔风景画的中偏右位置，作者杜比尼是户外绘画的先驱，对克劳德·莫奈和印象派影响深远。杜比尼引入了一种基于户外光线、水和大气条件研究的新型自然风景画。画中，地平线上的明亮光带衬托出前景岩石海岸的深色块。
items.cardline_zh: 地平线光痕破开云层，小镇静卧于呼吸之间
items.cardline: Light streaks horizon; village breathes beneath vast sky.

items.title: Rocky, Wooded Landscape with a Dell and Weir
items.description: In a sketchy manner, Gainsborough convincingly singled out the various elements comprising this sweeping landscape—meadow, rocks, trees—by only slightly varying colors and brushstrokes. At the same time, however, a great sense of unity prevails. Although the bulk of Gainsborough's artistic output was portraits, he insisted that landscape was his first love.
items.description_zh: 庚斯博罗以速写般的手法，仅通过略微变化的色彩和笔触，就令人信服地勾勒出这幅广阔风景中的各种元素——草地、岩石、树木。然而，同时，一种强烈的统一感贯穿始终。尽管庚斯博罗的大部分艺术产出是肖像画，但他坚称风景画是他的初恋。
items.cardline_zh: 岩石与树丛在笔触中呼吸，浑然天成
items.cardline: Rocks and trees breathe in one sweeping gesture.

items.title: Wrestlers in a Circus
items.description: Locked in a dangerous embrace, beefy wrestlers writhe on a brightly colored mat. Famous for his scenes of urban life, Kirchner uses loose brushstrokes to capture the blur of bodies in motion. Imagine watching this intense match, surrounded by screaming spectators.
items.description_zh: 粗壮的摔跤手紧紧相拥，在色彩鲜艳的垫子上扭动。基希纳以描绘都市生活场景而闻名，他用松散的笔触捕捉运动中身体的模糊感。想象一下，在尖叫的观众包围中观看这场激烈的比赛。
items.cardline_zh: 肌肉缠斗成模糊色块，呐喊在画布外震颤
items.cardline: Bodies blur in struggle; roar trembles beyond canvas.

items.title: A Cottage in the Woods
items.description: Inspired by the native Dutch landscape, Meindert Hobbema specialized in scenes of dense forest growth punctured by winding paths and views toward sunlit clearings that beckon with fertile fields and rustic cottages. Hobbema’s paintings were especially popular among Dutch citizens, who took enormous pride in their land. In addition to nationalistic pride in having recently gained independence from Spain, there was also a profound awareness of the preciousness of land in a low-lying nation, which required dikes, pumps, and constant vigilance to guard against flooding. While the Dutch earned much of their considerable wealth from sea trade, agriculture and livestock were important sources of prosperity.
items.description_zh: 受荷兰本土风景启发，梅因德特·霍贝玛专攻茂密森林场景，其间穿插蜿蜒小径和通往阳光明媚空地的视野，空地以肥沃田野和乡村小屋相迎。霍贝玛的画作在荷兰公民中尤其受欢迎，他们对自己的土地深感自豪。除了最近从西班牙独立带来的民族自豪感外，这个低地国家的人们还深刻意识到土地的珍贵，需要堤坝、水泵和持续警惕以防洪水。尽管荷兰人从海上贸易中获得了大量财富，但农业和畜牧业也是繁荣的重要来源。
items.cardline_zh: 幽径尽头，阳光推开林窗的刹那
items.cardline: Path ends in sunlight; forest opens to a cottage.

items.title: Portrait of Catherine Grey, Lady Manners
items.description: The Irish poet Lady Manners rejected as “unflattering” this portrait representing her as the goddess Juno, symbolized here by the peacock. Thomas Lawrence exhibited the painting at the Royal Academy in 1794 with the label “to be disposed of [sold],” but it was still in the artist’s collection when he died. Though it offended Lady Manners, the painting displays all the hallmarks of Lawrence’s flamboyant style:dazzling, fluid brushwork and an innovative use of unconventional colors that helped secure his role as the most fashionable portrait painter in turn-of-the-century Britain.
items.title_zh: 凯瑟琳·格雷，曼纳斯夫人肖像
items.description_zh: 爱尔兰诗人曼纳斯夫人认为这幅将她描绘为女神朱诺（以孔雀为象征）的肖像“不讨喜”，并予以拒绝。托马斯·劳伦斯于1794年在皇家艺术学院展出此画，标签注明“待售”，但画家去世时它仍在收藏中。尽管冒犯了曼纳斯夫人，这幅画展现了劳伦斯华丽风格的所有特征：炫目流畅的笔触和对非传统色彩的创新运用，这帮助他确立了世纪之交英国最时尚肖像画家的地位。
items.cardline_zh: 孔雀与女神，被一幅肖像拒绝的传说
items.cardline: Peacock and goddess: a portrait refused by its muse.

items.title: The Large Tree
items.description: Disgusted with the materialism of European society, Gauguin abandoned his family and career as a stockbroker and departed for Tahiti in 1891. This canvas is among the first paintings he completed on the island. Its rich colors and stylized figures were intended to be both symbolic and mysterious, evoking private thoughts and emotions. “I obtain symphonies,” he wrote, “harmonies that represent nothing real in the absolute sense of the word.”
items.description_zh: 高更对欧洲社会的物质主义感到厌恶，于1891年抛弃家庭和股票经纪人职业，前往塔希提岛。这幅画布是他在这座岛上完成的首批画作之一。其丰富的色彩和风格化的人物旨在兼具象征性和神秘性，唤起私密的思想和情感。“我获得交响乐，”他写道，“和声在绝对意义上不代表任何真实之物。”
items.cardline_zh: 巨树如交响乐，色彩是未言之语
items.cardline: Great tree becomes symphony; colors speak silent thoughts.

items.title: A Young Man with a Chain
items.description: This painting is an example of a tronie, a Dutch word meaning “head” or “facial expression.” Tronies were not intended to be identifiable portraits, but artistic investigations of facial appearance and fanciful costume. Particularly in the years around 1630, Rembrandt used this popular format to explore different lighting effects and facial expressions. Here, the strong contrast of light and shade shapes the contours of the young man’s face; unusual accessories such as the striped scarf and glittering pendant on a jeweled chain add an exotic touch. Although this painting bears Rembrandt’s monogram, the authorship is not certain. Some scholars have attributed it to Isaac de Joudreville (1613–1648), a pupil and associate of Rembrandt who painted comparable tronies in a similarly smooth and elegant style.
items.description_zh: 这幅画是“tronie”的一个例子，这是一个荷兰语词，意为“头部”或“面部表情”。Tronies并非旨在成为可识别的肖像，而是对容貌和奇装异服的艺术探索。尤其在1630年左右，伦勃朗利用这种流行形式来探索不同的光线效果和面部表情。画中，强烈的明暗对比塑造了年轻男子面部的轮廓；条纹围巾和珠宝链上闪烁的垂饰等不寻常的配饰增添了异国情调。尽管此画带有伦勃朗的姓名缩写，但作者身份并不确定。一些学者将其归于伦勃朗的学生兼助手伊萨克·德·茹德维尔（1613–1648），他以类似流畅优雅的风格绘制了可比的tronies。
items.cardline_zh: 光影在面容上雕琢，锁链垂于暗处轻响
items.cardline: Light carves the face; chain glints in shadow.

items.title: Terminus, the Device of Erasmus
items.artist: Hans Holbein the Younger (German, active England and Switzerland, 1497/98–1543)
items.description: In the spirit of the Italian Renaissance, Erasmus of Rotterdam (c. 1466–1536), the celebrated Dutch humanist and scholar, embraced ancient Greek and Roman literature and incorporated much of its moral and ethical messages into his own work. Holbein here conflates Erasmus’s features with Terminus, the Roman god of boundaries, who defied Jupiter by maintaining his position atop Capitoline Hill. Erasmus adopted Terminus, along with the motto concedo nulli (I concede to no one), as a personal symbol for devotion and steadfastness.
items.description_zh: 秉承意大利文艺复兴精神，鹿特丹的伊拉斯谟（约1466–1536年），这位著名的荷兰人文主义学者，拥抱古希腊罗马文学，并将其道德伦理信息融入自己的作品。霍尔拜因在此将伊拉斯谟的面容与终点神（罗马的边界之神）融合，后者曾违抗朱庇特而坚守卡比托利山山顶。伊拉斯谟采用终点神及其格言“我不向任何人让步”作为个人象征，代表奉献与坚定。
items.cardline_zh: 人文主义的边界，在神性与学识间矗立
items.cardline: Humanist god stands at boundary of wisdom and pride.

items.title: An October Day in the White Mountains
items.description: After railroad service to the area was improved in the early 1850s, the White Mountains in New Hampshire became a favored destination for tourists and artists seeking to commune with nature. New York-based Kensett painted several views, including this distant rendering across the Saco River of Mount Chocorua, one of the most distinctive peaks in the region.
items.description_zh: 19世纪50年代初，铁路交通改善后，新罕布什尔州的白山成为游客和艺术家亲近自然的热门目的地。纽约画家肯塞特创作了多幅白山风景，这幅远景描绘了萨科河对岸的乔科鲁阿山，是该地区最独特的山峰之一。
items.cardline_zh: 远山与秋水，静默如永恒的回响
items.cardline: Distant peak mirrors still water, eternity in silence.

items.title: Wineglass and a Bowl of Fruit
items.description:
Priceless objects crowd the corner of a stone table in Kalf's composition. Pieces like the silver platter, delicate glasswares, thick rumpled carpet, and a Chinese porcelain bowl with peaches and an orange not only represented the great wealth and global reach of the Dutch trading empire, but were also a way for the artist to demonstrate his skill in recreating various surface textures. Kalf used different kinds of highlights to render reflections—quick dots, lines, and dabs of paint—creating sparkling, twinkling light effects that subtly distinguish objects from the surrounding shadows.
In 1797, the German poet Johann van Goethe wrote that Kalf's paintings lead to understanding "in what sense art is superior to nature and what the spirit of man imparts to objects when it views them with creative eyes. . . . if I had to choose between the golden vessels or the picture . . . I would choose the picture."
items.description_zh:
卡尔夫的作品中，石桌一角堆满了珍贵物品。银盘、精致玻璃器皿、厚实皱褶地毯、盛有桃子和橙子的中国瓷碗，不仅代表了荷兰贸易帝国的巨大财富和全球影响力，也是艺术家展示其再现不同表面质感技巧的方式。卡尔夫运用不同高光——快速点、线、涂绘——来表现反射，创造出闪烁的光效，微妙地将物体与周围阴影区分开来。
1797年，德国诗人约翰·凡·歌德写道，卡尔夫的画作让人理解“艺术在何种意义上优于自然，以及人的精神以创造性的眼光看待事物时赋予其什么……如果必须在金器与画作之间选择……我会选择画作。”
items.cardline_zh: 华贵静物间，暗藏尘世的光影与纹理
items.cardline: Luxury and texture whisper of worldly transience.

items.title: Blessing of a Yacht on the Belon River
items.description: Maurice Denis made this painting during the summer of 1899 while vacationing with his family in the coastal village of Le Pouldu in Brittany in northwestern France. Two-thirds of this canvas is filled by the lavender-pink surface of the water, dotted with touches of color that suggest reflections of flags and sails on the Belon River. Local women wearing traditional headdresses observe the scene from the riverbank in the foreground, shaded by trees whose foliage frames the top of the composition.
items.description_zh: 莫里斯·德尼于1899年夏天在法国西北部布列塔尼的沿海村庄勒普尔杜度假时创作了这幅画。画布三分之二被薰衣草粉色的水面占据，点缀着色彩斑点，暗示贝隆河上旗帜和船帆的倒影。前景河岸上，戴着传统头饰的当地妇女在树荫下观看，树叶构成了构图的上框。
items.cardline_zh: 粉紫水面，帆影与祈祷的轻波
items.cardline: Lavender water holds sails and blessings in gentle waves.

items.title: Portrait of a Woman
items.description: Every detail of his subject’s elaborate costume is skillfully recorded: glittering jewels, filmy lace, knots of ribbons, heavy black silk, and shimmering white satin. The ability to recreate satin’s luster was Gerard ter Borch’s particular trademark: he used fine flicks and sweeps of the brush, occasionally adding and wiping away tiny bits of paint to make the white fabric appear to shine. Ter Borch offset the profusion of costume detail by deliberately isolating the figure within a spare setting, allowing us to appreciate the woman’s cool composure. Although her identity is not known, she was undoubtedly a member of the wealthy Dutch elite.
items.description_zh: 画中人物精致服饰的每个细节都被巧妙记录：闪耀的珠宝、薄纱蕾丝、缎带结、厚重的黑丝绸和闪光的白缎。再现缎面光泽是赫拉德·特·博尔赫的独特标志：他用细腻的笔触轻扫，偶尔添加并擦去少量颜料，使白色织物显得发光。特·博尔赫通过刻意将人物孤立在简洁背景中，平衡了服饰细节的繁复，让我们欣赏到女子冷静的仪态。虽然身份未知，但她无疑是荷兰富裕精英的一员。
items.cardline_zh: 丝绸光泽与繁饰，定格时光的奢华一瞥
items.cardline: Silk shimmers and jewels freeze time's lavish glance.

items.title: Painting of One Hundred Themes (obverse)
items.medium: Ten-panel folding screen affixed with album leaves (obverse), calligraphy (reverse), ink and color on silk
items.description: This screen depicts paintings on one side and poems on the other—an economical format often used in Korea to allow the viewer to enjoy both sides of one screen. The front features an assortment of bird-and-flower, landscape, and figural paintings executed according to the brush manner of more than 50 artists. A calligrapher has brushed several Chinese poems about the four seasons on the reverse side, among them "Composing in the Daytime of Summer" by Tang poet Liu Zongyuan (773–819) and "Composing when Spring Begins" by Song scholar Zhang Shi (1133–1180).
items.description_zh: 这扇屏风一面为画，另一面为诗——这是韩国常用的经济形式，让观者能欣赏屏风的两面。正面展示了超过50位艺术家笔法的花鸟、山水和人物画。背面由书法家题写了多首关于四季的中国诗，包括唐代诗人柳宗元（773–819）的《夏昼偶作》和宋代学者张栻（1133–1180）的《立春偶成》。
items.cardline_zh: 百景屏风间，四季诗画流转
items.cardline: Hundred scenes on silk, poems and paintings in seasonal flow.

items.title: Seaside (July: Specimen of a Portrait)
items.description:
This painting depicts a woman reclining upon an upholstered couch on a summer day while light filters through a partially closed awning that provides a glimpse of a beach in the background. James Tissot’s mature works from this time were
more likely to convey a mood—here of a languid summer afternoon—than a specific narrative. The model for this painting was Kathleen Newton, born Kathleen Ashburnham Kelly (1854–1882), the artist’s lover from 1876 until her untimely death from tuberculosis at the age of 28.
items.description_zh: 这幅画描绘了一位夏日午后斜倚在软垫沙发上的女子，光线透过半掩的遮阳篷，背景中露出一片海滩。詹姆斯·蒂索成熟期的作品更倾向于传达一种情绪——此处是慵懒的夏日午后——而非具体叙事。画中模特是凯瑟琳·牛顿，本名凯瑟琳·阿什伯纳姆·凯利（1854–1882），自1876年起成为画家的情人，直至她28岁因肺结核早逝。
items.cardline_zh: 夏日午后，慵懒光影里的海滩一隅
items.cardline: Summer light filters lazy afternoon by the sea.

items.title: Scenes of Witchcraft: Night
items.dimensions: Framed: 76.2 x 9.6 cm (30 x 3 3/4 in.); Unframed: 54.5 cm (21 7/16 in.)
items.description: In the pitch black of night, two groups of men are gathered in a forest. To the left, travelers apprehensively pause to watch a magician conjure terrifying apparitions. Since the Middle Ages, necromancy, the act of communing with the dead, was associated with male sorcerers. In Rosa's painting, the wizened necromancer who stands tall and resolute directly below a classical column is reminiscent of Moses, a predecessor to Renaissance depictions of sorcerers. Rosa's learned magicians not only invoke associations with philosophers and intellects, but they would have also referred to the artist himself and the intellectual elite with whom he associated in Florence. Just as a powerful magician could conjure strange creatures with his wand, so too could Rosa shape a strange world with his originality, intelligence, and the skillful use of paint and brush.
items.description_zh: 在漆黑的夜晚，两组男子聚集在森林中。左边，旅行者警惕地停下脚步，观看一位魔法师召唤恐怖的幻影。自中世纪以来，与死者沟通的巫术与男性巫师相关联。在罗萨的画中，那位高大坚定、站在古典柱式下的干瘪巫师让人联想到摩西，是文艺复兴时期巫师描绘的前身。罗萨的博学魔法师不仅唤起与哲学家和知识分子的联想，也指向艺术家本人及他在佛罗伦萨交往的知识精英。正如强大的魔法师能用魔杖召唤奇异生物，罗萨也能用他的独创性、智慧以及娴熟的画笔技巧塑造一个奇异世界。
items.cardline_zh: 暗夜森林，巫师唤起的恐惧与幻象
items.cardline: Dark forest, wizard conjures fear and phantom dread.

items.title: Portrait of a Woman
items.description: Born to a Sephardic Jewish family in Livorno, Italy, Amedeo Modigliani studied briefly in Florence and Venice before moving to Paris in 1906 where he became a key member of the avant-garde art world. His portraits, known for their subtle color and elegantly elongated forms, chronicle the lives of fellow artists and poets, although the woman in this painting remains unidentified.
items.description_zh: 阿梅代奥·莫迪利亚尼出生于意大利利沃诺的一个塞法迪犹太家庭，曾在佛罗伦萨和威尼斯短暂学习，1906年移居巴黎，成为前卫艺术界的关键人物。他的肖像画以柔和的色彩和优雅拉长的形体著称，记录了艺术家和诗人的生平，但画中女子身份未明。
items.cardline_zh: 修长的轮廓里，藏着神秘的灵魂
items.cardline: Elongated form holds a mysterious soul within.

items.title: Portrait of a Family Playing Music
items.description: The identity of the sitters remains unknown, although the marble floor and mantelpiece, the Transylvanian prayer rug on the table, and the silk clothes all point to the family’s considerable wealth. Furthermore, the richly carved chest at the right supports Chinese vases and Japanese lacquered boxes, a reference to the flourishing Dutch trade with Asia. Music making often took place at home, and its appearance here also signifies the harmoniousness of the family.
items.description_zh: 画中人物的身份未知，但大理石地板和壁炉架、桌上的特兰西瓦尼亚祈祷毯以及丝绸衣物都表明这个家庭相当富有。此外，右侧雕刻精美的柜子上摆放着中国花瓶和日本漆盒，暗示了荷兰与亚洲的繁荣贸易。音乐演奏常在家中举行，此处也象征着家庭的和谐。
items.cardline_zh: 华室乐音，流淌着家族的富足与和谐
items.cardline: Rich room, music flows in family harmony and wealth.

items.title: The Venetian Girl
items.description: Frank Duveneck loved Venice and spent several months there during the early decades of his career. Titled The Venetian Girl, this painting depicts a young woman—likely an artist’s model—who holds a shallow brass bowl containing lemons and grapes.
items.description_zh: 弗兰克·杜韦内克热爱威尼斯，在其职业生涯早期曾在那里度过数月。这幅题为《威尼斯女孩》的画作描绘了一位年轻女子——很可能是画家的模特——手持一个盛有柠檬和葡萄的浅铜碗。
items.cardline_zh: 铜盘柠檬，威尼斯少女的静谧时光
items.cardline: Brass bowl with fruit, a Venetian girl's quiet moment.

items.title: The Boatyard
items.description: Cazin exhibited this painting at the Paris Salon of 1876, marking the start of his official career. It depicts a boatyard in the artist's native Boulogne, a port city on the English Channel. The young man in the foreground is melting tar at a smoky fire, while men work on a boat just above him. Cazin was associated with the Realist movement during his early years and later with the Impressionists.
items.description_zh: 卡赞于1876年在巴黎沙龙展出了这幅画，标志着他正式职业生涯的开始。画作描绘了艺术家家乡布洛涅的一个船厂，布洛涅是英吉利海峡上的港口城市。前景中的年轻人在冒烟的火堆旁熔化沥青，而他上方的工人们正在修船。卡赞早期与现实主义运动相关，后来与印象派有关。
items.cardline_zh: 烟火与船影，劳工生活的真实一景
items.cardline: Smoke and boats, a real glimpse of working life.

items.title: The Apple Gatherers
items.description: Karl Anderson disliked the industrialized cities of his day and opted to paint rural settings seemingly untouched by modern development. The leisurely activity of apple picking contrasts with demanding work in urban factories, and the young girl dressed in all white may represent idealized innocence. The artist stated that his paintings were meant to capture “the fragrance of the beauty of life.”
items.description_zh: 卡尔·安德森厌恶当时的工业化城市，选择描绘看似未受现代发展影响的乡村环境。悠闲的摘苹果活动与城市工厂的繁重劳动形成对比，而身穿白衣的年轻女孩可能象征着理想化的纯真。艺术家表示，他的画作旨在捕捉“生活之美的芬芳”。
items.cardline_zh: 白衣少女摘苹果，田园的芬芳与纯真
items.cardline: White-clad girl picks apples, fragrance of rural innocence.

items.title: Susanna Anderson Rose
items.description: This keenly perceptive portrait of Susanna Anderson Rose (1795–1870) was painted when the sitter was 67 years old. The fine network of lines on her face are meticulously observed as are her silver gray curls and pale blue eyes. Frederick Sandys’s ability to render detail is evident in the subject’s bonnet, elaborately trimmed with lace and azure ribbon, and the texture of her brown fur cloak.
items.description_zh: 这幅对苏珊娜·安德森·罗斯（1795–1870）的敏锐肖像画于她67岁时完成。她脸上的细纹、银灰色卷发和淡蓝色眼睛都被细致观察。弗雷德里克·桑迪斯对细节的刻画能力体现在她的帽子上，帽子饰有蕾丝和天蓝色缎带，以及她棕色毛皮斗篷的质感。
items.cardline_zh: 皱纹与银发，岁月刻下的尊严与温度
items.cardline: Wrinkles and silver curls, dignity etched by time.

items.title: Scenes of Witchcraft: Evening
items.dimensions: Framed: 76.2 x 9.6 cm (30 x 3 3/4 in.); Unframed: 54.5 cm (21 7/16 in.)
items.description: At dusk, Rosa's hags gather around a cauldron. Above their incantations soars a conjured skeleton holding an hourglass symbolizing the brevity of life. In its left hand, the demonic creature holds a capital letter A from which extends a plumb line; as ancient signs of the moon and judgment, this strange combination of symbols embodies the nocturnal judgment the witches are doling out below. Though more rare in paint, the detailed portrayal of witches at their spells enjoyed a long literary tradition. The wax effigy alludes to classical literature by Virgil and Horace that described love magic. Poets in the 1500s and 1600s continued the tradition, writing verse about witches concocting potions and performing love spells. In Rosa's own poem "La Strega (The Witch)," the jilted Phyllis vows to take revenge on her feeble lover, listing the ingredients for her black magic: "ground powders, mystic gems, snakes and owls, stinking blood. . . ." Painting was considered "mute poetry" in the 1600s, and Rosa often explored the same themes in his written and painted art—a clever way to gain fame as a unique intellect.
items.description_zh: 黄昏时分，罗萨的巫婆们围着一口大锅。她们咒语上方，一个被召唤的骷髅手持沙漏飞翔，象征生命的短暂。恶魔般的生物左手握着一个大写字母A，延伸出一根铅垂线；作为月亮和审判的古老符号，这组奇怪的符号体现了巫婆们正在进行的夜间审判。虽然绘画中少见，但详细描绘巫婆施法的传统在文学中由来已久。蜡像暗指维吉尔和贺拉斯描述爱情魔法的古典文学。16和17世纪的诗人延续了这一传统，创作关于巫婆调制魔药和施展爱情咒语的诗歌。在罗萨自己的诗《女巫》中，被抛弃的菲利斯发誓要对懦弱的情人复仇，列出了她黑魔法的材料：“磨碎的粉末、神秘的宝石、蛇和猫头鹰、腥臭的血……”在17世纪，绘画被视为“无声的诗”，罗萨经常在他的文字和绘画艺术中探索相同主题——这是一种获得独特才智名声的巧妙方式。
items.cardline_zh: 暮色中，巫婆的咒语与骷髅的审判
items.cardline: Twilight hags chant, skeleton judges with hourglass and moon.

items.title: A Genoese Lady with Her Child
items.description: Prior to the 1600s, large, full-length portraits of wealthy clients usually emphasized the trappings of status above any other qualities. The accuracy of the likeness and vivacity of the person remained subordinate to an easily readable sense of the sitter’s power and position in society. Artists such as Van Dyck transformed this tradition, which sent shockwaves through Genoa, instantly changing the direction of elite portraiture in the city. While the elaborate garb of this unidentified sitter and the scale of the image certainly mark her status, the warmth Van Dyck imparted to her face, the drama of the background, and the touching detail of the child spontaneously reaching upward add life, energy, and theatricality to the work.
items.description_zh: 在17世纪之前，富有的客户的大型全身肖像通常强调地位的象征，而非其他品质。肖像的相似度和人物的生动性从属于易于识别的权力和社会地位。凡·戴克等艺术家改变了这一传统，在热那亚引起轰动，立即改变了该城精英肖像画的方向。虽然这位身份不明的模特华丽的服饰和画作的规模无疑标志了她的地位，但凡·戴克赋予她面部的温暖、背景的戏剧性以及孩子自发向上伸手的感人细节，为作品增添了生命、活力和戏剧性。
items.cardline_zh: 母子静立，高贵中透出人性的温度
items.cardline: Mother and child, nobility warmed by human grace.

items.title: Trees on a Rocky Hillside
items.description: Durand was one of the first American artists to paint outdoors, rather than in the confines of a studio, which was the longstanding tradition. This outdoor oil sketch of a seemingly random corner of the woods offers an intimate view of a rock outcropping amid lush vegetation. Its small-scale, unusually cropped composition contrasts with the sweeping vistas in many other landscapes of the era.
items.description_zh: 杜兰德是最早在户外而非传统画室中作画的美国艺术家之一。这幅看似随意的森林角落的户外油画速写，提供了岩石露头在茂密植被中的亲密视角。其小尺寸、不寻常的裁剪构图与当时许多其他风景画的广阔视野形成对比。
items.cardline_zh: 岩石与树木，自然角落的亲密私语
items.cardline: Rocks and trees whisper intimate secrets of wild nature.

items.title: Young Woman with a Turban
items.description: Known as a tête d'expression or "expressive head," this painting represents an essential part of the academic training of history painters who specialized in religious, mythological, or historical subjects. In 1760, such exercises became standard at the École des Beaux-Arts (School of Fine Arts) in Paris, then known as Académie royale de peinture et de sculpture. Focusing on the relationship between morality and beauty, the goal of a history painter is not only to create a pleasing image, but also to represent the emotions of the soul.
items.description_zh: 这幅画被称为“表情头像”，是专攻宗教、神话或历史题材的历史画家学术训练的重要组成部分。1760年，这类练习成为巴黎美术学院（当时称为皇家绘画与雕塑学院）的标准课程。历史画家的目标不仅是创造悦目的图像，还要表现灵魂的情感，关注道德与美的关系。
items.cardline_zh: 头巾与眼神，古典表情的细腻演绎
items.cardline: Turbaned gaze, a classic expression finely rendered.

items.title: White Pansy
items.description: Among O'Keeffe's most admired works are her close-up images of flowers whose compositions border on abstraction. In this example, a large image of a white pansy is juxtaposed against much smaller blossoms of light blue forget-me-nots.
items.description_zh: 欧姬芙最受赞赏的作品包括她那些构图近乎抽象的花朵特写。在这幅作品中，一朵巨大的白三色堇与更小的淡蓝色勿忘我花朵并置。
items.cardline_zh: 白花与蓝影，微观世界的宏大诗意
items.cardline: White pansy and blue blooms, grand poetry in small scale.

items.title: Memento Mori, "To This Favour"
items.description: The Latin term memento mori describes a traditional subject in art that addresses mortality. In Harnett’s example, the extinguished candle, spent hourglass, and skull symbolize death. A quote from William Shakespeare’s Hamlet, inscribed on the inside cover of a tattered book, reinforces the theme. It comes from the play’s famed graveyard scene where Hamlet discovers a skull and grimly ponders his beloved Ophelia, ironically unaware that she is already dead. The "paint" in the quote not only refers to Ophelia’s makeup, but also wittily evokes the artifice of Harnett’s picture.
items.description_zh: 拉丁术语“memento mori”描述了一种探讨死亡的传统艺术主题。在哈尼特的例子中，熄灭的蜡烛、用尽的沙漏和骷髅象征死亡。一本破旧书的内封上刻有威廉·莎士比亚《哈姆雷特》中的一句引文，强化了主题。它出自剧中著名的墓地场景，哈姆雷特发现一个骷髅，阴郁地思考他心爱的奥菲莉娅，讽刺的是他并不知道她已经死了。引文中的“颜料”不仅指奥菲莉娅的化妆品，也巧妙地暗示了哈尼特画作的人为性。
items.cardline_zh: 骷髅与沙漏，生命的短暂与沉思
items.cardline: Skull and hourglass, life's brevity in silent thought.

items.title: Study, North Conway, New Hampshire
items.description: Johnson’s devotion to carefully observing nature is evident in his detailed rendering of a riverbed. Located in a village that provided a popular jumping-off point for tourists visiting the White Mountains, this untamed site features a haphazard arrangement of angled boulders, slippery moss, splintered tree branches, and dense undergrowth. Contemporary travel guidebooks often cautioned against the physical challenges—and the potential for injury—encountered by venturing into such inner recesses of the woods; one warned that “wild forest-clambering” could be akin to “fighting a phalanx of porcupines.”
items.title_zh: 习作，新罕布什尔州北康威
items.description_zh: 约翰逊对自然细致观察的投入体现在他对河床的精细描绘中。这个位于村庄的原始地点，是游客前往白山的常见出发点，呈现出倾斜的巨石、滑溜的苔藓、断裂的树枝和茂密灌木丛的杂乱排列。当时的旅行指南常警告进入森林深处可能面临的体力挑战和受伤风险；一本指南警告说，“狂野的攀爬”可能如同“与豪猪方阵搏斗”。
items.cardline_zh: 乱石青苔，荒野河床的细致笔触
items.cardline: Mossy rocks and tangled branches, wild riverbed in detail.

items.title: The Apple Seller
items.description: This painting depicts a young country girl offering apples to Renoir's wife, Aline. The boy in the straw hat may be the artist's nephew, Edmond, but the young girl with the ribbon in her hair has not been identified. Bathed in soft, dappled sunlight, the figures are united through the fluid brushstrokes that cover the canvas. The leaping dog provides an accent of humor and motion in an otherwise tranquil scene. The picture was probably completed at Essoyes, in eastern France.
items.description_zh: 这幅画描绘了一位年轻的乡村女孩向雷诺阿的妻子阿琳递上苹果。戴草帽的男孩可能是艺术家的侄子埃德蒙，但头发上系着丝带的年轻女孩身份尚未确定。画面沐浴在柔和斑驳的阳光中，人物通过覆盖画布的流畅笔触融为一体。跳跃的小狗为原本宁静的场景增添了幽默与动感。这幅画很可能完成于法国东部的埃索耶。
items.cardline_zh: 光影斑驳间，苹果传递着乡野的温柔。
items.cardline: Dappled light weaves a rustic gift of apples.

items.title: The Judgment of Paris
items.description: The Trojan prince Paris was asked to determine which goddess was the most beautiful. Hera (tothe left of the group) offered him political power, and Athena (seen from the back), military prowess, but Paris awarded the golden apple to Venus, the goddess of love, who promised him Helen, the most beautiful mortal in the world.
items.description_zh: 特洛伊王子帕里斯被要求判定哪位女神最美。赫拉（位于群体左侧）许诺他政治权力，雅典娜（背对观众）许诺他军事才能，但帕里斯将金苹果判给了爱神维纳斯，因为她许诺给他世上最美丽的凡人女子海伦。
items.cardline_zh: 金苹果的诱惑，神祇的抉择凝于铜面。
items.cardline: A golden apple decides divine beauty's fate.

items.title: The Doge's Palace, Venice
items.description: At the age of 22, Bonington achieved overnight success at the official (state sponsored) Paris exhibition known as Salon. Two years later, in 1826, he journeyed to Venice, a city that had long attracted landscape artists. At first, constant rain greatly depressed Bonington, but suddenly the weather changed and Venice was at its glorious best. Bonington frequently painted outdoors, capturing immediate impressions of Venetian architecture and sunlight. He made this particular study from a boat anchored in the lagoon near the Doge's Palace. The thick swirls of paint, with colors mixed together while still wet, indicates how rapidly the artist worked.
items.description_zh: 22岁时，博宁顿在官方举办的巴黎沙龙展上一举成名。两年后的1826年，他前往威尼斯——一个长期吸引风景画家的城市。起初，连绵大雨让博宁顿十分沮丧，但天气突然转好，威尼斯展现出最辉煌的面貌。博宁顿经常在户外作画，捕捉威尼斯建筑与阳光的即时印象。这幅作品是他从停泊在总督府附近泻湖的小船上完成的。厚涂的颜料与湿画法中混合的色彩，显示出艺术家作画速度之快。
items.cardline_zh: 雨后威尼斯，总督府在光中苏醒。
items.cardline: Venice glows after rain, palace anew.

items.title: View of the Gulf of Pozzuoli from Solfatara
items.description: Attracted to its dramatic vistas, volcanoes, exotic peasants, and classical ruins, landscape painters of the 1700s and 1800s flocked to the countryside around Naples. This view looks west toward the Gulf of Pozzuoli from just above the Solfatara, an area of volcanic steam vents. The ancient town of Pozzuoli, where the apostle Paul landed on his way to Rome, lies in the distance. Hackert's attention to detail and rendering of form with extreme lucidity is characteristic of German Romantic painting.
items.title_zh: 从索尔法塔拉看波佐利湾
items.description_zh: 18、19世纪的风景画家被那不勒斯周边壮丽的景色、火山、异域农民和古典遗迹所吸引，纷纷涌向此地。这幅画从索尔法塔拉（一片火山蒸汽喷口区域）上方朝西望向波佐利湾。远处是古城波佐利，使徒保罗在前往罗马途中曾在此登陆。哈克特对细节的关注以及以极度清晰的方式表现形体的手法，是德国浪漫主义绘画的典型特征。
items.cardline_zh: 火山蒸汽弥漫，古湾静卧远方。
items.cardline: Steaming earth meets ancient gulf's quiet gaze.

items.title: Pine Tree
items.description: Segantini painted this view of a pine tree in the Swiss Alps with his distinctive combination of brilliant colors applied in small, broken strokes. Rather than a direct transcription of nature, the extreme close-up view and the flat, decorative space are designed to arouse an emotional response. Finding spiritual consolation in the lush forests and clear light around his home in the Alps, Segantini wrote: “I am now working passionately to wrest the secret of Nature’s spirit from her. Nature utters the eternal word to the artist: love, love; and the earth sings life in spring, and the soul of things reawakens.”
items.description_zh: 塞冈蒂尼以他独特的风格——用细碎笔触涂抹鲜艳色彩——描绘了瑞士阿尔卑斯山中的一棵松树。极端的特写视角和平面的装饰性空间并非对自然的直接转录，而是旨在唤起情感反应。塞冈蒂尼在阿尔卑斯山家附近茂密的森林和清澈的光线中找到了精神慰藉，他写道：“我正充满激情地工作，试图从自然中夺取她精神的秘密。自然对艺术家说出永恒的话语：爱，爱；大地在春天歌唱生命，万物的灵魂重新苏醒。”
items.cardline_zh: 松枝贴近，色彩低语自然的慰藉。
items.cardline: Close pine whispers solace in broken hues.

items.title: Low Tide at Pourville, near Dieppe, 1882
items.description: This is one of several views Claude Monet painted of Pourville, a fishing village on the north coast of France in Normandy. Pourville featured a boardwalk, a broad beach, and stunning cliffs. According to the artist's correspondence, the village was nothing special, but he was inspired by the landscape: "One could not be any closer to the sea than I am, on the pebbled beach itself and the waves beat at the foot of the house." During the spring of 1882 in Pourville, Monet stayed in a small hotel similar to the dwellings seen at the foot of the cliff to the right in this painting.
items.title_zh: 迪耶普附近普尔维尔的低潮，1882年
items.description_zh: 这是克劳德·莫奈描绘普尔维尔（法国北部诺曼底的一个渔村）的几幅作品之一。普尔维尔有海滨步道、宽阔的沙滩和壮观的悬崖。根据艺术家的信件，这个村庄本身并无特别之处，但他受到风景的启发：“没有人能比我更靠近大海了，就在卵石海滩上，海浪拍打着房屋的脚下。”1882年春天在普尔维尔，莫奈住在一家小旅馆里，类似这幅画中右侧悬崖脚下看到的房屋。
items.cardline_zh: 退潮时分，海浪轻抚卵石滩。
items.cardline: Low tide breathes on pebbled shore's hush.

items.title: Hunting near Hartenfels Castle
items.medium: oil, originally on wood, transferred to masonite
items.description: The Protestant rulers of Saxony commissioned this animated hunt scene, set near their residence seen in the background, Hartenfels Castle (in eastern Germany). John Frederick the Magnanimous, in the bottom left corner, wears dark green hunting attire; he spans his crossbow and waits for his courtiers and dogs to chase a stag across the river. His wife, the Electress Sibylle, stands at right, poised to take the first ceremonial shot. The prince electors of Saxony were passionate practitioners of hunting with dogs—elaborate, highly rehearsed occasions, coordinated by the use of signals from hunting horns. Cranach dated this work and his signature is the winged snake at lower right. 1577 in the right corner is an inventory number.
items.title_zh: 哈滕费尔斯城堡附近的狩猎
items.description_zh: 萨克森的新教统治者委托创作了这幅生动的狩猎场景，背景是他们居住的哈滕费尔斯城堡（位于德国东部）。左下角的宽宏者约翰·弗里德里希穿着深绿色猎装，他张开十字弓，等待朝臣和猎犬将一头雄鹿赶过河。他的妻子、选帝侯夫人西比勒站在右侧，准备射出第一枪。萨克森的选帝侯们是猎犬狩猎的热衷实践者——这种狩猎活动精心策划、高度排练，通过猎号信号协调。克拉纳赫在作品上标注了日期，他的签名是右下角的有翼蛇。右上角的1577是库存编号。
items.cardline_zh: 猎犬跃溪，城堡静观贵族的追逐。
items.cardline: Hounds leap stream as castle watches hunt.

items.title: Haverstraw Bay
items.description: Located about 40 miles north of New York City, Haverstraw Bay is the widest point on the Hudson River. During the 1860s, it was a prime area for shad fishing, and Gifford’s painting records this activity taking place amid a delicately luminous morning haze.
items.description_zh: 哈弗斯特罗湾位于纽约市以北约40英里，是哈德逊河最宽处。19世纪60年代，这里是西鲱鱼捕捞的主要区域，吉福德的画作记录了在柔和明亮的晨雾中进行的这一活动。
items.cardline_zh: 晨雾如纱，渔舟静泊哈德逊河湾。
items.cardline: Morning haze veils bay's shad boats still.

items.title: Portrait of Mrs. Leneve
items.description: This woman’s heavily lidded eyes, fleshy chin, and slender fingers conform to 17th-century ideals of beauty, but when the museum acquired this painting in 1942, her features had been extensively overpainted to bring her more in line with modern standards of beauty. This later intervention included lowering the sitter’s eyebrows, reducing her prominent eyes and lips, and adding curls to make her forehead appear smaller. Mrs. Leneve now appears as the fashionable artist Peter Lely originally intended.
items.description_zh: 这位女士沉重的眼睑、丰满的下巴和纤细的手指符合17世纪的审美标准，但博物馆在1942年购入此画时，她的面容已被大量重绘以符合现代审美标准。后来的修改包括降低眉毛、缩小突出的眼睛和嘴唇，并添加卷发使额头显得更小。莱内夫夫人现在呈现出时尚画家彼得·莱利最初设想的样子。
items.cardline_zh: 眼睑低垂，暗藏时光的改笔。
items.cardline: Lidded eyes hold centuries of repainted grace.

items.title: On a Terrace
items.description: Trained as a printmaker and book illustrator, Meissonier specialized in small paintings depicting scenes from French history and literature. This painting on wood panel depicts a man dressed like a character from The Three Musketeers (published in 1844) by Alexandre Dumas père. Such paintings, admired for their technical virtuosity and romantic subjects, were immensely popular. Meissonier also painted contemporary military subjects and themes and became an arch-foe of the Realist painter Gustave Courbet.
items.description_zh: 梅索尼埃受过版画家和书籍插画家的训练，专攻描绘法国历史和文学场景的小型绘画。这幅木板油画描绘了一个打扮成大仲马《三个火枪手》（1844年出版）中角色的人物。这类画作因其技术精湛和浪漫题材而备受推崇，极为流行。梅索尼埃还创作当代军事题材作品，并成为现实主义画家古斯塔夫·库尔贝的死对头。
items.cardline_zh: 阳台一隅，火枪手气息犹存。
items.cardline: A musketeer's terrace breathes romantic past.

items.title: Card Rack with a Jack of Hearts
items.description: Like modern-day bulletin boards, card racks were used to gather newspaper clippings, scraps of paper, and other ephemera in one place. Although it is unclear if the items seen here carry symbolic meaning, they nevertheless demonstrate the human desire to collect and recollect. Their tattered state and frayed edges further evoke a sense of memory and nostalgia. The illusionistic quality of Peto’s pictures makes the objects appear to exist in real space. Even the cigarette butt perched at the bottom looks ready to be plucked from the painting’s frame.
items.description_zh: 像现代的公告板一样，卡片架用于将剪报、纸片和其他 ephemera 集中在一处。尽管这些物品是否具有象征意义尚不明确，但它们展示了人类收集和回忆的欲望。其破旧状态和磨损边缘进一步唤起记忆与怀旧之情。佩托画作的错觉主义效果使物体仿佛存在于真实空间中，甚至底部边缘的烟蒂也像是要从画框中摘取下来。
items.cardline_zh: 纸牌泛黄，记忆在皱褶里安放。
items.cardline: Tattered cards hold memory's quiet fold.

items.title: Harbor of Boston, with the City in the Distance
items.dimensions: Framed: 66 x 104.8 x 14 cm (26 x 41 1/4 x 5 1/2 in.); Unframed: 43.2 x 68.6 cm (17 x 27 in.)
items.description: In this late afternoon view of Boston Harbor, Lane masterfully evoked the diffuse quality of sunlight reflecting off water. The artist was a son of a shipbuilder, so perhaps it is not surprising that his renderings of ships and their intricate riggings are painstakingly accurate. On the far left side of the painting, evolving technology appears in the guise of a steamboat, which ferried passengers between Boston and Gloucester.
items.description_zh: 在这幅波士顿港的傍晚景色中，莱恩巧妙地表现了阳光在水面上反射的漫射质感。艺术家是造船匠之子，因此他对船只及其复杂索具的描绘极其精确也就不足为奇了。画面最左侧，一艘蒸汽船代表了技术的演进，它往返于波士顿和格洛斯特之间运送乘客。
items.cardline_zh: 余晖洒落，帆影与汽笛共渡港湾。
items.cardline: Sunset gleams on sail and steam alike.

items.title: The Triumph of Neptune and Amphitrite
items.medium: oil on copper, mounted on wood
items.description: Neptune—the Roman god of water—and his wife, Amphitrite, ride a chariot pulled by sea horses. Water fowl and flying fish glide in the sky above them, while in the distance a group of nymphs beckon from a grotto. Neptune’s trident represents his power over water and storms; the fish, shells, coral, and pearls carried by his subjects show his wealth.
items.title_zh: 尼普顿与安菲特里忒的凯旋
items.description_zh: 尼普顿——罗马水神——和他的妻子安菲特里忒乘坐由海马拉动的战车。水鸟和飞鱼在他们上方的天空中滑翔，远处一群宁芙从洞穴中招手。尼普顿的三叉戟代表他对水和风暴的掌控；他的臣民携带的鱼、贝壳、珊瑚和珍珠则显示了他的财富。
items.cardline_zh: 海马驭车，珠贝随行神祇的凯旋。
items.cardline: Sea horses draw triumph through pearl and shell.

items.title: Travelers in Hilly Countryside
items.description: While many Dutch painters focused on depicting the local countryside, others were inspired by the hilly topography, golden sunlight, and picturesque monuments of Italy. Although Aelbert Cuyp never visited Italy, this serene and beautifully preserved panel demonstrates his skill in evoking the idyllic qualities of a distant land.
items.description_zh: 许多荷兰画家专注于描绘本地乡村，而另一些则受到意大利起伏的地形、金色阳光和如画古迹的启发。尽管阿尔伯特·克伊普从未去过意大利，但这幅宁静且保存完好的画板展示了他唤起遥远国度田园诗般特质的技巧。
items.cardline_zh: 丘陵之上，旅人浸染异国的金光。
items.cardline: Golden hills echo Italy's imagined light.

items.title: Tannhäuser
items.description: Tannhäuser, the male figure seated on the left, does not seem fully engaged in this merry scene. Among pale bodies and pastel hues, he alone is cast in shadow. The Wagner opera that inspired the painting centers on a struggle between profane and sacred love, and the tension between them appears embodied in the brooding Tannhäuser.
items.description_zh: 唐豪瑟，左侧坐着的男性形象，似乎并未完全融入这欢乐的场景。在苍白的身体和柔和的色调中，唯有他处于阴影之中。启发这幅画的瓦格纳歌剧聚焦于世俗之爱与神圣之爱的斗争，两者间的张力似乎体现在忧郁的唐豪瑟身上。
items.cardline_zh: 阴影中的沉思者，游离于欢宴之外。
items.cardline: Shadowed guest broods amid pale revelry.

items.title: Countess Széchenyi
items.description: This portrait typifies painting in Vienna between 1815 and 1865, an era known as the Biedermeier period, during which the Habsburg government promoted positive artistic depictions of Viennese life and culture. The mountains in the background express both the artist's romantic fascination with nature and patriotic devotion to his Austrian homeland. The sitter, Crescentia Seilern, was a prestigious member of the aristocracy who married Hungarian reformist patriot István Széchenyi.
items.description_zh: 这幅肖像是1815年至1865年间维也纳绘画的典型代表，这一时期被称为比德迈时期，哈布斯堡政府鼓励对维也纳生活与文化进行积极的艺术描绘。背景中的山脉既表达了艺术家对自然的浪漫迷恋，也体现了他对奥地利祖国的爱国热忱。画中人物克雷森蒂娅·塞勒恩是贵族中的显赫成员，嫁给了匈牙利改革派爱国者伊什特万·塞切尼。
items.cardline_zh: 青山为伴，伯爵夫人凝望故乡。
items.cardline: Mountains frame her Biedermeier grace.

items.title: Scenes of Witchcraft: Morning
items.dimensions: Framed: 76.2 x 9.6 cm (30 x 3 3/4 in.); Unframed: 54.5 cm (21 7/16 in.)
items.description: Rosa's first scene depicts a young witch who plunges her knife into a writhing amphibian at dawn. The dark clouds of daybreak and anthropomorphic crags provide a gloomy atmosphere, while malevolent birds with piercing beaks hover around the central stabbing, focusing the viewer's attention on the witch's vicious act. The only beautiful enchantress Rosa ever painted, her elegance and ability to transform men into animals evokes the goddess Circe. But Rosa wasn't interested in classical imagery; he inverted expectations by transforming Circe into an explicitly violent sorceress. Her calm expression makes the terrifying gesture of upraised human hands among the birds even more disturbing.
items.description_zh: 罗莎的第一幅场景描绘了黎明时分，一位年轻女巫将刀刺入扭动的两栖动物。破晓的乌云和拟人化的峭壁营造出阴郁的氛围，而尖喙的恶鸟盘旋在中心刺杀场景周围，将观者的注意力集中在女巫的残忍行为上。这是罗莎笔下唯一美丽的妖女，她的优雅和将男人变成动物的能力让人联想到女神喀耳刻。但罗莎对古典意象不感兴趣；他颠覆了预期，将喀耳刻转变为一位明显的暴力女巫。她平静的表情使得鸟儿中举起的人类双手这一恐怖姿态更加令人不安。
items.cardline_zh: 黎明前，利刃刺入蠕动的黑暗。
items.cardline: Dawn's knife pierces writhing dark.

items.title: Sweet Melancholy
items.description: The chair, brazier, table, and the setting all show Vien's interest in bringing archaeological accuracy to a new level of precision. Yet his delicate handling and graceful palette derive squarely from 18th-century French painting. The gesture of the melancholic figure, with her head on her hand, has roots going back to the Renaissance. Nonetheless, the painting has a wistful rather than tragic tone. Indeed, images of women in interiors, contemplating a letter with longing or sadness, derive from earlier Dutch paintings of daily life, here transformed into an ancient context.
items.description_zh: 椅子、火盆、桌子以及整个场景都显示出维恩对将考古学精确性提升到新水平的兴趣。然而，他细腻的处理和优雅的色调完全源自18世纪法国绘画。忧郁人物以手托头的姿势可追溯到文艺复兴时期。尽管如此，这幅画传达的是惆怅而非悲剧的基调。事实上，女性在室内凝视信件、带着渴望或悲伤沉思的形象，源自早期荷兰风俗画，在此被转化为古代背景。
items.cardline_zh: 手托香腮，古物间泛起温婉哀愁。
items.cardline: Head in hand, antique sorrow softens.

items.title: The Contemplator
items.description: This painting illustrating Victor Hugo's poem Boaz Sleeping is one of five images Carrière created to commemorate the centennial of Hugo's birth. A reclining poet gazes at the stars above, as if in a dream, placing one hand over his heart and gesturing toward a crescent moon. The moon was thought to exert a special influence on poets and artists, inspiring not only creativity but darker thoughts of melancholy and suicide.
items.description_zh: 这幅画描绘了维克多·雨果的诗《波阿斯入睡》，是卡里埃为纪念雨果诞辰一百周年创作的五幅图像之一。一位斜倚的诗人凝视着上方的星星，仿佛在梦中，一只手放在心口，另一只手指向新月。月亮被认为对诗人和艺术家有特殊影响，不仅能激发创造力，也能引发忧郁和自杀的阴暗思绪。
items.cardline_zh: 诗人望月，手抚心口暗涌灵感。
items.cardline: Poet gazes moon, hand on heart's dream.

items.title: Mount Vesuvius at Midnight
items.description: For centuries, artists and tourists have been attracted to Mount Vesuvius, a volcano near Naples. While living in London in 1868, Albert Bierstadt heard that the volcano was active once again, and rushed immediately to Italy. It is not certain that he actually saw an eruption, although the resultant painting presents a convincing image of spewing lava and ash.
items.description_zh: 几个世纪以来，艺术家和游客一直被那不勒斯附近的维苏威火山所吸引。1868年住在伦敦时，阿尔伯特·比尔施塔特听说火山再次活跃，便立即赶往意大利。他是否真的看到了喷发并不确定，但这幅画呈现了喷涌的熔岩和火山灰的令人信服的图像。
items.cardline_zh: 午夜火山喷薄，熔岩点亮夜空。
items.cardline: Midnight volcano spills fire into dark.

items.title: Self-Portrait in Painting Studio
items.dimensions: Platemark: 20.5 x 15 cm (8 1/16 x 5 7/8 in.); Matted: 55.9 x 45.7 cm (22 x 18 in.)
items.description: Dolard, a portrait painter, may have offered photographs to prospective clients of lesser means. In this image, possibly made as an advertisement for his studio, he identifies himself as a painter, surrounded by the tools of the trade. The coat and the hookah suggest an interest in orientalism, a fashion that occupied many mid-19th-century artists. A remarkable technical achievement, this whole-plate image required Dolard to remain motionless for well over a minute, at least 30 times longer than the exposure for the smaller plates in the case below.
items.description_zh: 肖像画家多拉德可能曾向经济条件有限的潜在客户提供照片。在这幅可能是为其画室制作的广告中，他自称画家，周围环绕着绘画工具。外套和水烟袋暗示了对东方主义的兴趣，这是19世纪中期许多艺术家热衷的时尚。这幅全版照片是一项非凡的技术成就，要求多拉德保持静止超过一分钟，至少是下方小版照片曝光时间的30倍。
items.cardline_zh: 光影定格的画家身份，东方烟斗与画架间漂浮
items.cardline: Light fixes the painter's identity, floating between easel and hookah.

items.title: The Beach at Deauville
items.description: During the 1860s, Boudin executed many paintings and watercolors representing well-to-do tourists and vacationers enjoying seaside resorts in Normandy, principally Trouville and Deauville. In this scene, the informally posed figures suggest a sense of relaxation and intimacy. The overturned chair in the foreground underscores the impression of a casually observed moment, as though a sea breeze or a quick departure by its former occupant has upended it. The majority of Boudin's small oil paintings of beach scenes of the 1860s were executed on wood panel. After laying down a thin white ground, Boudin seems to have begun painting directly, not drawing or laying in guidelines for the forms. The result is a freshness and airiness appropriate to a windy day at the beach.
items.description_zh: 在1860年代，布丹创作了许多描绘富裕游客和度假者在诺曼底海滨度假胜地（主要是特鲁维尔和多维尔）享受时光的油画和水彩画。在这幅场景中，随意摆姿势的人物传达出放松和亲密感。前景中翻倒的椅子强调了随意观察到的瞬间的印象，仿佛海风或前一位坐者的匆忙离开将其掀翻。布丹在1860年代创作的大部分海滩小油画都是在木板上完成的。在涂上一层薄薄的白色底漆后，布丹似乎直接开始绘画，没有绘制草图或为形体设置引导线。结果呈现出一种适合海边风天的清新和轻盈感。
items.cardline_zh: 海风掀翻空椅，闲适恰好坠落沙滩
items.cardline: Sea breeze tips an empty chair, leisure spills onto sand.

items.title: Catherine Greene
items.description: The Boston painter Copley's virtuosity at rendering luxurious textiles was precisely the kind of talent that appealed to his patrons. In 1769 the prosperous merchant John Greene commissioned a portrait of himself and this portrait of his wife, Catherine (1735–1785), after thirteen years of marriage. Shown at the age of 34, Mrs. Greene wears a fanciful costume inspired by ancient styles, strikes a pose derived from an English engraving, and pretends to lean against a rock that looks as insubstantial as a theatrical prop. This likeness places Mrs. Greene in an ideal realm far removed from mundane matters. The frame is original.
items.description_zh: 波士顿画家科普利在描绘奢华纺织品方面的精湛技艺正是吸引其赞助人的那种才华。1769年，富商约翰·格林在结婚13年后委托绘制了一幅自己的肖像和这幅他妻子凯瑟琳（1735–1785）的肖像。格林夫人时年34岁，身穿受古代风格启发的幻想服装，摆出源自英国版画的姿势，假装靠在一块看起来像戏剧道具一样不真实的岩石上。这幅肖像将格林夫人置于一个远离世俗事务的理想领域。画框是原装的。
items.cardline_zh: 丝绸裹住时光，她凝望成古典的谜
items.cardline: Silk holds time; she gazes into a classical mystery.

items.title: The Sacrifice of Isaac, Tobias and the Angel, The Angels Appearing to Abraham, Abraham Welcoming the Three Angels (painting series)
items.description: This series of paintings predominantly tells the Old Testament story of Abraham and Isaac, and each of the canvases treats the interaction of humans and angels. In three works, angels appear to Abraham and, in one particularly dramatic canvas, an angel intercepts Abraham before he sacrifices his own son. Like another work in this series, Tobias and the Angels, the Sacrifice of Isaac is a narrative about fathers and sons as well as obedience inspired by faith.
items.title_zh: 以撒的献祭、托比亚斯与天使、天使向亚伯拉罕显现、亚伯拉罕迎接三位天使（组画）
items.description_zh: 这组画主要讲述《旧约》中亚伯拉罕和以撒的故事，每幅画都描绘了人与天使的互动。在三幅作品中，天使向亚伯拉罕显现，在一幅尤为戏剧性的画中，天使在亚伯拉罕献祭自己儿子之前阻止了他。与本系列的另一幅作品《托比亚斯与天使》一样，《以撒的献祭》是一个关于父与子以及因信仰而顺从的故事。
items.cardline_zh: 天使的羽翼拂过祭坛，刀锋悬停于信仰边缘
items.cardline: Angels' wings brush the altar, the blade hovers at faith's edge.

items.title: Mountain Market, Clear with Rising Mist
items.dimensions: Painting: 115.3 x 60 cm (45 3/8 x 23 5/8 in.); Overall: 179 x 70.7 cm (70 1/2 x 27 13/16 in.); Overall with knobs and cord: 181.8 x 77 cm (71 9/16 x 30 5/16 in.)
items.description: This painting depicts several vignettes of the scholar-hermit’s various activities. In the lower left corner, the scholar-hermit is portrayed welcoming his guest in the courtyard. In the middle section on the lower bottom, the scholar and his guest are shown encountering another traveler on the bridge and exchanging their greetings. After crossing the bridge, the scholar visits a friend who lives in a compound of multiple buildings. The two friends decide to take a short walk. Both appear in a terraced cliff, enjoying a mountain vista and the fragrance of an old pine tree carried by the gentle wind. The scholar continues his journey with his two servants. They are portrayed crossing a winding wooden bridge. Right beyond the colossal rocky mountain, a panoramic view to a bustling market place unfolds, suggesting that it may be the place where the scholar-hermit’s entourage would visit. Perhaps the scholar need to buy items such as books, rolls of papers, and brushes, all essential to scholarly life. Toward the upper left corner, another group of travelers is landing on the river bank that leads to the city gate; they may be headed to the multistoried Buddhist stupa nestled on the top of the mountains.
items.description_zh: 这幅画描绘了隐士学者的多种活动场景。左下角，隐士学者在庭院中迎接客人。底部中间，学者和客人在桥上遇到另一位旅人并互相问候。过桥后，学者拜访了一位住在多栋建筑院落中的朋友。两人决定散步片刻，出现在悬崖平台上，欣赏山景和微风中老松树的芬芳。学者带着两名仆人继续旅程，他们穿过一座蜿蜒的木桥。在巨大的岩石山之后，一个熙熙攘攘的集市全景展开，暗示这可能是隐士随从将要参观的地方。也许学者需要购买书籍、卷纸和毛笔等学术生活必需品。左上角，另一群旅人正在通往城门的河岸登陆，他们可能前往山顶的多层佛塔。
items.cardline_zh: 桥头揖让间，云雾吞吐山中的闲情
items.cardline: On the bridge, bows exchange; mist swallows mountain leisure.

items.title: The Kiss of Peace and Justice
items.description: The figures of Peace (burning the weapons of war) and Justice (holding a sword and scales) embrace in a quiet landscape. The Latin inscription under the antique urn reads “Justice and Peace kissed” (referring to an Old Testament verse, Psalms 85:10). The subject may have had political significance: the painting’s date coincides with the end of the Fronde, a period of civil war in France during which the parlement (courts of appeal) and the nobility sought—unsuccessfully—to limit the power of the monarchy
items.description_zh: 和平（焚烧战争武器）与正义（手持剑和天平）的人物在宁静的风景中拥抱。古瓮下的拉丁文铭文写着“正义与和平亲吻”（引用《旧约》诗篇85:10）。这一主题可能具有政治意义：这幅画的创作日期恰逢投石党运动结束，这是法国内战时期，在此期间高等法院（上诉法院）和贵族试图——但未成功——限制君主权力。
items.cardline_zh: 武器熄灭于拥抱，正义与和平在亲吻中融化
items.cardline: Weapons die in an embrace; justice and peace melt in a kiss.

items.title: Portrait of Charlotte and Sarah Carteret-Hardy
items.description: Charlotte (left) and Sarah were 19 and 21, respectively, when this portrait was painted on the occasion of Sarah’s marriage to the topographer Daniel Lysons. The sisters wear fashionable gowns and jewelry, but their affectionate posture conveys a sisterly tenderness that transcends glamour. There was a seemingly insatiable demand for Thomas Lawrence’s portraits representing stylish English society, but he was notoriously slow working. Although this picture was begun in 1801, it still was not finished in 1806 when a friend noted its presence in the artist’s studio.
items.title_zh: 夏洛特与莎拉·卡特雷-哈迪肖像
items.description_zh: 夏洛特（左）和莎拉在绘制这幅肖像时分别为19岁和21岁，画作是为莎拉与地形学家丹尼尔·莱森斯的婚礼而作。姐妹俩穿着时髦的礼服和珠宝，但她们充满爱意的姿态传达出一种超越魅力的姐妹情谊。托马斯·劳伦斯的肖像画描绘了时尚的英国社会，需求似乎永无止境，但他以工作缓慢而闻名。尽管这幅画始于1801年，但到1806年仍未完成，当时一位朋友注意到它仍在艺术家的工作室中。
items.cardline_zh: 姐妹间温柔的倾斜，胜过所有珠宝的光泽
items.cardline: Sisters lean in tenderness, outshining every jewel's gleam.

items.title: Spring Flowers
items.description: This early work reveal's Monet's fascination with capturing the transitory effects that became the primary focus of his later innovations. Painted with almost scientific accuracy, this still life has a freshness and immediacy derived partly from its composition. Isolated against a dark background, the fully mature peonies, potted hydrangeas, and basketed lilacs spill downward and outward from the geraniums at the rear. At the same time, Monet's energetic brushwork conveys the sparkling play of light on leaves and petals.
items.description_zh: 这幅早期作品揭示了莫奈对捕捉转瞬即逝效果的迷恋，这成为他后来创新的主要焦点。这幅静物画以近乎科学的精确度绘制，其新鲜感和即时性部分源于构图。在深色背景的衬托下，完全盛开的牡丹、盆栽绣球花和篮装丁香花从后方的天竺葵向下和向外倾泻。同时，莫奈充满活力的笔触传达了光线在叶片和花瓣上闪烁的效果。
items.cardline_zh: 繁花从暗处溢出，仿佛春天突然失重
items.cardline: Blooms spill from darkness as spring suddenly loses weight.

items.title: Café Wepler
items.description: The Wepler, a large brasserie restaurant on Place Clichy in the Montmartre district of Paris, is composed of several rooms on different levels. Once frequented by bohemian artists like Pablo Picasso and Amedeo Modigliani, the Wepler is still in business today.
items.description_zh: 韦普勒是巴黎蒙马特区克利希广场上一家大型啤酒馆餐厅，由几个不同楼层的房间组成。这里曾是巴勃罗·毕加索和阿梅代奥·莫迪利亚尼等波西米亚艺术家的常去之地，如今仍在营业。
items.cardline_zh: 杯沿与灯火之间，巴黎的夜晚在低语
items.cardline: Between cup rim and lamplight, Paris nights whisper.

items.title: Cordelia Parting from her Sisters
items.medium: pen and oil on paper on panel
items.description: Reimagining English history was one way that Victorian (1837–1901) artists rooted themselves during a period of tremendous social and political change and life-altering technological advances. Ford Madox Brown’s expressive and vividly colored sketch for an unrealized project takes a story from Britain’s most famous playwright, Shakespeare. The artist chooses the dramatic moment when King Lear’s daughter Cordelia parts ways with her sisters.
items.description_zh: 重新想象英国历史是维多利亚时代（1837–1901）艺术家在社会和政治巨变以及改变生活的技术进步时期扎根的一种方式。福特·马多克斯·布朗为一项未实现的项目创作的这幅富有表现力和生动色彩的草图，取材于英国最著名剧作家莎士比亚的故事。艺术家选择了李尔王的女儿科迪莉亚与姐妹们分道扬镳的戏剧性时刻。
items.cardline_zh: 挥别的手掌里，握着整个王国的余温
items.cardline: In her parting hand, the kingdom's last warmth lingers.

items.title: Woman Meditating
items.description: This painting, acquired by the Cleveland Museum of Art in 1949, as a work by Corot, proved to be a copy when the signed original reappeared at a public auction in New York in 1981. Although the original, Jeune Femme Pensive or La Méditation, is not in the Corot catalogue raisonné compiled by his friend Alfred Robaut, that painting does have an unquestionable origin. According to one source, Corot painted the original version in Paris in 1866–68 and allowed his pupil, Eugène Lavieille, to make a copy with permission.
items.description_zh: 这幅画于1949年被克利夫兰艺术博物馆作为柯罗的作品购得，但当签名的原作于1981年在纽约公开拍卖中重新出现时，被证明是复制品。尽管原作《沉思的年轻女子》或《冥想》并未收录在柯罗的朋友阿尔弗雷德·罗博编纂的柯罗作品全集中，但那幅画确实有确凿的出处。据一位消息来源称，柯罗于1866–68年在巴黎绘制了原作，并允许他的学生欧仁·拉维耶在获得许可的情况下制作复制品。
items.cardline_zh: 低垂的眼睑后，思绪在静谧中生长
items.cardline: Behind lowered lids, thoughts grow in stillness.

items.title: Scenes of Witchcraft: Day
items.dimensions: Framed: 76.2 x 9.6 cm (30 x 3 3/4 in.); Unframed: 54.5 cm (21 7/16 in.)
items.description: Rosa's scene at noon showcases several hoary hags that exemplify his treatment of witches. Clutching skulls, wielding brooms, and slicing lizards, the witches prepare to travel to the Sabbath, an orgy of witches. One witch flays the skin of a thrashing lizard to extract the innards needed to concoct magical unguents, while her companions brandish skulls. Goats were the common mode of transportation for witches, but Rosa substitutes an owl, a harbinger of evil. Although Rosa foregrounds the violent cruelty of witchcraft, situating the grotesque hags in the full light of day introduces a comic aspect to the scene. Rosa's use of comedy and the unexpected to critique the world around him stemmed from the satirical poetry he wrote throughout his stay in Florence and his return to Rome in 1649. By lightening the palette and mood of the painting, Day reflects his interest in satire and his self-appointed role to expose and critique human folly.
items.description_zh: 罗莎的正午场景展示了几个老巫婆，体现了他对女巫的处理方式。女巫们抓着骷髅、挥舞扫帚、切割蜥蜴，准备前往安息日——女巫的狂欢。一个女巫剥去扭动蜥蜴的皮以提取内脏来调制魔法药膏，而她的同伴则挥舞着骷髅。山羊是女巫常见的交通工具，但罗莎用猫头鹰代替，猫头鹰是邪恶的预兆。尽管罗莎突出了巫术的暴力残忍，但将怪诞的巫婆置于白天的光线下为场景引入了喜剧元素。罗莎运用喜剧和意外来批判周围世界，这源于他在佛罗伦萨期间以及1649年返回罗马后创作的讽刺诗歌。通过提亮画作的色调和情绪，《白昼》反映了他对讽刺的兴趣以及他自封的揭露和批判人类愚蠢行为的角色。
items.cardline_zh: 正午的烈日下，女巫的暗影在蜥蜴皮上扭动
items.cardline: Under noon sun, witches' shadows writhe on lizard skin.

items.title: Prater Landscape
items.description: In this depiction of the Prater, a large public garden in Vienna, Austria, a man sits beside a tree in the left foreground. Brilliant sunlight floods into the park illuminating a multitude of trees, their leaves rendered with fine detail. Although celebrated for his portraits, Waldmüller also produced closely observed landscapes and often visited the Prater to paint the majestic oak trees. He was forced to retire from his position as a professor at the Vienna Academy for rejecting doctrines of idealized, moralizing art in favor of truth to nature based on direct observation.
items.description_zh: 在这幅描绘奥地利维也纳大型公共花园普拉特的画作中，一名男子坐在左前景的一棵树旁。灿烂的阳光涌入公园，照亮了众多树木，树叶细节精细。尽管以肖像画闻名，瓦尔德米勒也创作了细致观察的风景画，并经常前往普拉特描绘雄伟的橡树。他因拒绝理想化、道德化的艺术教条，转而追求基于直接观察的自然真实，被迫从维也纳学院教授职位上退休。
items.cardline_zh: 阳光穿透橡树叶，在地面筛出古老的宁静
items.cardline: Sun sieves through oak leaves, scattering ancient calm on the ground.

items.title: Storm in the Mountains
items.description: Dominating this composition is a lightning-blasted tree embodying nature's awe-inspiring power and endless cycle of life and death. A popular motif because of these associations, the blasted tree was favored by many landscape painters like Church, who prominently featured it here.
items.description_zh: 这幅画的主体是一棵被闪电击中的树，体现了自然令人敬畏的力量以及生与死的无尽循环。由于这些联想，被雷击的树成为流行主题，受到许多风景画家的青睐，如丘奇，他在这幅画中突出地展现了它。
items.cardline_zh: 闪电撕裂的枯木，成为天空最深的叹息
items.cardline: Lightning-split deadwood becomes the sky's deepest sigh.

items.title: Portrait of Hugh Hope
items.description: Henry Raeburn created this likeness of fellow Edinburgh native Hugh Hope before Hope departed for India, where he worked as a civil servant for the renowned East India Company (a British joint-stock company founded in 1600 to trade in the Indian Ocean). This painting hung in the drawing room of Hope’s estate, Pinkie House in Scotland, until 1928. Although greatly influenced by his English predecessor Joshua Reynolds, Raeburn worked in a looser style, more like the work of his younger contemporary Thomas Lawrence. This portrait is painted directly on the canvas without any preliminary drawing, giving it an informal, spontaneous air.
items.description_zh: 亨利·雷伯恩在休·霍普前往印度之前创作了这幅同乡爱丁堡人的肖像，霍普在印度为著名的东印度公司（一家成立于1600年的英国股份制公司，从事印度洋贸易）担任文官。这幅画挂在霍普位于苏格兰的庄园平基楼的客厅里，直到1928年。尽管深受英国前辈约书亚·雷诺兹的影响，雷伯恩的风格更为松散，更像其年轻同代人托马斯·劳伦斯的作品。这幅肖像是直接画在画布上的，没有预先绘制草图，因此具有非正式、自发的氛围。
items.cardline_zh: 远行前的凝视，定格在苏格兰的晨光里
items.cardline: A gaze before departure, frozen in Scottish morning light.

items.title: Clio, Muse of History
items.description: Clio, the Greek muse of history, is the daughter of Zeus and Titaness Mnemosyne, the goddess of memory. Clio is depicted here writing and surrounded by objects associated with preserving the memory of historical figures and events: busts, reliefs, and sculptures. This painting belongs to a cycle of works commissioned by businessman François Boyer-Fonfréde for his home in Toulouse.
items.description_zh: 克利俄是希腊神话中的历史缪斯，是宙斯与泰坦女神谟涅摩绪涅（记忆女神）的女儿。这幅画中，克利俄正在书写，周围环绕着与保存历史人物和事件记忆相关的物品：半身像、浮雕和雕塑。这幅画属于商人弗朗索瓦·布瓦耶-丰弗雷德为其在图卢兹的住宅委托创作的一组作品。
items.cardline_zh: 笔尖蘸着石像的沉默，写下时间的故事
items.cardline: Her nib dips into stone's silence, writing time's tale.

items.title: Sophie Crouzet
items.description: The sitter's dress deliberately evokes ancient Roman costume. However, the white muslin and straightforward cut also derives from earlier English fashions that favored simplicity in contrast to the elaborate, colorful clothing favored earlier in the 1700s. The transparency of her dress also carries political and cultural meaning: during the French Revolution in 1789, costume began to signify political allegiance, a sign of the character of the person who wore it. For women, transparency became increasingly literal, as in the sheer fabric worn by Crouzet, who came from a family of active revolutionaries.
items.description_zh: 画中人的服装刻意唤起古罗马服饰的联想。然而，白色细棉布和简洁剪裁也源自早期英国时尚，这些时尚偏爱简洁，与18世纪早期流行的华丽多彩服装形成对比。她服装的透明性也具有政治和文化含义：在1789年法国大革命期间，服装开始象征政治忠诚，是穿着者性格的标志。对于女性而言，透明性变得越来越直接，如克鲁泽所穿的透明面料，她来自一个活跃的革命者家庭。
items.cardline_zh: 薄纱下的身体，承载着一个时代的透明宣言
items.cardline: Beneath thin gauze, a body carries an age's transparent claim.

items.title: Boy in a Blue Coat
items.description: George Bellows created this work during July 1915 in Ogunquit, Maine. Because of frequent rainy weather, the artist tended to paint indoors, focusing on portraiture. The identity of the sitter in this example remains unknown.
items.description_zh: 乔治·贝洛斯于1915年7月在缅因州奥甘奎特创作了这幅作品。由于频繁的阴雨天气，艺术家倾向于在室内作画，专注于肖像画。这幅画中模特的身份至今未知。
items.cardline_zh: 蓝外套里的少年，被雨季留在画室里
items.cardline: Boy in blue coat, left in the studio by rainy days.

items.title: Young Woman Arranging Her Earring
items.description: During his later years, Pierre-Auguste Renoir moved away from pure Impressionism toward a classicizing style featuring strong, monumental figures of substantial weight and volume.
