This depiction of a woman arranging her earrings, a superb example of that trend in the artist's late work, may have been inspired by one of the figures in Eugène Delacroix’s Women of Algiers in their Apartment (Musée du Louvre, Paris).
items.description_zh: 在晚年，皮埃尔-奥古斯特·雷诺阿从纯粹印象主义转向一种古典风格，特点是坚实、宏伟的人物形象，具有显著的重量和体积感。这幅描绘女子整理耳环的作品，是艺术家晚期作品中这一趋势的绝佳范例，可能受到欧仁·德拉克罗瓦《阿尔及尔的女人》（巴黎卢浮宫）中某个人物的启发。
items.cardline_zh: 指尖触碰耳垂的瞬间，整个画面微微倾斜
items.cardline: Fingertip meets earlobe, and the whole frame tilts slightly.

items.title: General Jean-Baptiste Kléber (Sketch for "The Battle of the Pyramids")
items.description: In 1810, Gros exhibited a massive painting of Napoleon at the 1798 Battle of the Pyramids, one of the rare French triumphs in the failed campaign to conquer Egypt (1789-1801). After Napoleon first fell from power in 1814, the painting went into storage, until the new king Louis-Philippe chose to resurrect it for a history museum in Paris. However, perhaps to diminish Napoleon's significance, the government asked Gros to amplify the original with an addition at each end. This painting incorporates General Kléber, a famously successful military leader who had been excluded from the original painting.
items.title_zh: 让-巴蒂斯特·克莱贝尔将军（《金字塔战役》草图）
items.description_zh: 1810年，格罗展出了一幅描绘拿破仑在1798年金字塔战役中的巨幅画作，这是征服埃及（1789-1801年）失败战役中罕见的法国胜利之一。拿破仑于1814年首次倒台后，这幅画被存放起来，直到新国王路易-菲利普选择将其重新用于巴黎的一座历史博物馆。然而，也许是为了削弱拿破仑的重要性，政府要求格罗在两端各添加一部分以扩大原作。这幅画包含了克莱贝尔将军，一位著名的成功军事领袖，他在原画中被排除在外。
items.cardline_zh: 战马与刀光之外，将军的灵魂在颜料中徘徊
items.cardline: Beyond warhorse and blade, the general's soul haunts the paint.

items.title: Madeleine Lerolle and Her Daughter Yvonne
items.description: Albert Besnard was a good friend of fellow artist Henri Lerolle, whose wife Madeleine and daughter Yvonne are the subjects of this double portrait set in a studio. The artist’s profession is referenced in the large portfolios of drawings at lower right, and the easel at upper left, which displays a canvas tipped forward at an unusual angle. The canvas has been strapped to the easel in this position so the artist can more easily paint while seated.
items.title_zh: 玛德琳·勒罗尔和她的女儿伊冯娜
items.description_zh: 阿尔伯特·贝斯纳是艺术家亨利·勒罗尔的挚友，这幅双人肖像画以工作室为背景，描绘了亨利的妻子玛德琳和女儿伊冯娜。画面右下角的大幅素描画册暗示了艺术家的职业，左上角的画架上以不寻常的角度向前倾斜的画布，便于画家坐着作画。
items.cardline_zh: 未完成的画架，静候时光落笔。
items.cardline: Easel waits, canvas holds a pause.

items.title: Portrait of a Couple
items.description: While the attribution remains hotly debated, this work exemplifies how Italian portraiture of the 1500s could articulate family alliances through marriage. The inscription gives the sitters’ ages as 35 and 28, and their elaborate jewelry, weapons, and garments, made of expensive materials, convey their elite status. The marten skin attached to the woman’s waist-its head decorated with gems-symbolized propriety. These expressions of wealth convey achievements and position rather than accurate personalities, and the figures, though lifelike, stand in awkward relationship to each other, their interaction one of alliance not love.
items.description_zh: 尽管归属问题仍存争议，但这幅作品体现了16世纪意大利肖像画如何通过婚姻来展现家族联盟。题记标明画中人物年龄分别为35岁和28岁，他们精致的珠宝、武器和昂贵材质的服装彰显了其精英地位。女子腰间悬挂的貂皮（头部饰有宝石）象征着得体。这些财富的表达传达的是成就与地位，而非真实个性；人物虽栩栩如生，但彼此关系尴尬，互动更像是联盟而非爱情。
items.cardline_zh: 貂尾缀宝石，契约凝于织锦。
items.cardline: Gems on marten, vows in silk.

items.title: Villas at Trouville
items.description: Gustave Caillebotte painted this dramatic view of the blue-green sea, illuminated by a cloud-dappled sky, from one of the cliffs of Trouville, a popular resort town on the Normandy coast in northern France. The brick villas in the foreground, just outside the center of the town, offered homeowners a private shoreline retreat. During the early 1880s, Caillebotte spent several summers on the Normandy coast where he painted, participated in sailing regattas, and visited friends.
items.description_zh: 古斯塔夫·卡耶博特从特鲁维尔（法国北部诺曼底海岸的一个热门度假小镇）的悬崖上，描绘了这片被云彩斑驳的天空照亮的蓝绿色大海的壮丽景色。前景中的砖砌别墅位于镇中心外，为房主提供了私密的海滨休憩地。19世纪80年代初，卡耶博特在诺曼底海岸度过了几个夏天，在那里作画、参加帆船赛并拜访朋友。
items.cardline_zh: 云影下的砖红，海蓝浸透夏日。
items.cardline: Cloud shadows on brick, sea bleeds summer.

items.title: A Hare and a Leg of Lamb
items.description: Oudry used a starkly simple composition and sterile background to emphasize his virtuosity in depicting textures, a highly desirable skill of still-life painters at this time. These artists were aiming for the highest level of accuracy—an effect of the Enlightenment, the contemporary intellectual movement that emphasized scientific reasoning in all pursuits. These paintings were generally displayed in hunting lodges or dining rooms, as a glorification of the hunt and the bounty it brings.
items.description_zh: 乌德里运用极其简洁的构图和单调的背景，强调其在描绘质感上的精湛技艺，这是当时静物画家极为推崇的技能。这些艺术家追求最高水平的精确性——这是启蒙运动（强调科学理性的当代思想运动）的影响。这类画作通常陈列在狩猎小屋或餐厅中，以颂扬狩猎及其带来的丰饶。
items.cardline_zh: 皮毛与脂肪，静物在沉默中呼吸。
items.cardline: Fur and fat breathe in still silence.

items.title: Portrait of a Man
items.description: This portrait of Albert Courtier was painted in the fourth-floor apartment at 31 boulevard Hausmann in Paris that Gustave Caillebotte shared with his brother Martial. The interior and balcony of this apartment provided the setting for numerous paintings of men in Caillebotte’s social circle surveying the city. Courtier’s pose and unguarded gaze suggest a comfortable rapport with the artist. Their relationship was close: Courtier posed for Caillebotte on several occasions, owned four of the artist’s paintings, and designated his friend as godfather to his eldest daughter.
items.description_zh: 这幅阿尔伯特·库尔蒂耶的肖像画是在巴黎奥斯曼大道31号四楼公寓中绘制的，古斯塔夫·卡耶博特与他的兄弟马夏尔曾同住于此。公寓的内景和阳台为卡耶博特描绘其社交圈中男子俯瞰城市的众多画作提供了背景。库尔蒂耶的姿态和坦率的目光暗示了他与画家之间的舒适关系。两人关系密切：库尔蒂耶多次为卡耶博特担任模特，拥有画家的四幅作品，并指定这位朋友为长女的教父。
items.cardline_zh: 阳台之上，目光与城市平行。
items.cardline: On the balcony, gaze meets city's eye.

items.title: The Bistro
items.description: The hustle and bustle of eager customers in a crowded bistro is captured in this painting. One can almost hear the loud chatter and shouts of orders being called to the busy servers behind the countertop. All in jackets, the men by the bar have rosy cheeks and noses, perhaps from the cold outdoors contrasting the warmth of their drinks.
items.description_zh: 这幅画捕捉了拥挤小酒馆中顾客的喧嚣。人们几乎能听到嘈杂的谈话声和向柜台后忙碌的服务员喊单的声音。吧台边的男子都穿着夹克，脸颊和鼻子红润，或许是因为室外的寒冷与杯中热饮的温暖形成对比。
items.cardline_zh: 酒杯碰撞声，淹没在暖雾里。
items.cardline: Clink of glasses lost in warm haze.

items.title: Study for "Bathers at Asnières"
items.description: This oil sketch is one of many preparatory studies Seurat made for his monumental painting Bathers at Asnières (1883-84), depicting men leisurely bathing in the Seine near an industrial suburb of Paris. Seurat borrowed the brilliant colors of Impressionism to suggest outdoor light, but added structure by emphasizing basic geometric shapes and firm outlines. As a leader of the movement to reform Impressionism, his style became known as Neo-Impressionism.
items.description_zh: 这幅油画草图是修拉为其巨作《阿涅尔的浴者》（1883-84年）所作的多幅预备研究之一，描绘了男子在巴黎郊区工业区附近的塞纳河中悠闲沐浴的场景。修拉借鉴了印象派的鲜艳色彩来表现户外光线，但通过强调基本几何形状和清晰轮廓增加了结构感。作为改革印象派运动的领袖，他的风格被称为新印象派。
items.cardline_zh: 几何的河岸，阳光尚未凝固。
items.cardline: Geometric banks, sunlight still flows.

items.title: Drying the Linen, or Moonrise at the Priory
items.medium: oil on canvas glued to cardboard
items.description: This painting depicts the enclosed backyard garden of a former priory—a religious building where monks and nuns live under the leadership of a prior—that Maurice Denis purchased in 1914. He transformed the building into a home for his large family and lived there for the last 30 years of his life. A woman dressed in black hangs linens to dry, so that—according to custom—they will be bleached by moonlight. The full moon casts a golden glow over the entire nighttime scene.
items.title_zh: 晾晒亚麻布，或修道院的月出
items.description_zh: 这幅画描绘了莫里斯·德尼于1914年购买的前修道院（修士和修女在院长领导下生活的宗教建筑）的封闭后院花园。他将这座建筑改造成大家庭的住所，并在那里度过了生命的最后30年。一名黑衣女子正在晾晒亚麻布，按照习俗，它们将在月光下漂白。满月为整个夜景洒下金色的光辉。
items.cardline_zh: 月光漂白衣衫，静默如修道院。
items.cardline: Moon bleaches linen, silent as cloister.

items.title: The Lock at Pontoise
items.description: Pissarro was instrumental in developing the radically new Impressionist technique of painting quickly outdoors to capture fleeting effects of light and atmosphere. The rushing water and overcast sky in this view of a river lock near the artist’s home at Pontoise, a rural commune about 17 miles northwest of Paris, are rendered with rapid, broken brushstrokes of pure color. Painting directly on canvas without preliminary drawing, Pissarro may have executed this work in a single session. The shimmering surface of broken color conveys the sensation of natural, outdoor light.
items.description_zh: 毕沙罗在开创全新的印象派技法中发挥了关键作用，即在户外快速作画以捕捉光线和氛围的瞬间效果。这幅描绘画家位于蓬图瓦兹（巴黎西北约17英里的乡村公社）家附近水闸的景色中，湍急的水流和多云的天空以快速、断续的纯色笔触呈现。毕沙罗直接画在画布上，没有预先素描，可能一次就完成了这幅作品。闪烁的破碎色彩表面传达出自然户外光线的感觉。
items.cardline_zh: 水流与笔触，共舞于光影之间。
items.cardline: Water and brush dance in light's flux.

items.title: Landscape with Peasants Dancing
items.description: Before a rustic inn, a group of villagers converse, embrace, and dance to a bagpiper’s tune. Teniers was one of many Netherlandish artists whose paintings celebrate the joys of country life. These delicately painted works were created primarily for an elite urban audience nostalgic for a simpler existence.
items.description_zh: 在一家乡村客栈前，一群村民交谈、拥抱，并随着风笛手的曲调起舞。特尼尔斯是众多歌颂乡村生活乐趣的尼德兰画家之一。这些精致的作品主要为怀念简单生活的精英城市观众创作。
items.cardline_zh: 风笛声里，乡土入画成梦。
items.cardline: Bagpipes dream of rustic joy.

items.title: Woman Ironing
items.description: This spare painting depicts a woman ironing a garment, while a sliver of light illuminates the side of her face and the cup held in her left hand. The subject belongs to a series of paintings of women ironing or sewing inspired by Édouard Vuillard’s close observation of his mother and sister making corsets and dresses in their crowded Parisian apartment.
items.description_zh: 这幅简洁的画作描绘了一名女子正在熨烫衣物，一缕光线照亮了她的侧脸和左手握着的杯子。该主题属于一系列描绘熨烫或缝纫女性的画作，灵感来自爱德华·维亚尔对母亲和妹妹在拥挤的巴黎公寓中制作紧身胸衣和裙子的细致观察。
items.cardline_zh: 熨斗的蒸汽，晕开一日辛劳。
items.cardline: Iron's steam softens toil's edge.

items.title: A View of Naples through a Window
items.medium: oil on paper, mounted on canvas
items.description: Franz Ludwig Catel worked in Rome and later traveled south to Naples. This painting presents that city’s most distinctive attraction: Mount Vesuvius, an active volcano that appears against an otherwise calm sky. Catel portrayed the scene from within a hotel room, contrasting the potentially overwhelming force of nature with the illusion of protection offered by distance and enclosed space.
items.description_zh: 弗朗茨·路德维希·卡特勒在罗马工作，后来南下那不勒斯。这幅画展现了该城最独特的景观：维苏威火山，一座活火山，在平静的天空映衬下显现。卡特勒从酒店房间内描绘了这一场景，将自然潜在的强大力量与距离和封闭空间所提供的保护假象形成对比。
items.cardline_zh: 窗内静观火山，远方自有雷霆。
items.cardline: Window frames volcano, thunder stays afar.

items.title: Boy Drinking
items.description: Together with his brother Agostino and cousin Lodovico, Annibale Carracci introduced artistic reforms in Italy based on close observation of the natural world. Annibale’s innovative and highly influential style steered a path between the smooth artificiality of Mannerism––in which style and technique took precedence over fidelity to nature––and the heightened drama of paintings by Caravaggio. In this remarkable early work, the coarse surface of the canvas, the inelegant subject matter, and the striking distortion of forms from light passing through glass all speak to his naturalistic approach.
items.description_zh: 安尼巴莱·卡拉奇与他的兄弟阿戈斯蒂诺和堂兄洛多维科一起，基于对自然世界的细致观察，在意大利引入了艺术改革。安尼巴莱创新且极具影响力的风格在矫饰主义（风格和技巧优先于对自然的忠实）的平滑造作和卡拉瓦乔画作的强烈戏剧性之间开辟了一条道路。在这幅非凡的早期作品中，画布的粗糙表面、不雅的主题以及光线穿过玻璃造成的形态扭曲，都体现了他自然主义的手法。
items.cardline_zh: 仰头的瞬间，清凉滑入喉间。
items.cardline: Cool water slides down a tilted throat.

items.title: Portrait of the Jester Calabazas
items.description: Calabazas, a jester in the court of King Philip IV of Spain, had physical and mental disabilities, a state that the artist emphasizes, rather than tries to hide. While the Spanish court prized and doted upon people with disabilities such as Calabazas, Velázquez transformed their depiction in portraiture. This startling work uses the format of the formal, full-length court portrait to present a type of person who was generally perceived as marginal at this point in history and profoundly humanizes him.
items.description_zh: 卡拉巴萨斯是西班牙国王腓力四世宫廷中的小丑，患有身体和智力残疾，艺术家强调而非试图隐藏这一点。尽管西班牙宫廷珍视并溺爱像卡拉巴萨斯这样的残疾人，但委拉斯开兹改变了他们在肖像画中的描绘。这幅惊人的作品采用了正式的全身宫廷肖像画格式，呈现了在历史上通常被视为边缘人物的一类人，并深刻地赋予其人性。
items.cardline_zh: 小丑的尊严，立于宫廷阴影中。
items.cardline: Jester's dignity stands in court's shadow.

items.title: Sleep
items.description: The tradition of painting nude male figures in a studio setting was the cornerstone of artistic practice, teaching artists to depict the human body in complex poses in order to create larger narratives. However, by the late 1700s, some artists began to see these studies as independent works of art. By adding the wings and the poppies, Restout transformed his study into a more specific subject, and he first exhibited the work in a privately organized exhibition in 1783 under the title of Morpheus, the god of sleep.
items.description_zh: 在工作室中绘制裸体男性形象的传统是艺术实践的基石，教导艺术家描绘复杂姿态的人体以创作更大的叙事。然而，到18世纪末，一些艺术家开始将这些习作视为独立的艺术品。通过添加翅膀和罂粟花，雷斯图将他的习作转化为更具体的主题，并于1783年首次在私人组织的展览中以“摩耳甫斯”（睡眠之神）的标题展出。
items.cardline_zh: 罂粟与羽翼，沉睡即神话。
items.cardline: Poppies and wings, sleep becomes myth.

items.title: Still Life
items.description: Preston Dickinson’s composition presents a seemingly casual arrangement of eggplants and lemons amid a tabletop setting with a bowl, knife, and napkins. A loosely corked bottled beverage stands at left, and a lightly sketched chairback occupies an otherwise empty background.
items.description_zh: 普雷斯顿·迪金森的构图呈现了桌面上看似随意摆放的茄子和柠檬，搭配碗、刀和餐巾。左侧有一个松塞的瓶装饮料，背景中隐约画有椅背，其余部分空无一物。
items.cardline_zh: 茄紫与柠檬黄，静物窃窃私语。
items.cardline: Eggplant and lemon whisper in still life.

items.title: Portrait of a Man, Possibly Girolamo Rosati
items.description: The gesture of the elegantly dressed man is a mystery. Is he rising from his chair to address someone or is he pointing to something outside the frame? His right hand rests on a piece of paper (a letter?), while on the table are clover—a symbol of abundance, good fortune, and happy marriage—and jasmine, associated with purity and love, further emphasized by the roses on the trellis. There may have been a companion portrait of the sitter’s bride, to whom he acknowledges his devotion. Another recent interpretation identifies the sitter as Girolamo Rosati, a high official in Fermo, on Italy’s east coast. The paper may be a design for one of his important architectural projects, to which he may be pointing in the distance. The interrupted moment is a naturalistic touch characteristic of Lotto, quite distinct from the formality of the other Italian Renaissance portraits in the museum's collection. Though this portrait clearly articulates the man’s status, Lotto emphasizes his individuality and interiority as much as a socially constructed identity.
items.title_zh: 男子肖像，可能是吉罗拉莫·罗萨蒂
items.description_zh: 这位衣着优雅的男子的手势是个谜。他是从椅子上站起来与人交谈，还是指向画框外的某物？他的右手放在一张纸（一封信？）上，而桌上摆着三叶草——象征丰饶、好运和幸福婚姻——以及茉莉花，与纯洁和爱情相关，格子架上的玫瑰进一步强调了这一点。可能曾有一幅画中人的新娘的配对肖像，他正表达对她的忠诚。另一种最近的解读认为画中人是吉罗拉莫·罗萨蒂，意大利东海岸费尔莫的一位高级官员。那张纸可能是他重要建筑项目的设计图，他可能正指向远处的项目。这个被打断的瞬间是洛托特有的自然主义笔触，与博物馆收藏的其他意大利文艺复兴肖像画的正式风格截然不同。尽管这幅肖像清晰地表达了男子的地位，但洛托同样强调了他的个性和内心世界，而非仅仅社会建构的身份。
items.cardline_zh: 手指指向虚空，三叶草暗藏祝福。
items.cardline: Finger points to void, clover hides a vow.

items.title: The Artist in His Room at the Villa Medici, Rome
items.description: Léon Cogniet was one of many artists who traveled from across Europe to Rome during the early 19th century, drawing upon the city’s ancient past and its surrounding landscape for inspiration. In this formally composed scene, the artist stands within his studio, its walls bare and colorless in contrast to the dramatic light cast through his open window.
items.title_zh: 艺术家在罗马美第奇别墅的房间中
items.description_zh: 莱昂·科涅是19世纪初从欧洲各地前往罗马的众多艺术家之一，他们从这座城市的古老历史和周围景观中汲取灵感。在这幅构图正式的场景中，艺术家站在他的工作室里，墙壁光秃无色，与透过开着的窗户投射进来的戏剧性光线形成对比。
items.cardline_zh: 空墙与光，旅人自囚于画室。
items.cardline: Bare walls and light cage the wanderer.

items.title: Allegorical Figure
items.description: During the 1630s, the Genoese artist Bernardo Strozzi painted a number of female figures representing various intellectual and artistic pursuits, reflecting the appeal of such allegories among learned patrons in northern Italy. Recent scholarship has convincingly identified the subject of this painting as Minerva. The Roman goddess of war has put aside her armor for more contemplative pursuits; her upward gaze—as if seeking inspiration—recalls her other associations with wisdom, eloquence, and the arts.
items.description_zh: 17世纪30年代，热那亚艺术家贝尔纳多·斯特罗齐绘制了许多代表各种智力和艺术追求的女性形象，反映了这类寓意画在意大利北部博学赞助人中的吸引力。最近的学术研究令人信服地确认这幅画的主题是密涅瓦。这位罗马战争女神放下了盔甲，转向更具沉思性的追求；她向上凝视——仿佛在寻求灵感——让人联想到她与智慧、口才和艺术的其他关联。
items.cardline_zh: 卸下铠甲，智慧便如泉涌。
items.cardline: Armor laid down, wisdom overflows.

items.title: Portrait of Mary Anne Jolliffe
items.description: The only assistant his famous uncle ever had, Gainsborough Dupont apprenticed with Thomas Gainsborough before setting up his own studio. It is often difficult to distinguish the late works of both artists. This painting is among Dupont’s best portraits. He captures the beauty of his sitter with soft, flickering brushstrokes, detailing her frilly collar and impressive hairstyle, curled and powdered according to the current style. This portrait originally hung alongside that of her husband Thomas in the drawing room of the couple’s home, Ammerdown House in Somerset, where Thomas’s portrait still hangs today.
items.description_zh: 作为他著名叔叔唯一的助手，庚斯博罗·杜邦在开设自己的工作室之前曾师从托马斯·庚斯博罗。两位艺术家晚期的作品常常难以区分。这幅画是杜邦最优秀的肖像之一。他以柔和、闪烁的笔触捕捉了画中人的美丽，细致描绘了她荷叶边的衣领和令人印象深刻的发型——按照当时的时尚卷曲并扑粉。这幅肖像最初与她的丈夫托马斯的肖像一起挂在夫妇位于萨默塞特郡阿默当庄园的客厅里，托马斯的肖像至今仍挂在那里。
items.cardline_zh: 粉彩与卷发，年华定格于轻笔。
items.cardline: Powdered curls frozen in a gentle stroke.
